--- Chapter 798 ---

Chapter 798. Skull Island (5)

I used to catch bugs with my bare hands when I was young.

IГўВҖВҷd press a bug on the wall with my palm, then twist my hand to make sure it was dead.

After that, IГўВҖВҷd run to the bathroom, toss the bug in the toilet, and wash my hands.

After I got a little older, something about it felt gross, so I started using tissuesГўВҖВҰ

This time, the method wasn't much different.

Instead of a tissue, I was just using a shield.

The process was the same.

Squeeze.

Press.

"Ugh...!"

Twist and crush.

Crack-

The important part here is to do the twisting and crushing part well.

If you don't do it properly, the bug might escape.

Shhh.

As I slowly lowered the shield that was pressed against the wall, a half-crushed woman collapsed to the floor with a thud.

"Fortunately, she's not dead."

As I muttered this while looking down at her, Raven, who had scurried over, added a word.

"She looks like she'll die if you leave her like that."

"Parab! Come here and use a healing spell."

There was no reason to waste money on a potion, so I called over our clan's only religious member and started the treatment.

Ah, of course, she could run away after she was healed, so I had her bound in two or three layers while she was being treated.

I stripped off all her gear.

Then tied her hands and feet, laid her facedown, and pressed down on her back with one of my feet, putting a little weight on itГўВҖВҰ

"....."

It wasn't a pretty sight, but what could I do?

Barbarians pursue true gender equality.

No, not just Barbarians, but this entire world.

A world where itГўВҖВҷs only natural for anyone, man or woman, to pay the price for their dirty deeds.

"She's awake."

As I murmured, looking at the woman lying as if unconscious, she answered immediately.

Hmm, is it right to call it an answer?

"... Kill me. I did this on my own."

I was a little surprised.

I mean, I didn't expect her to say something like that as soon as she woke up.

Kaislan let out a scornful laugh.

"She seems to think she's a member of the Rose Knights or something."

In any case, it was an unexpected situation.

For a mere clan member, not a former Royal Army soldier who had undergone intense brainwashing, to act like this.

"Why are you so loyal to Golden Ghost?"

"... Like I said, I did this on my own."

"Ha, I've gotten tangled up with the wrong one again."

I let out a deep sigh and asked once more.

"What is Golden Ghost's objective?"

"As I said before-."

"Then what was your objective?"

"... Just kill me."

"What, does Golden Ghost have your family hostage or something?"

At my question, the woman's shoulder twitched slightly.

'What, I just threw that out there, but it's true?'

Just what kind of person was this Golden Ghost?

I couldn't tell, but this woman was clearly different from a soldier who had been subjected to intense brainwashing.

Which meant there was room to persuade her.

"Your reaction to the word 'family' tells me you're in debt too, right? If you die, they'll collect that debt from your family, and then they won't be able to pay taxes and will naturally die. Something like that?"

The woman's shoulder twitched again at my speculative remark.

This made the situation a little easier.

"I swear on my honor and name as a warrior. If you speak honestly, I will help you with all my strength."

"......."

"As soon as we return to the city, we will protect your family. If you wish, I am willing to accept them as my vassals."

The woman was silent for a long time, and I waited patiently, telling her she could take her time to think.

ButГўВҖВҰ

"... Kill me."

The answer that came back was the same.

I just couldn't understand.

With my reputation, wasn't it more rational for her to side with me, no matter what Golden Ghost had threatened her with?

'What on earth did that Golden Ghost bastard do to make her act like this?'

Still, the fortunate thing was that this woman wasn't skilled at lying.

Her thoughts were all written on her face.

Then again, it's not easy to maintain a poker face in an extreme situation where death is imminent.

"Is Golden Ghost still on this island?"

A flinch.

"And Golden Ghost sent you to kill us?"

No movement.

"Hmm, if that's not the purpose... then it's just a background check."

A flinch.

"It seems he thought I wouldn't have come to this island just to hunt the Skull King."

A flinch.

"If he caught on, it must have been then..."

"......"

"When I first offered to give you the right to enter first if you handed over Abman Urikfrit. He must have been certain then. That we weren't here just to hunt the Skull King. It seems he was very curious about what it was."

The woman, who had flinched as if startled by my words, soon closed her eyes with a sense of resignation.

She had completely shut down the channel of communication herselfГўВҖВҰ

"Raven."

"Yes?"

"Block her ears first. We need to talk amongst ourselves for a moment."

"Yes, just a moment."

Raven then used magic to make sure our voices couldn't be heard, and so the discussion began.

When I said to share their opinions freely, the first to speak was Misha.

"Well, it seems to be true that they have no intention of harming us. They probably just wanted to find out what was hidden."

I agreed with that opinion.

Killing a noble was a grave crime, and it was nearly impossible to completely silence ninety witnesses.

Even if they succeeded in harming us, a massive time bomb of a necklace would be fastened around Golden Ghost's neck.

"He's a cunning one."

"Since there's no physical evidence, even if we raise the issue upon returning to the city, no punishment will befall that Golden Ghost fellow."

"So what are we going to do with this woman? It seems she has her reasons, but to let her go out of pityГўВҖВҰ The Labyrinth isn't that warm a place, is it?"

Well, that's true.

I had no intention of just letting her go either.

Whether she was threatened or not, this woman tried to harm us, and from that moment on, we bear no moral responsibility even if we kill her.

Yes, so...

"Let me borrow this for a moment."

I borrowed Amelia's dagger and pierced her heart in a single motion.

Stab-!

To let her die in an instant, unable to hear or see anything.

"... I guess you can undo the Voice Control spell now."

This was the only kindness one could offer in the Labyrinth.

***

"So, what's the plan now?"

That question made me think.

"Shouldn't we obviously go and confront them? Secretly tailing other explorers in the Labyrinth is a major crime deserving of summary execution!"

"Do you think he'll admit to it if we do?"

"If he won't, we have to make him admit it!"

Erwen offered an unusually aggressive opinion, and Misha cautiously countered.

"Um... still, wouldn't it be better to take care of our business first and deal with them later when we get back to the city..."

"... Why?"

"There are ninety of them. It's too dangerous for us to handle on our own."

The Guild Master agreed with Misha.

"Indeed... there is such a rumor about Golden Ghost. That he holds a weakness over every member and works them like slaves... If even half of that rumor is true, they will be completely different from that clan we met on the fifth floor. They will fight to the death."

The Guild Master's words were probably true.

Just look at this woman, for example.

Despite me offering her a way out several times, she ultimately refused it.

'It was the same with Bear UncleГўВҖВҰ'

I don't know the circumstances, but Bear Uncle never asked me for help.

And now we're supposed to have an all-out war with a clan of ninety such people?

There would definitely be casualties, big or small.

ButГўВҖВҰ

'Still, it's bothering me to just ignore it and do our own thing until we return to the city...'

As my worries deepened, Amelia offered an opinion.

"I will stay behind and watch them."

"Huh?"

"They don't yet know that this woman was captured by us. They will just wait for her to return. I'll keep an eye on them, so you go inside and finish what you need to do."

"... It could be dangerous."

"It's fine. I have this."

Saying so, Amelia picked up one of the spoils of war that had been gathered in one place.

No. 16 Ebony Emblem.

An item that provides a very strong boost to the wearer's stealth-type skills when equipped.

Because of this, the detection magic installed at the entrance was useless, and even Belleg hadn't noticed the rat that had latched onto us.

'Certainly, if Amelia wears this, it seems unlikely she'll get caught in any ordinary situation...'

Despite that, I couldn't easily give my permission, and Amelia approached me.

AndГўВҖВҰ

"Honestly, the feeling of being worried about isn't bad."

Amelia then took my hands.

"Don't worry. Nothing will happen."

"...... Uh."

"Then."

Before I could even reply, Amelia let go of my hands and disappeared from sight.

I couldn't hear the sound of her departing footsteps, but I knew instinctively.

She was already running towards the outside of the cave.

"Phew......."

As I sighed, looking at the opposite passage where Amelia had run, I suddenly felt gazes on me.

"... Why are you all looking at me like that?"

Kaislan answered my question on behalf of the group.

"Are you asking because you really don't know?"

"....."

"Never mind. We'll try not to pay attention to that part. But please, do that sort of thing when you're alone. It makes my own knees weak."

"Knees?"

"... If you don't know, then never mind."

I feel a little wronged.

'I mean, what did I do?'

I just cherish my companions.

***

After Amelia left to watch the Golden Tree Clan, we resumed the expedition that had been temporarily halted.

We had intentionally come to a strange place while catching the ratГўВҖВҰ

But the entrance was actually nearby.

"ГўВҖВҰThis is the entrance? It doesn't look any different."

After passing a fork in the road and following a short passage, we came to a dead end.

However, there was a difference from the previous place.

'It's no different? The number of torches in the passage is forty-four.'

The structure of the cave on Skull Island changes slightly with each run, much like the First Floor's Crystal Cave, but one thing remains the same.

The entrance to the next stage is always in a place with forty-four torches in the passage.

Well, you have to meet the prerequisite conditions to open the entrance, of course.

Anyway.

"Adnus. Go and touch it."

I sent the Guild Master, who had played an important role (?) in this Labyrinth expedition, to the wall.

With a faint vibration, the wall slid sideways.

And at the same time.

ГЈВҖВҢThe Cursed One has discovered the Memory of the Apocalypse.ГЈВҖВҚ ГЈВҖВҢA piece of a long story has been completed.ГЈВҖВҚ

The portal that had been hidden within flickered and revealed itself.

A portal, blood-red in color, distinctly different from the usual blue of a dimensional stone tablet's portal.

Wooooooosh-!

Looking at the portal, I quietly steadied my breathing.

So it has come to this.

Honestly, even though I've planned everything and come this far, I'm a little reluctant to enter.

ButГўВҖВҰ

'Well, what can I do.'

I have to go in.

This is where I can get three Graduation Essences to feed my companions.

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has entered the Memory of the Apocalypse.ГЈВҖВҚ

I wonder how many I can get.

I don't know, but suddenly I became curious about one thing.

"Adnus, by the way, what's that Golden Ghost fellow's name?"

"ГўВҖВҰYou didn't know?"

"Would I be asking if I knew?"

"No, didn't you introduce yourselves then? It's Bill Ironred."

Oh, right.

Well, if I can't remember, it couldn't have been *that* name.

I asked a pointless question.

-That's what I thought.

"However, if my memory is correct, I believe he changed his name once."

What?

"ГўВҖВҰWhat was it originally?"

"Hmm, I'm not sure about that. It was before he became so famous. He said himself that things started going well for him after he changed his name."

"ГўВҖВҰIs, is that so?"

"In any case, it was definitely a very common name. He himself said he changed it because he disliked it."

At those words, my heart began to pound.

No, to be precise.

"A common name...?"

Those three words made my heart pound.

Not in a good way, but in a bad way.

"That's right, but is there a problem?"

This will be fine, right...?

--- Chapter 799 ---

Chapter 799: Rank 1 (1)

SchrГғВ¶dinger's cat.

It is a famous experiment in the world of quantum mechanics, but frankly, I do not know much about it in detail.

As someone who studied the arts, there is only one thing I know.

Before opening the box, two futures exist, but the moment it is observed, one is confirmed.

Of course, I still cannot comprehend why that is so important scientifically, but this experiment has at least taught me one lesson.

Sometimes, it is better not to know.

Just like right now.

"......."

I barely managed to swallow the question that rose to the tip of my tongue.

A common name.

Could it be Hans?

The moment I ask, an answer will come, and the future will be confirmed.

A gacha with two possible outcomes.

It is nothing new, but I do not trust my luck.

Right, so...

'Let's leave it at that.'

It is better not to know.

At least then, there is still a chance it is not true.

Thump-!

I tried to ignore the pounding of my heart and checked my surroundings.

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has entered a Special Zone.ГЈВҖВҚ

The bones that had been ground into the floor, ceiling, and walls were gone, but the cave had the exact same structure as before entering the portal.

"First, let's get out of here."

Passing the passage where I caught the rat earlier, I remembered the way I came and headed out of the cave.

A new field, completely transformed from before, greeted us.

A sky as crimson as blood.

A cracked earth.

And even lava flowing through those cracks.

Kwaaaaaaang-!

A field designed to perfectly match the concept of an 'apocalypse'.

Ah, this place also had a peculiar field effect.

ГЈВҖВҢField Effect - The End is applied.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢStatus Effect [Eternal Scorching] is applied.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢThe character will continuously take Fire Damage, and this Fire Damage will amplify over time.ГЈВҖВҚ

Continuous damage is common, so that is one thing.

ГЈВҖВҢStatus Effect [Terminal] is applied.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢThe character will die in 24 hours.ГЈВҖВҚ

Every character who enters the Special Zone, 'Memory of the Apocalypse', will die instantly after 24 hours.

Of course, there is a way to prevent this.

To put it bluntly, just going back out through the portal you came in from will remove the status effect.

'But once you leave, you can't re-enter.'

Thus, the standard strategy is to reset the timer with items found while exploring this field, but...

"This is that island...?"

"I've never seen such a terrifying sight in my life."

The members had dazed expressions at the sight of an entire island exploding like a bomb.

To be honest, I felt similarly.

After all, there is a big difference between 2D and real life, right?

"Everyone, be careful not to stray too far from my side."

Before beginning the expedition in earnest, I first activated the Orb of Fire to counter the [Eternal Scorching] field effect.

ГЈВҖВҢThe Orb of Fire has been activated.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢAll continuous Fire-attribute damage originating within a 15m radius is reduced by 50%.ГЈВҖВҚ

The Orb of Fire was a top-tier, helpful item in the Memory of the Apocalypse.

Well, a point would come when even this could not block the damage, but...

'This alone is like doubling the time we can stay here.'

Ah, of course, this was all assuming we handled the [Terminal] effect well.

"Alright, let's move out. Form up as I instructed. We're getting off this island first."

"Huh? Leaving the island?"

"You'll see when we get there."

I then led all the members to the coastline where we had first arrived by ship.

The coastline, too, was vastly different from before.

"Skulls or a sea of fire... This island is quite extreme."

Lava flowed out as if the entire island had turned into an active volcano, and the lava that flowed out fell into the sea, hardening into black rock.

It was difficult to secure a line of sight due to the smoke generated from the meeting of seawater and lava.

With the shackle of [Terminal] on us, a normal explorer would not even think of venturing beyond, but...

The thought process of a Veteran Player is different.

Huh? You can summon a ship and leave the island?

Then there must be something hidden out there, right?

Indeed, that thought turned out to be correct.

Well, it took hundreds of tries and the evaporation of hundreds of characters to figure it out, but still.

Anyway.

"Then... we shall depart...!"

Once everyone boarded the ship I had summoned on the shore, the vessel, its hull plastered with fire resistance enchantments for this very moment, pushed through the black smoke and headed out to sea.

Shwaaaaaaa-!

The section where our vision was blocked by smoke was not that long.

After about a few minutes of moving in a straight line, the smoke gradually lifted, and looking back from the stern, I could see the island exploding with bangs!

"So where are we going now?"

"Ah, did you not hear?"

"You only said we were going to find something, you didn't say exactly how."

Ah, I guess I only told Auyen.

"From now on, we are going to Arta Island."

"... Arta Island?"

Raven tilted her head, then continued as if she remembered something.

"Ah! I know that island! One of the seven wonders of the Labyrinth! The small island with just three strange pillars standing there!"

"That's right."

Arta Island had long been famous among explorers and mages.

It makes sense, doesn't it?

An island with no monsters, only strange structures?

Naturally, one would think something was hidden there...

"Wow! So we're going to find the secret hidden on that island? Can I write a paper on it?"

"No."

"But it's one of the seven wonders..."

"Write it after I die. I won't stop you then."

"Hey, why would you say that...? Don't say things like that, even as a joke."

Hmm, was that a bit much this time?

As Raven spoke with a serious face, I felt a little embarrassed myself.

Actually, if you just look at the content, it was not entirely a joke.

"Anyway, according to the clan's internal rules, all details of the expedition are secret. If you want to write a paper, just organize your data for now and write it later when we've all retired. Got it?"

"I get it, I get it.... I was just saying. But is that island nearby?"

It was Auyen, who was at the helm next to her, who answered Raven's question.

"Arta Island is also the closest island to the Catacomb, Lady Raven."

"Oh, really? Then why didn't we see it on the way here?"

"Charting the most efficient sea route. That is the role of a navigator."

It was also the reason I had brought Auyen, who had almost no combat ability, this far.

We needed a ship here.

Well, to be blunt, finding Arta Island would not be a problem even without a navigator, but...

With [Terminal] active, it was better to save as much time as possible.

Just like this.

ГЈВҖВҢAuyen Lokrov has cast [Tailwind].ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢThe movement speed of the current ship increases by 50%, and the Soul Power of all characters on board recovers rapidly.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢAuyen Lokrov has cast [Trade Route].ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢWhile the helm is fixed, the movement speed of the current ship increases by 300%.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢAuyen Lokrov has cast [Ghost Ship]...ГЈВҖВҚ

A Supernatural Ability imbued the ship, and its speed increased dramatically.

Raven was slightly surprised by this.

"...[Trade Route]? Wow, you really are a capable navigator. I know that's a rare Essence."

"Haha... Actually, it's a Supernatural Ability I acquired recently."

"What? How, when the Labyrinth was closed?"

"I used the Exchange. I used up all the money I had saved, but... I thought this was the least I could do to not hold you all back. Haha..."

"... You've been through a lot too. How did you end up in a place like this?"

"It is alright. Because I am happy."

Raven seemed to have taken a liking to Auyen, as they continued to chat for a while.

I quietly stepped aside, and Ainar approached me.

"Bjorn! I have a question!"

"Go ahead."

"Where do you get Supernatural Abilities like that?"

Normally, I would have told her to ask Raven, but since Raven was talking with Auyen, I just answered myself.

"Some monsters pilot ships."

"Eh? I've never seen any."

"That's because it's a region people don't go to often."

A prime example was the Pirate Archipelago in the southwestern seas, where Orcs and Goblins rode ships and attacked explorers.

Ghost Island also had ghost ships passing around it.

Ah, for reference, neither of them were on the route to the Seventh Floor, so we had no reason to pass by them until now.

'...

Getting her to learn [Flying Ship] would be tough, right?'

The navigator's graduation Essence, obtainable on the Tenth Floor, came to mind, but I quickly let go of the thought.

If she could learn it, we could pilot a ship on any floor, not just the Sixth, and our travel time for all future expeditions would be cut by more than half, but...

It was a monster you might never see if you were unlucky in the first place.

And right now, we did not have the stats to hunt it.

"Captain, we're within visual range!"

Hearing Auyen's shout, I ended my thoughts and headed towards the bow.

A small island was visible in the distance.

No, should I say three pillars were visible?

"Oh... That's one of the wonders of the Labyrinth!"

A strange-looking island where the pillars felt larger than the island itself.

"But it seems different from when I last saw it?"

Just as Kaislan muttered, the pillars of Arta Island were slightly different from their usual state.

It was not a big difference.

"Were those pillars... blue?"

"No, they're originally black."

The color of the black pillars had turned blue.

Well, not that the difference in appearance was that important.

"Dock the ship."

"Yes, Captain."

We brought the ship to the island, and everyone disembarked.

And the time it took to get here was...

'Four hours and forty minutes.'

If it took about the same time to return, that would be a total of nine hours and twenty minutes.

But since [Terminal] was a maximum of 24 hours...

'To be safe, it's best to finish the strategy within ten hours.'

It could take longer to return, and even after returning to the island, we would need at least a two-hour buffer to reset the terminal status.

Tuk.

After unsummoning the ship, I entered the center of the island.

And...

"So what do we do now?"

"Hold on."

The center of the three pillars forming a triangle.

On the small altar there, I would spill blood...

"Parab, you do it."

"Yes?"

"My skin is too tough for a knife."

"Ah..."

Following my command, Parab cut his palm with a knife.

The blood that trickled down dripped onto the altar.

And...

Shwaaaaaaa-!

Light began to pour out from within the pillars, and an unknown script, not even in the Ancient Language, glowed on their surfaces.

Chijijit, chijit-!

Unstable magic flowed out like sparks, and as if drawn to each other, they connected at the center and gradually took form.

"... Another portal?"

"That's just how the Labyrinth is."

Saying so, I let out a small laugh.

Should I call it a bit much?

In a way, the Memory of the Apocalypse we just entered was a Hidden Piece, and now there was another Hidden Piece hidden within it...

'I don't know who made the Labyrinth, but they sure love their secrets.'

Well, as long as the reward was certain, I would enter even hell.

"So, are we going in right away?"

"Just after this."

With that, I set a ten-hour timer.

Tick, tock-

We had to come out before then.

If we did not want to lose anyone.

--- Chapter 800 ---

Chapter 800: First-Class (2)

The moment I stepped into the portal, my vision flickered once more, and a new space appeared.

"This place is..."

"What is this? It's so quiet..."

Expecting it to be teeming with monsters, the members tilted their heads at the unexpectedly quiet atmosphere.

It was understandable.

Compared to the Catacomb of Skull Island, which was full of bones, and the previous field that spewed lava as if the whole island were exploding in real time, this place was merely dark, quiet, and showed no immediate threats.

"What a peculiar place. It looks like some kind of research institute."

The unfamiliarity and tension from being in an unknown space were fleeting.

Raven scanned the area, her curiosity as a scholar showing through.

'She was talking about revenge for her master and all that, but I guess people don't really change.'

Of course, that wasn't a bad thing.

To be honest, I wished she would just drop the whole revenge thing and live her life doing what she loves.

Though, I suppose I'm not one to talk.

Clang, clang...

The whole place was covered in iron plates, clanging with every step.

The walls were filled with metal pipes, hatch handles, and what looked like thick electrical wires, which were actually magic circuits.

It felt like being inside a massive submarine, or maybe a bunker.

"Oh! I have this sudden urge to turn this-"

"Don't! Miss Fenelin! Don't touch anything!"

"Ah, I didn't touch it! I only thought about it!!"

Regardless of Ainar's protests, I hurried forward.

After all, time was still ticking, wasn't it?

'It's about time he started talking...'

As I thought that and slowly walked toward the center, a voice, just as I expected, rang out.

[Who are you? Are you pilgrims?]

The voice was tinged with static, as if heard through a speaker.

"...!"

The moment the voice was heard, most of them raised their weapons and looked around cautiously.

That was to be expected.

There were only two people here who could understand those words.

"Ancient Language...?"

There was Raven, who could understand a little Ancient Language thanks to her long years of scholarly study.

And...

[Good to see you, Arta.]

...there was me, who had never learned the Ancient Language but could speak it like a native.

[...

You have not yet answered my question.

Who are you? Are you pilgrims?]

Hearing that question, I let out a smirk.

Even when I played the game, Arta Island was a very interesting chapter.

Arta is the only NPC you can meet in the Labyrinth.

Well, once you learn the whole story, it's a bit ambiguous whether you can call him an NPC.

In any case, the fact that a sentient being appears and gives out a quest makes it a very special case.

For the record, the way it progressed was also very unique...

"Bjorn! Is it an enemy? Just say the word! I'll smash it!"

"Stay still, Ainar. It's useless anyway."

"Useless...? W-what, is it a ghost?! Don't worry! I-I can beat up ghosts now, too!"

"Really? Then try hitting that door with your weapon."

"... Huh?"

Following my order, Ainar, though wondering what I was talking about, complied obediently.

But...

"Uh...?"

Ainar froze, her spear still in hand.

It was as if an invisible force wouldn't allow such an act.

"W-what is this! My body won't move...! H-help me!"

"Don't worry. You'll be fine once you let go of your hostility."

Strictly speaking, in the game, it wasn't hostility, but the attack button itself that was disabled...

If you apply a reality patch, that's probably what it would feel like.

And sure enough, as soon as she heard my words, Ainar was back to normal.

"Ugh...! What was that!"

To put it simply, it was a Field Effect.

This was a very special place, even among the many Hidden Pieces.

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has entered a Special Zone.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢField Effect - Arta Secret Base is now active.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢAll hostile actions are prohibited within the field.ГЈВҖВҚ

No combat of any kind was possible here.

Therefore, to clear this place, something other than force was needed...

[...

What are you talking about with them? Answer my question at once.

Otherwise, I will banish you immediately.]

The strategy was, simply, conversation.

In the game, choices would appear when talking to this guy, and only by selecting the right ones could you receive the quest.

Ah, of course, that was on the condition that you could speak 'Ancient Language'.

'It took me over a hundred tries just to get the quest...'

Conversely, that meant with my countless experiences of failure, getting the quest now would be a piece of cake.

[We are not pilgrims.]

First, saying you're a pilgrim is the wrong answer.

If you choose that option, a series of proofs begin, and the player can never pass them.

The only problem is...

[...

Then how do you know my name?]

Ah, the name...

[That is...]

I wondered what excuse to make.

Tsk, I just said his name because I was happy to see him.

Was that a mistake?

In the game, he only spoke his scripted lines, and I only chose from the given options, so I hadn't thought that far ahead.

But so what?

Unfortunately, I haven't lived such a peaceful life that a variable like this would make my mind go blank.

[We are the survivors of the fallen Empire.]

A line only possible for me, who knew the story that followed.

The order was a bit messed up, but... it didn't matter, since you had to choose this option when asked about your identity to get the quest anyway.

[We came here to stop the end, so of course, we know of your existence.]

[I see... survivors of the fallen Empire...

I suppose you wouldn't wish for this world to end either.]

[Of course.

Is there anything we can do to help?]

[Well...

I don't think there's anything you can do.

I respect your desire to save the world, but if you truly wish for it, leave this place.

I am in the midst of trying various things to resolve this myself.]

Uh... what's this?

Why is he just telling me to leave?

Is it because things went wrong from the start?

Well, it's not the first time reality has been different from the game.

'I guess I'll just have to freestyle from here on out.'

Having made up my mind, I quickly continued.

[Arta, we know how to stop the end.]

The very words he could never ignore, according to the story.

[...

Haha, you?]

But for some reason, a heartfelt, mocking laugh came back.

Is this another reality patch?

This bastard wasn't this annoying in the game.

In fact, once our identities were confirmed, he would assess our abilities and, if he deemed us useful, would give us quests to try and use us somehow.

Sigh... how did I mess up the first step like this.'

Well, since I know the whole story, it shouldn't be a problem.

[We know how to kill the 'Beast'.]

[...

The Beast is not something that can be killed.

Even for me, sealing it is the best I can do.]

His voice was dripping with the nuance of, 'How could the likes of you?'

[I have no intention of persuading you with long words.

I will catch that serpent and kill it.]

[...

You even know that much?]

[Is that important? What should matter to you is whether or not we can solve your problem, right?]

[You're not wrong, but-]

[We know it's a First-Class Monster, and we also know we have to activate the Divine Artifact below after catching it, so don't worry.]

[.....]

Arta no longer asked how I knew.

His attitude toward me, who casually knew such top-secret information, had changed.

He was much more serious than before, should I say?

[...

Very well.

I will trust you this once.

Survivors of the fallen Empire.]

With those words, a sound like hissing gas was heard, and a hidden hatch on the floor opened.

Well, the story got skipped somehow, but...

Chiiiiik-!

Whatever the path, as long as it leads to Seoul, right?

***

We were going down.

Endlessly, down a ladder that seemed connected to an abyssal pit.

"What did you talk about? I thought I heard something about an empire and trials."

"It was nothing special."

"No, in the first place, how did you even know about this island?"

At Raven's question, I just gave a smirk in response.

She knows I'm a former player.

Of course, it wasn't like the other members who didn't know were questioning it either.

"He's a Viscount, after all. Someone of the Captain's standing would know things that a regular explorer wouldn't! Isn't that right, Mr. Parab?"

"Haha, yes... that's right."

"But, this means the Royal Family already knew the secrets of Arta Island..."

Quietly listening to the murmurs of the members who didn't know I was a player, I smiled awkwardly.

"Don't think too deeply about it. It's not a topic for me to discuss."

"I know that fact better than anyone, so don't worry. I was just wondering why the Royal Family left Arta Island as a mystery."

Is it because he's from the Royal Family?

Kaislan always tends to be suspicious of the Royal Family and thinks they are the masterminds behind every conspiracy.

...

Well, he's not entirely wrong.

"Everyone, focus. I told you about the monster in the city, didn't I?"

"Ah... could this be the place you spoke of? Where the Serpent of the End, Sniktura, appears?"

"Yes."

"... This is nerve-wracking. Even that Kashan was such a monster."

Kaislan's voice trembled, as if he was recalling Kashan, which we hunted on the underground first floor.

It was understandable.

Back then, even with hundreds of our best, we suffered so many casualties.

There aren't many explorers who have faced a First-Class Monster, so few truly understand the danger... but those who have, all have an unforgettable experience.

A First-Class Monster is a calamity.

It's not about being a monster or a human; it feels like a completely different kind of existence.

"Don't worry too much. As I said in the city, the monster we're facing this time is completely different from Kashan. Taking all the conditions into account, you can consider the actual combat difficulty to be around that of a high-tier Second-Rank monster."

"Um... isn't Second-Rank still a monster?"

Hmm... she has a point.

In fact, you often hear rumors of clans that were at their peak being annihilated by a single Second-Rank monster.

But from the perspective of a hardcore K-Gamer, it's a whole different story.

A First-Class Monster that can be beaten at a Second-Rank difficulty?

You'd be a fool not to do it.

Thud.

As I was lost in thought while climbing down the ladder, my feet finally touched the ground.

Chapter 803: Rank 1 (5)

The Sniktura Raid in the Arta Secret Base is largely divided into three chapters.

At this time, Sniktura only fights with basic attacks and passives, and the standard strategy is to just endure no matter what.

That is, assuming all members have excellent equipment and passive setups, allowing them to deal damage to a First-Class Monster without MP.

Anyway.

Fixed at 100%.

This meant that resources would never be consumed.

The character has used Wild Surge.

Temporarily, the character's Threat Level increases by 3 times, and Physical Stats increase in proportion to that value.

The skill of the First-Class Monster, Cryptid: [Omen of Corruption].

Chapter 806: The Final Day (3)

Eighth Rank and Ninth Rank.

Looking at the gap between a Goblin and a Death Fiend, one might think it's as wide as the heavens and the earth, but in truth, the gap between these two ranks isn't that large.

Compared to the gap between the First and Second Ranks.

Generally, five Ninth-Rank Explorers can easily take down an Eighth-Rank.

The same goes for the Seventh Rank.

If five Eighth-Rank Explorers gather, they can hunt it down, and this formula continues.

'At least up to about the Third Rank.'

From the Third Rank, even if the entire party is Fourth-Rank, a raid of at least twelve people is recommended.

For the Second Rank, an expedition of more than thirty is essential.

So, what about the First Rank?

In truth, it's difficult to say for sure, as the characteristics of each First-Rank creature vary drastically.

However, considering that when we hunted the Wolf of Prophecy, Kashan, on the Library Island, hundreds were mobilized and yet so many elite explorers were lost...

'Beyond measure.'

I can certainly say this much.

First-Rank monsters are in a different league altogether, even from Second-Rank ones.

That's why simply possessing a First-Rank Essence earns one immense fame and becomes the subject of endless gossip.

'Evil Eye, that fellow was a perfect example.'

Anyway, that's not what's important right now.

Kkigigik, kkiik, kkik-!

Is it because Sniktura warped everything?

Unlike before, when it opened smoothly, the door now opened with a loud, grating noise.

Watching the gap in the door, my heart pounded.

Thump-!

Did it drop, or not?

Kkigigik, kkiik-!

Perhaps because of the dopamine rush in my brain, every moment the door opened felt incredibly slow.

Kuuung-!

Finally, the door opened completely, and the answer to the question revealed itself through Raven's lips.

"Th-there..."

"..."

"That's... that's an Essence, right...?"

To that dazed question, I rubbed my eyes, opened them, and answered.

"Behel-La..."

The Essence of Sniktura had dropped.

***

The impact of a First-Rank Essence was truly immense.

"Wow..."

"So that's a First-Rank Essence..."

"Adnus, don't get any funny ideas."

"Ahem, I was just admiring it."

"Indeed, it has a different feel to it..."

Everyone in the room stared with their mouths agape, overwhelmed by the unknown pressure the Essence exuded.

It was understandable.

Even if one spent a lifetime as an Explorer, seeing a First-Rank Essence in person was an incredibly rare experience.

Well, as someone mentioned earlier, its luster was indeed different from a regular Essence.

"It has two colors..."

The Essence of Sniktura, floating in the air and emitting a brilliant light, had two colors.

For reference, it wasn't a mix of two colors, but rather divided like a Taegeuk mark.

"Ah, now that I think about it, I've heard of this. Some First-Rank Essences are like this."

"If it has two colors, does that mean it has two Supernatural Abilities?"

"Yes. That's what I heard."

"Then that Essence..."

"Well... I'm not sure about that... Information about First-Rank Essences is so rare... It's practically treated as a legend."

"But wasn't there at least one person in every generation who had one?"

"That's true. But in any generation, would someone who possesses such a thing be cooperative with research?"

Raven said, glancing at me from the corner of her eye.

It was a look that seemed to ask if I, of all people, knew something about that Essence.

Well, it wasn't an incorrect assumption.

But...

"Raven, take out the vial first."

We have to do what needs to be done first.

We still have plenty of time, but you know how it is.

Even knowing it's unnecessary, when a rare item drops, a gamer's instinct is to rush over and grab it first, setting everything else aside.

"But."

Just then, Raven took out the vial and asked me.

"Is it not the color you wanted?" Ah, that?

Actually, it's a bit ambiguous.

Well, the reason for putting it in the vial isn't because of the color anyway.

"This Essence is Emily's."

"Aha? So you're going to take care of a woman who wasn't even here first?"

Uh...?

Is that how it comes across?

Certainly... from the perspective of the clan members who were just risking their lives, they might feel a little disappointed...

"... It was a joke, why are you taking it so seriously?"

"Uh. It's not that..."

"Hah, I don't know how a person like this made such a rule. 'The Captain has as many votes as all the members combined,' was it?"

"..."

Feeling like I would only make things worse by talking, I chose to remain silent.

And Raven, perhaps because she was focusing more than usual on putting the Essence into the vial, didn't pester me further.

I'm not sure how much time passed.

"It's done."

Soon, Raven handed me the vial containing the Essence of Sniktura, and with that, everything we needed to do here was finished.

And at the same time...

"Ugh, uwaaaah!!!"

Just then, Sven Parab, who had been unconscious, regained consciousness.

"Heop!"

He woke up screaming as if from a nightmare, immediately taking a stance as if to fight something.

It was an understandable reaction.

His last memory would have been of fighting Sniktura.

Vanguards fighting on the front lines often lose consciousness for a moment, so things like this happen from time to time.

"Wh-where am I...?"

However, after looking around and seemingly concluding it was safe, he relaxed his stance and looked around with a bewildered expression.

"D-did we win?!!"

"Yes. We won, so calm down."

"A-and no one died?"

"Can't you see for yourself?"

"Phew..."

When I answered with a shrug, he let out a sigh of relief as if it were a great fortune.

'Do I look like that to other people...?'

I couldn't know, but even if I did, it wasn't a bad feeling.

Seeing him check on his comrades' safety before his legs gave out made him seem reliable, and that actually looked good.

"But what happened?"

"What's the last thing you remember?"

"Well... you told us we were all going to die and to say our last words... Ah! I was despairing, thinking this was really how I was going to die..."

Sven Parab, who had been trailing off, suddenly raised his voice excitedly as if he remembered something.

"Ah! Divine Power! So, grasping at straws, I used the new Divine Power I obtained! But... did it work?"

"..."

"Huh? Why aren't you saying anything? Did it work? No, it must have worked, right? That's how we all survived, isn't it?"

His excited voice held more than just a sliver of hope; it carried a conviction that it had to be so, which made my heart even heavier.

"What was it? The effect of my Divine Power? Was it a defensive one? Or an offensive one? Could it be... it didn't kill Sniktura in one blow, did it?"

"..."

"Huh? Why is no one saying anything?"

At my silence, Sven Parab tilted his head and looked at the other members, but they too quickly avoided his gaze the moment their eyes met.

No, to be precise, they glanced at me, gauging the situation.

"H-hello...? Can you not hear me...?"

"..."

"Kaislan? Lady Marone? Miss Karlstein...?"

"..."

Tsk, I guess I have no choice but to take the fall.

It feels like we're ostracizing him, which weighs on my mind, but...

"Hoo..."

It can't be helped.

Wasn't it advice personally given by the Goddess herself when she descended?

Sven Parab must not find out about that Divine Power.

Right, so...

"Divine Power? What are you talking about?"

"... Huh?"

"... Nothing happened."

Even if I feel a little sorry, this is the right thing to do.

***

After leaving the laboratory and climbing the ladder back up to the initial bunker, Auyen came running out.

"Captain! I'm so glad you're safe...!"

"Thank you. You did something great this time."

"Not at all! It was just a coincidence that things worked out well. I didn't do anything!"

"What do you mean, you did nothing? Everyone survived thanks to you."

As I was sincerely praising Auyen's contribution, Sven Parab suddenly expressed his confusion.

"Huh? What do you mean everyone survived because of Lokrov?"

"..."

"Didn't we all work together to defeat Sniktura?"

"..."

"No, why is everyone suddenly silent again...?"

Sensing the sudden cold atmosphere, Parab once again wore a look of confusion.

Geez, if people say it's so, just accept it.

Still, thankfully, I didn't need to change the subject.

Because someone spoke up at just the right time.

[...

I was watching everything.

You've finally done it, heroes of the Empire!]

The voice of Arta, the master of the secret base, boomed through the speakers.

His tone was as if blessing heroes who had successfully completed a quest, but for me, who remembered him calling us selfish citizens of the Empire just a moment ago, it was simply absurd.

There was also a part I couldn't just ignore.

[When you suddenly destroyed the laboratory, I wondered what would happen, but in the end, my choice was the right one.]

"... The right one?"

[Look, what would have happened if I had opened the door? This world would surely not have been saved.]

Hah, as if that makes any sense...

I had a lot I wanted to say, but I held it back, thinking, 'what's the point of arguing with an AI?'

After all, I just need to get what I need from this guy and be on my way.

[Thanks to you, we have taken a great step on the path of salvation.

If you help just a little more, the end of this world can surely be prevented.

Ah, of course, we'll have to restore the laboratory you destroyed first.]

Restoring the laboratory...

This wasn't in the original game...

Then again, since the order of events changed and the laboratory was destroyed, I guess it's natural.

"So?"

[Heroes of the fallen Empire, I wish to ask one more thing of you.]

"Speak."

In truth, it was a story I didn't really need to hear.

Because the framework of the story had changed a bit, there were some minor differences, but the core was the same.

To put it briefly, he would help us, so we should go to the island that is the starting point of the apocalypse, defeat the boss, and bring back the byproducts...

"Alright, we'll do it."

[I knew you would answer thus, hopes of humanity.]

Sometime between then and now, I had been promoted from a hero of the Empire to the hope of humanity, but it wasn't worth paying attention to.

Because an unforgettable piece of information came up.

[Ah, and a human observed a little while ago at the starting point of the apocalypse bore the same mark as you.]

"You mean this?"

When I showed him the insignia of the Anabada Clan that I had, Arta affirmed.

[Yes, that insignia.]

"Was it a woman? With red hair?"

[Yes, is she a comrade?]

"Yes, she's my comrade."

At my answer, Arta showed a truly meaningful reaction.

[Hmm, then you must hurry.]

"...ГҜВјВҹГўВҖВқ

[That woman, it seems she's about to die.]

...

An unexpected quest has appeared.

--- Chapter 807 ---

Chapter 807: The Final Day (4)

Why was Amelia, who was supposed to be watching them on Skull Island, here?

I couldn't know for now, but I could guess one thing.

'For Amelia, who was supposed to be watching the Golden Ghost Clan, to be here...'

It was highly likely that they had come here as well.

Amelia wouldn't have come here without a reason.

"Did you happen to see anyone else besides my companion?"

However, Arta's answer to my question, which was based on that assumption, was different from what I expected.

[Well, so far, only that woman has been confirmed.]

"... Only one person?"

[Is there a problem?]

"No, there isn't. More importantly, I have a question. What situation is my companion in that you said she's about to die?"

[I don't know if she's lost, but that woman has already entered the Beast's territory.

It wouldn't be strange if she encountered it at any moment.]

The 'Memory of the Apocalypse' I was currently in was a kind of Hidden Field, just like the 'Larkaz's Maze' I had visited before.

And the 'Beast' could be called the boss of this field.

But Amelia had entered its territory?

'Damn it.'

There was no time to lose.

Not a single moment.

"Arta, is there any other information I should know?"

[As I said before, to defeat the Beast, you must keep my words in mind.]

"So there's nothing. Understood. I'll see you later."

There was no reason to hear the strategy guide again, so my conversation with Arta ended there.

Afterward, I quickly gathered the members and left the Arta Secret Base.

"You know what to do without me saying it, right?"

"Yes. We will not be late, Captain."

With Auyen's reliable confirmation, the ship set sail, quickly cutting through the waves.

Shwaaaaaaa-!

In a way, it was faster than when we first came here.

Part of it was due to jamming Magic Stones into the propulsion device without caring for efficiency, but...

The reason for this speed wasn't just the Magic Stones.

ГЈВҖВҢAuyen Rob has cast [Tailwind].ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢAuyen Lokrov has cast [Trade Route].ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢAuyen Rob has cast [Ghost Ship]...ГЈВҖВҚ

Seeing Auyen activate all of her skills, the members wore worried expressions.

"Uh... navigator? Are you alright? I know we're in a hurry, but they say the more haste, the less speed..."

"If you go all out from the start like that, you might collapse midway."

Just as you'd expect from explorers with strong rational tendencies, what I heard wasn't just simple empathetic concern.

If her mana ran out midway and her skills deactivated, we might end up arriving even later.

Therefore...

"Auyen, are you alright?"

I asked only this on behalf of the members.

"When we were coming, I had to think about the return trip, but that's not the case now, is it?"

After hearing that answer, I didn't ask anything else.

"Understood."

If we were experts in fighting monsters, she was an expert in navigation.

So it was probably right to just trust her and leave it to her.

Shwaaaaaaa-!

Like that, as we headed towards the island that still showed no sign of appearing from the ship's prow, I wonder how much time passed.

Tiri-ring, tiri-ring-

The 10-hour timer I had set when we entered the Arta Secret Base went off.

It was a timer I had set with plenty of leeway just in case of any unforeseen variables...

'I thought we'd be able to go a bit more leisurely on the way back...'

This is why you never know what will happen in life.

In the first place, the Sniktura raid also ended in a completely different way than I had thought.

"..."

Was it because there was nothing I could do but wait for the ship to reach its destination?

Looking at the endlessly stretching horizon, all sorts of thoughts came to mind, and I unknowingly clenched my fists and jaw.

"Don't worry too much... It'll be okay..."

"Yes, that's right, Old Man. It'll be okay."

The other members thought my expression was stiff because I was worried about Amelia, but that wasn't the whole reason.

While I was worried about Amelia, the things that had happened before kept coming to mind and tormenting me.

"Something big happened this time too, but in the end, everyone survived, right? Don't worry too much."

Erwen might have said it to comfort me, but in truth, it was a needle piercing my heart.

Just a few hours ago, we were almost wiped out.

No, in fact, we were already wiped out once.

I couldn't dare to find a way to overcome that situation, so I just accepted that fate and, for the first time since becoming a Barbarian, prepared a 'will'.

Of course, things somehow worked out, and it ended with us catching a First-Class Monster and an Essence dropping, but...

It wasn't because I was so great.

We only survived today because we were lucky.

To be honest, I hadn't even thought about the [Agent of the Star].

...

No, was it because I was incompetent that things turned out this way?

In fact, if I reviewed the last battle, we could have avoided being 'wiped out'.

If I had given up on Belleg sooner, the raid would have proceeded according to my plan.

But I couldn't, and I spun a complacent hopeful thought that if I sacrificed a bit more, things would work out somehow.

And the result of such stubbornness and obstinacy was disastrous.

Of course, in the end, it turned out well, but...

I wonder how long that luck will follow me.

No, in the first place, is it even plausible for a person like me to have such a complacent mindset, keeping 'luck' in mind?

'Of course not...'

Luck was with me only this time.

There won't be a next time.

If I'm in this state next time, I'll undoubtedly face the worst possible ending.

After thinking that, I could admit it more clearly to myself.

Kwaaaang-!

Right now, there was a problem with my head.

A very serious problem, at that.

***

Was it because I, who had been standing at the prow, suddenly slammed my head against the railing?

"..."

"..."

Suddenly, a silence began to descend upon the deck.

Belatedly realizing the silence, I looked around, and the members who met my eyes were startled and avoided my gaze.

"Ahem..."

"Well, I should tend to my equipment. Where is the polishing agent..."

Hah, I've done it again.

Whether I had a problem in my head or not, as a leader, I shouldn't have shown it on the outside.

It wasn't even like I was the only one having a hard time.

Just because you're an explorer doesn't mean you don't get stressed.

It's true that it's a profession close to death, but any great explorer feels extreme stress in the process.

That's also why there are particularly many explorers with ruined personalities.

Anyway, to make them walk on eggshells around me when they've all just overcome a near-death crisis and must be unknowingly accumulating fatigue and feeling sensitive...

"Hoo. Everyone, I'm sorry! I just felt like banging my head for a moment!!"

Still, when I shouted it out, a little jokingly with my honest feelings, the atmosphere loosened up a bit.

"... Haha, is that so?"

"... Ah, yes! Of course! You can feel like banging your head sometimes...! Isn't that right?"

"Ahaha, that's right...!"

They were slightly awkward reactions, but it was better than silence.

Perhaps my effort worked, as Raven, who had gone far away to avoid the gloomy me, came up beside me at some point.

"Looks like you have the energy to open your mouth now?"

"... Is there something you want to say?"

"Sniktura's Essence."

Ah, that...

I thought she had run right over to comfort me.

Well, maybe it's better that she's treating me normally like this?

"So what's the Supernatural Ability? I've been dying of curiosity since earlier. Is it the color you wanted?"

"Half."

"Then that means one of the colors was what you wanted?"

"Yes."

"No, why are you suddenly talking like that? You know full well what I'm curious about. Can't you just tell me plainly?"

"That's for later, not now..."

"Later? What later! That's the worst habit for an explorer. What if you die before 'later' comes? I'd probably die of frustration and not even be able to decompose into mana particles!"

"... Decompose into mana particles?"

At the strange word, I tilted my head without realizing, and Raven avoided my gaze.

"Ah, that. There are quite a few among mages. They believe that when they die, they don't go to the embrace of the gods or anything, but just decompose and turn into mana particles..."

Ah, so for us, it would be like being an atheist?

If I had to say, I'm on that side too.

I believe there's no such thing as an afterlife, and when you die, you just biologically decompose and return to nothingness.

Of course, I hope I'm wrong.

Separate from my beliefs, it would be nice if an afterlife really existed, and a place to rest comfortably appeared after death.

So that after a long time has passed, we can meet again, talk about old times, and see each other just one more time.

"Hello?"

Ah, we were in the middle of a conversation.

"Really! You're not going to tell me?"

"No, I was just about to tell you. But what was the question?"

"Sniktura's Essence!"

Ah, right...

Maybe because there's a problem with my head, my mind isn't working well.

So I just recited the information I had organized in my head.

"Sniktura's Essence basically includes two passive abilities. One is [Myriad Poison Body], and the other is [Final Flare]..."

As I went on to recite the effects of the passive skills and then the active skills, Raven, who had been listening with interest at first, was now looking at me with a frown.

"No, so what was the color you wanted?"

Ah, that was the question.

"To be honest, there wasn't a color I wanted that desperately."

"What?"

"Because the most important thing about this Essence was the passive ability, [Final Flare]."

"What? Not [Myriad Poison Body]?"

Well, that one is a great fit too, but it can't compare to this.

It makes sense, since the core Essence for Amelia is the DoppelgГғВӨnger's [Self-Replication].

So, later on, I fed her the Twin Hydra's Essence to greatly enhance her control over her clone with [Dual Dominion], and linked it with the Cyclops's [Wave Link] to strengthen the clone's performance to the level of the main body.

"That [Final Flare], that's the skill that guy used at the end, right? The one that activates right before death..."

Hearing that the key was the passive skill, Raven muttered something to herself, and then, as if she had realized something, she got excited.

"Ah! So that's why! If you use a clone, the drawback of not being able to turn it off at will once it's activated is practically gone!"

"Yes, that's right."

Of course, there's a restriction of being unable to act for one minute upon activation, but if you just wait that time out, the strongest clone, with stats several times more powerful than the main body, is born.

'If I just feed her that Essence and tweak the rest of her setup, she should meet the minimum requirements for the Tenth Floor.'

"Hey, so what's this Essence's Supernatural Ability?"

Ah, that.

Well, one of them is [Form Shift].

"Oh, isn't that really good?"

"When he used it, he could freely transform into other monsters and use their unique abilities, but in reality, it's a bit different."

"How is it different?"

"You can only transform into the last monster you defeated, and even if you transform, your physical abilities don't change."

"Ah... I see."

Her voice sounded somewhat disappointed, but that wasn't all there was to it.

After all, it wouldn't be a First-Class Essence for nothing, would it?

Of course, there are more varied ways to use it.

However, those detailed know-hows just need to be taught to Amelia, who will be the one using it.

"So what's the other one?"

"...[The Final Day]."

"What? There was a Supernatural Ability like that?"

"There was..."

"... You don't look too happy about it?"

"Because it's ambiguous."

I said they were all fine, but this one was an exception.

If [Seed of Destruction] had come out instead, it would have been more useful.

"What's ambiguous about it? Is it bad?"

Hmm, well...

I don't know if I should call this bad.

"Strictly speaking, it's on the good side, but several conditions must be met to use it."

"Ah, now that I think about it, I don't think he ever used it during battle? What kind of Supernatural Ability is it?"

Afterward, I spent a considerable amount of time explaining the skill's effects and activation conditions, and with her curiosity satisfied by my efforts, Raven also left.

And finally, the time I was alone.

Only then did I remember.

"Ah, wait a minute."

This wasn't the time for me to be worrying about someone else's Essence.

"...[Omen of Corruption]."

I got hit by that, didn't I?

--- Chapter 808 ---

Chapter 808: The Final Day (5)

[Omen of Corruption].

It was a peculiar skill that, upon being hit, would strangely alter the ability of one of your Essences.

In my case, it was Bayon's [Transcend] that was affected.

'It's a relief it wasn't Stormgush, at least...'

Even if I had avoided the worst-case scenario, I couldn't help but feel a sting of regret.

After all, [Transcend] was a core Essence that could be linked not only with [Giant Form] but also with various other Essences.

Though not as high on the priority list as [Giant Form], the fact remained that it had earned its place in the final version of the Shield Barbarian build.

'I wonder how it changed...'

I was dying of curiosity, wondering if it had turned into some ridiculously crappy build, but there was no way to know right now.

Once you were hit by this skill, the affected skill became unusable for 180 days.

'This is day 33 of the expedition...'

If we returned to the city on day 75 as originally planned, that would be 42 days.

Then 30 days in the city.

'Even if I complete the full 75 days on the next expedition, there'll still be 33 days left.'

In other words, I wouldn't be able to use [Transcend] and test what had changed until the expedition after next...

"Captain, the island is in sight now...!"

As I stood on the deck lost in thought, our destination finally came into view.

The sky around the island was stained red, as if it were a different world.

Lava had flowed into the sea and hardened.

And a dark red smoke covered the entire island.

"We are disembarking...!"

The total time it took to dock the ship and set foot on the island was three hours and fifty minutes.

We had cut about fifty minutes from our journey to the secret base...

"Good work, Auyen."

"Not at all..."

Auyen, who had been using his Supernatural Ability at maximum pace throughout the voyage, did not look well.

It seemed like he would fall into a state of Soul Exhaustion if he used his ability just a few more times.

"Rest now."

"Pardon?"

There was no need for a long explanation.

"Ainar!"

The moment her name was called, Ainar immediately understood the context of the conversation.

"Got it! I just need to carry him, right?"

"Excuse me? I-I'm fine-!"

"Haha! Don't worry about it! I'm used to carrying people!"

Okay, so Auyen's transportation was sorted...

The Orb of Fire had been kept on since we arrived on the island, so there was no need to turn it on again.

Therefore...

"We move at maximum speed from now on."

I led the members, who had recovered their stamina during the four-hour voyage, and quickly traversed the island.

Our destination was the center of the island, where Amelia had been seen.

However, even as we headed toward our destination, we conducted a thorough search of our surroundings.

After all, Amelia wasn't the only one in danger of dying.

ГЈВҖВҢYou have been afflicted with the status effect [Eternal Scorching].ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢYour character will continuously take Fire Damage, which will amplify over time.ГЈВҖВҚ

The damage increase from this field effect wasn't significant yet, so that was a secondary concern.

ГЈВҖВҢTime until death: 05:50:17.ГЈВҖВҚ

With about six hours remaining before the status effect [Time Limit] went off, we had to work quickly to reset everyone.

Like this.

"Raven, Marone. From now on, use the 'Distortion' spell on every monster you hunt."

While focusing on moving in our breakthrough formation, we cast the 'Distortion' spell on any monsters we encountered before hunting them.

A monster under the effect of the Distortion spell, instead of dropping an Essence upon defeat, has a chance to leave behind a corpse, allowing us to obtain a byproduct...

"Ah! It's a success!"

Instead of turning into particles of light and disappearing, the defeated monster collapsed to the ground.

Thud-!

It meant the Distortion spell had succeeded.

However, soon after, the corpse was engulfed in flames and turned to ash.

And...

Fwoosh-!

A red haze floated up into the air.

"Something that looks like... an Essence has appeared...?"

"That's the one."

In the game, its official name was 'Original Sin.'

I didn't know why it was given that name, but consuming it had two effects.

ГЈВҖВҢYour character has consumed 'Original Sin'.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢFire Resistance has permanently increased by +1.ГЈВҖВҚ

Fire Resistance increased by +1.

ГЈВҖВҢThe status effect [Time Limit] has been reset.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢYour character will die in 24 hours.ГЈВҖВҚ

The status effect [Time Limit] from the field effect would be reset, giving us another day.

For reference, the Fire Resistance increase was an effect you could only get the first time you consumed it; it wouldn't increase with subsequent uses...

Anyway, that wasn't what was important.

Unlike in the game, I couldn't just hover a cursor over the status effect icon to see the remaining time, so as I fed 'Original Sin' to the members sequentially, I had them immediately set a timer on their watches.

"This feels strange. To think this is my remaining time. Don't you think so, Mr. Parab?"

"Haha, yes... I suppose so."

"Wouldn't it be nice if we could actually see it like this all the time?"

"Pardon?"

"The remaining time. I was just thinking how great it would be if we could always know how much time we have left. It was like that a moment ago, wasn't it? When I thought everything would be over in a minute... I was just so bewildered. It made me wonder if there were really that many things I hadn't done in my life..."

"Not things you haven't done, but words?"

Even in this situation, Marone and Parab were sharing a conversation that felt somewhat sweet and awkward.

'Is there really something going on between them...?'

I couldn't be sure, but I had a feeling.

In the past, I would have been concerned about such personal relationships forming within the team.

But now, my thinking had changed a little.

"... There might not be a 'later.'"

After giving a vague reply to Erwen's question, I turned my attention away from Marone and Parab.

I didn't know what would become of their relationship, but I didn't want to interfere.

If it works out, it works out.

If not, then it doesn't.

As long as there are no regrets, that's what matters.

With that mindset, I focused on the present and headed towards the center of the island.

How much time had passed?

ГЈВҖВҢAinar Fenelin has consumed 'Original Sin'.ГЈВҖВҚ

"Well done."

A prime example is Riakis on the third floor.

Upon his appearance, he blocks the portals between floors and changes the entire field.

"No, that won't happen."

"...?"

"R-Really...? I'm jealous of you both..."

She just looked at me intently...

"Thank you. I'll make good use of it."

Amelia expressed her gratitude in such a simple manner.

It felt more sincere than a hundred words.

And...

I couldn't for the life of me understand what part of it was romantic; that sensibility was beyond me, but I answered her anyway.

"Ah, don't worry about that for now. The conditions are such that you might never be in a situation to use it properly in your entire life."

"Right, so..."

"[The Final Day]."

That curiosity of hers, really.

Flash-!!

"... Huh?"

What was that?

...

"... Perhaps, did I cause this mess?"

Things had gone seriously wrong.

ГўВҖВңAgain, huh.ГўВҖВқ

ThatГўВҖВҷs also one of the effects of [The Final Day].

Besides meteors and lightning, it occasionally spawns portals that spit out random monsters...

ГўВҖВңWonder whatГўВҖВҷll come out this time.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңEveryone, stay alert! According to Yandel, monsters up to Rank 2 can appear!ГўВҖВқ

As soon as the portal appeared, everyone formed up and prepared for battle.

And then...

ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs small?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңA small-type?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңDoesnГўВҖВҷt look very strong.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң.....e?ГўВҖВқ

[UwaaaaaaaaahhhhhhhГўВҖВ”!!!]

A hidden piece I didnГўВҖВҷt know about.

--- Chapter 810 ---

Chapter 810: Hidden Story (2)

Arta Secret Base.

And the Mechanical Beast Arta, who appears there as the Final Boss.

'This can't be a coincidence.'

My instincts as a Gamer who has played [Dungeon & Stone] for nearly ten years tell me.

That the two couldn't possibly just share the same name.

'Could it be that using 'The Final Day' on this island is the condition to meet this guy?'

As of now, I can't know.

So, I'll have to find out from now on.

"Raven, use a Barrier spell."

"Suddenly? You told me not to use it because of the mana consumption."

"This time, I think we need to talk for a moment, even if it means bearing the cost."

"I understand."

Receiving the order, Raven immediately cast a dome-shaped barrier around us.

A sort of umbrella, perhaps?

Kwwaaaaaa-!

Flash-!

Krrrrrumble-!

A translucent curtain blocking the meteorites and lightning falling from the sky.

Okay, now the environment for a quiet talk is set-.

[Oh, this is interesting.

A language system similar to the common tongue, yet different...

Still, seeing that some of you speak the common tongue, it must not have been completely lost? Well, the language doesn't matter much anyway.]

Just then, the man who was observing us muttered something.

"Activate Mental Will communication."

It was from that moment that his words began to sound different.

"Now, can you hear my words?"

"...!"

"Huh? Can you understand him, Marone...?"

"Yes? Yes, I don't know what it is... but I can hear his words. Even though my mind recognizes it as a different language..."

At Marone's reaction, the man asked cautiously.

"Excuse me... uh... is this your first time with Mental Will communication...?"

"Yes? Ah, yes. I don't know what it is, but I've never even heard of such a thing."

"Hoo. Seeing your attire, I thought that might be the case. It seems many technologies have been lost. Please don't be alarmed. We've simply connected the frequency circuits emitted by your brainwaves so that we can 'understand' each other's words. It... it's only natural to feel unfamiliar at first."

The man, who had introduced himself as 'Arta', said this and then shifted his gaze beyond the barrier Raven had cast.

And...

"Indeed, this is the world's last day. Isn't it exactly as I imagined...!"

The man's eyes shone as he looked at the outside world where meteorites and lightning poured down.

From suddenly popping out of a mysterious portal, he was suspicious in more than one or two ways, but despite that, he didn't feel particularly threatening.

And why would he...

"Hehe... with these research results, I won't have to worry about next year's budget...!"

First, his appearance, the way he spoke, what he was wearing...

The aura the man himself exuded, while a bit gloomy, felt harmless.

"Hey, you said your name was Arta?"

"... Yes? Ah, yes. I was distracted for a moment. But who are all of you? If it's alright, could you please tell me what the current situation is-."

"We'll be the ones asking the questions."

"Ah...! Yes! I suppose you all must be curious as well. I will answer everything you ask. In return, may I make one request? Ah! It's nothing grand, so please don't be so wary!"

"... Go ahead."

"If I provide you with all the answers you seek, could you also answer my questions? To be honest, I am also very curious about this situation, hahaha..."

What's with this guy?

Is he actually hiding his power?

I was slightly flustered by his bold attitude, which contrasted with his frail appearance, but getting information came first, so I readily nodded.

"Alright, I promise."

"Well then, please ask. What are you curious about?"

"Who are you?"

"As I introduced myself earlier, my name is Arta. Currently... no, should I say in the past? Anyway... I am a Chief Researcher at the Empire's First Magi-Tech Research Institute. Haha, it's not a boast... but above me, there are only the Deputy Director and the Director."

"Research Institute...?"

For a moment, that word reminded me of something, and as I furrowed my brow, the man showed his curiosity.

"Oh, do you know of our institution?"

Well, not really.

It's just that something comes to mind.

"By any chance, is it a Research Institute that used monsters as Test Subjects to create living weapons?"

"Pardon? We are not such a place. Our Research Institute is, so to speak, at the forefront of Magi-tech development. While weapons are made using our research data, we are not a Research Institute for making weapons."

Hmm, so that's how it is...

After all, the place I explored on the Underground First Floor was officially called the Panthelion Research Institute.

Not something like 'Magi-tech' this or that.

"Anyway, so what is a great guy like you doing here?"

"What else would a researcher from a Research Institute do? Research, of course."

"I don't have time, so stop playing with words."

"Ah... I suppose from your perspective, time must be short. To explain it simply so that a non-expert can understand... the research we were conducting was the observation of future Spacetime."

"Spacetime observation?"

"Yes, observing the future to change the present... That is the core of our research."

"... Not the past?"

"Haha! It seems you're talking about time travel. It was concluded a long time ago that changing the present through the past is impossible. That's why we decided to focus on the future."

What, so the research was a success, and this guy flew to the future? Is that the setup?

'All sorts of things appear in this fantasy world...'

It's absurd, but I get the setup itself.

"So, would it be alright if I asked just one question in the middle of this? To be honest, I've been dying of curiosity since a while ago."

"......"

"What year is it here now?"

So the setup is that we're people from the future.

***

RPG is short for Role-Playing Game.

Literally, it means a game where you play a role.

Broadly speaking, what I'm doing now is no different.

If you proceed with the story according to the concept, the episode is bound to end someday, and at its end, there is always a sweet Reward.

Therefore... now is the time to get clues to advance the story.

Having made that judgment, I continued to converse with the man for a long time, gathering information.

This isn't a game where you can just hold down the spacebar to skip dialogue and have it all neatly summarized in the quest window.

"The Year of the Dawn...? It can't be...! You're saying the era name itself has changed?"

"The world was once destroyed by a Witch, and now only a single city-state ruled by the Rafdonia Royal Family remains... Truly astonishing...!"

"Ah, you think I already knew the situation here? Haha... that is somewhat true. The time coordinate I designated was the end of infinite time, literally The Final Day!"

"Pardon? Why did I do such a thing? Isn't it obvious! I was curious about the end of the world I lived in!"

"Ah? Haha! You ask if I'm not scared? It's fine. If my heart stops or I'm in a dangerous situation, I can return to my original timeline at any time. The best Magi-tech researchers, who can revive anyone as long as they haven't died, are on standby there."

"Pardon? You ask why there are Magi-tech researchers and not Priests? Don't tell me Magi-tech has been lost here?"

"But then, why did the world become like this? According to what you said earlier, didn't the Witch who once tried to destroy the world already die?"

Through the conversation, Arta gained information about our side, and we, in turn, were able to hear Arta's story.

And to conclude...

"To think the end of the world really exists... I must find the cause of this apocalypse and a way to stop it. People of the future! Will you help me? If you help me, I will return to the past and find a way to prevent this tragedy!"

The quest content was roughly like this.

Well, the most important thing here is the Reward.

"... What will I give you as a Reward? Don't you want to stop the apocalypse? I have nothing I can give you..."

Hmm, so the Reward comes in a different way?

Like there's a treasure room that you can only enter with him, or something like that.

"It was a joke. If we fail, the world ends, so what does a Reward matter? I'll do anything if it can help stop this apocalypse."

"I knew it, I knew you were joking!"

Thus, the talk about the Reward ended on a lighthearted note.

"From now on, let's talk while we move."

"Yes, that's fine. I don't have much permitted time either."

"Permitted time?"

"Ah, I didn't mention that. The time I can stay here is limited. So, I must gather as much information as possible within that time and return."

"If you can't return within that limited time, what happens then?"

"That's unlikely to happen. Well, in that case, I would also face The Final Day here with you."

"I see."

I brushed it off as if it were simple curiosity.

I can't just tell him outright, can I?

That if no special situations or rewards appear while I'm taking him around, I'm considering forcibly preventing him from returning.

'Maybe if he can't go back, the real quest begins.'

Since this is a Hidden Element I've never encountered while playing the game, I judged that I should try everything I can.

"Raven! Dispel the magic now!"

Afterward, I had Raven release her Barrier spell and began to seriously tour the island again.

And, at the same time.

"Excuse me. You're Mr. Arta, right?"

Raven, who hadn't been able to join the conversation because she was concentrating on her magic, also began to seriously talk to Arta, and the conversation proceeded...

"Mr. Arta?"

"......"

"Perhaps. You don't want to talk to me?"

"Ah, it's not that... I'm a bit shy around strangers..."

"But you were talking just fine with Mr. Yandel?"

"......"

I was concentrating, hoping for some useful information to come up, but so far, all that's been revealed is that Arta is shy in front of women.

And...

"I'm also interested in Magi-tech. Could you tell me a little about it?"

"Oh! You're so beautiful, and you're interested in Magi-tech?! Is that true!"

"... Pardon? Ah, yes, that's right..."

"Haha! Please ask anything. I will answer you...!"

It seems Arta has a weak spot for women who are interested in Magi-tech.

'Okay, so from now on, if there's anything to dig up, I should just have Raven do it.'

I listened to the conversation a bit more after that, but the talk was all about Magi-tech, which sounded like a foreign language, so I skipped it.

However, since it was a good thing for Raven's knowledge to increase, I didn't stop them and just focused on conquering the island.

And...

[Over 50% of the monsters in the area have been defeated.]

[The Divine Power of the Ancient Beast is weakened.]

[Over 60% of the monsters in the area have been defeated.] [The Divine Power of the Ancient Beast is weakened.]

[Over 70% of the monsters in the area have been defea...]

When about 70% of the island had been cleared.

"Ahem... I, I'm sorry but... now is not the time for this. I must focus more on gathering information about this time period."

"Uh..."

"D-Don't worry! I'll find a way to save you, Miss Raven, no matter what...!!"

It seemed Arta was slowly coming to his senses, as he ended the Magi-tech talk and returned to his main business.

"Um... you said your name was Mr. Yandel, right? You said the cause of the apocalypse is a Beast, so why aren't we going there?"

Ha, to think he's only asking that now.

Just how weak is he to women who are his type?

It was absurd, but I answered him anyway.

"It's because the Beast weakens only after we defeat the island's monsters."

"Ah. I see."

"Did you find out anything while looking around?"

"Um... I'm not sure yet."

Right, of course.

He was too busy excitedly chatting with Raven.

"Ah! But there is one thing I found out!"

"What is it?"

"The center of the island, where the Beast that caused the apocalypse is said to be! I'm detecting some kind of powerful, unknown Magic Wave from there."

A Magic Wave...

Could it be a hidden space that I failed to discover in the game?

Or is it simply the boss's location?

Well, either way, I'll find out soon enough.

[Over 90% of the monsters in the area have been defeated.] [The Divine Power of the Ancient Beast is removed.]

Because now, I'm planning to go to the Boss fight.
