--- Chapter 811 ---

Chapter 811: Hidden Story (3)

'Memory of the Apocalypse'

A Hidden Field accessible through Skull Island.

In truth, this place was originally supposed to end after defeating the Beast, the boss of the island I called Doom Island.

But if you find the hidden elements, you can enjoy additional content.

For example...

If you leave the island and go to the Arta Secret Base, you can proceed with the Sniktura Raid.

After that, you can accept a Quest to defeat the Beast, the boss of Doom Island, and return to receive an additional Reward.

And if you refuse the additional Reward there, it opens up the Raid for the true boss, Mechanical Master Arta...

'I planned to skip the Arta Raid, so there's no need to think about it.'

In reality, the only content left on this island is the second stage: defeating the Beast and obtaining the additional Reward.

Ah, of course, that would be the case if it weren't for this guy.

"Hmm, at first I thought it was due to the difference in mana concentration, but now I see that the very nature of the mana in the air is peculiar. This kind of arrangement is a structure that usually only appears when artificially created..."

Just what is this guy?

Could there have been a hidden chapter on this island besides the final one where you fight the mechanical beast Arta?

A Gamer's intuition says that's highly likely.

And because of that...

"I'll have to return to the original timeline and find the island at these coordinates to know for sure. If the original island also has this arrangement, it could simply be seen as a characteristic of the island itself."

Since I couldn't be sure, I listened carefully to every one of his lines.

That's usually how it is, right? Most hidden elements in a game often start from a minor comment by an NPC.

'Well, in his case, I honestly don't even know what he's talking about.'

Mana, arrangement, concentration... no matter what he says, there's no way I, who am not even a Mage, could understand.

But I do know that the correct path is to take him and finish the Beast Raid first.

The reason is simple.

"The center! We must hurry to the center! Only by observing that place can we confirm the definite difference when we return later-"

This guy, he keeps trying to send us in that direction.

"Don't be so impatient. It's not like it'll appear right away just because we go to the center."

"Pardon?"

"Just know that it is what it is-"

Just as I was about to finish my sentence.

[Groooooooooooowl!]

The Beast's howl covered the entire island.

A ferocious cry echoed from all directions, making it pointless to determine its origin.

Character has entered the radius of [Song of the End].

I hurriedly stopped moving and shouted.

It was much sooner than I had expected, but.

"Prepare for battle!!"

The beast has appeared.

***

The boss of Doom Island, the Beast.

The method to summon it is very simple.

Because if you stay within its territory for a long time, it appears naturally.

For reference, there were no other summoning methods.

At least, that's how it was in the game I played.

"To your formations...!!"

That's why, even as we quickly formed a battle formation, a little doubt arose.

'...

It appears as soon as we enter?'

In fact, this never happened when I played the game.

No matter how fast I was, I had to wander around its territory for at least an hour or two before it appeared.

But for it to be summoned the moment we stepped inside.

"Oh...! Can it be that the beast has appeared?"

As expected... is this this guy's influence?

Well, that's not what I should be thinking about right now.

"Alright, from now on, stay over there."

"Yes, understood!"

First, after sending Arta to the safe rear.

Thump-!

My body, quickly heated up before the battle, headed for the front line.

And then...

"Hoo..."

As always, I calmed my body by steadying my breath.

It was like a habit athletes have before a match.

After exhaling a long breath like this, I somehow feel a little calmer...

Squeeze-

I believe.

That feeling of having become calmer will raise the odds of victory, even if only by a little.

Rrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrumble-!

"It's coming...!"

Soon, the ground undulated like a wave, and with lava surging through it, the 'Beast' revealed itself from within.

Kraaaaaaaaaaaash-!

From now on, every minute and every second is important.

And for good reason, because its aura skill, [Song of the End], has a very peculiar effect.

All time is accelerated.

It's only a one-line explanation, but as you can see from ability-battle manga, most abilities are more broken the simpler and shorter their descriptions are.

And the [Song of the End] is no exception.

Time remaining until death.

22:50:43.

From now on, the remaining time for the status effect [Terminal] will decrease several times faster.

Character continuously takes fire damage, and the said fire damage is amplified over time.

The field effect [Eternal Scorching] is the same.

A time when even the 'Orb of Fire', which halves the damage, becomes unbearable will arrive much faster.

Therefore, in my original exploration plan, I had set a fixed time for this Raid.

'30 minutes.'

The maximum is 30 minutes, and if I judge it will take longer, it's considered a Raid failure, and we will flee without looking back.

'...

Though that's unlikely to happen.'

The Beast, it's true that it's a difficult boss, but it's much easier than the Serpent of the End, Sniktura.

Ah, of course, on the premise that I know the strategy.

"Oh.. Bjorn!! Is that the one!!"

Seeing the Beast appear in front of her, Ainar shouted in a strangely excited voice.

After seeing its bones on Skull Island, she'd been singing about wanting to catch it later, and it seems she wasn't just talking.

'Still, I thought she'd be a little intimidated seeing it in person.'

Having activated [Giant Form] first, I covered my upper body with my Shield and stared at the beast beyond it.

Height, about 9 meters.

A size large enough to comfortably fit into the 'colossal' category by monster classification standards.

Its skeleton is a bit similar to the Lord of Chaos Riakis.

In that it walks on two legs and has a large tail.

From a Modern Person's perspective, it's like a dinosaur.

[Groooooooooooowl-!!!!]

Soon, the beast crouched its upper body and let out a monstrous scream.

A vibration strong enough to feel the air tremble on my skin.

In truth, this isn't just a simple howl, but a proper skill.

The Ancient Beast has cast [Final Ultimatum].

For 5 minutes, the Physical Stats of all characters within the radius will gradually decrease.

If hit by this skill, physical-related stats decrease for a certain period.

It's an area-of-effect Debuff, but the reduction value is low, and the 5-minute time limit is quite long, so this skill alone isn't that valuable, but...

All time is accelerated.

When combined with this effect from [Song of the End], it becomes a sufficiently broken skill.

"Ugh.. what is this! My strength is draining... I keep hearing strange noises too...!"

A skill that would normally take 5 minutes now only takes a mere 1 minute to reach full stacks.

Moreover, since it's a skill with no cooldown, you can assume the effect lasts for the entire battle.

Well, it's not like I didn't bring a way to counter it.

"Ugh... do we have to fight like this?"

Hah, she's at it again.

I definitely explained everything during the exploration plan briefing.

"Raven!"

At my short call, Raven cast a spell she had prepared in advance.

Arua Raven has cast the Fifth-Rank Curse Magic [White Sound].

Character's hearing is temporarily paralyzed.

As soon as the spell took effect, a silence fell over us, like wearing earplugs in a quiet room.

A state of perfect silence where I couldn't even hear rustling or my own breathing.

The reason for casting this spell is simple.

[Final Ultimatum] has been lifted.

Casting this spell lifts a Debuff that cannot be removed by any Magical Tool, Consumable, Divine Spell, or Purification-Type Ability.

And surprisingly...

"......"

I don't actually know the principle behind it either.

For a Gamer, that's not what's important, is it?

A Gamer who values efficiency doesn't ask questions.

They simply use it because they can.

'So it works in reality too.'

During the game, I happened to discover that using this spell would lift that Debuff, and at the time, I thought it was a bug.

But, after actually entering the city of Rafdonia and exploring the Labyrinth countless times, I came to know.

There are no such things as bugs in that game.

'Still, it feels good.'

In truth, it would be a lie to say I wasn't worried at all.

If this hadn't worked, I would have had to give up on the Raid and run.

Shhh.

After entering silent mode due to the magic, we continued the Raid by communicating through hand signals as promised.

Just like this.

The Ancient Beast has cast [Knot of Destruction].

For a certain period, damage taken is greatly reduced, and upon completion of the cast, stats are greatly increased in proportion to the damage taken...

Raising a clenched fist means stop dealing damage.

The Ancient Beast has cast [Devour].

Waving an open hand back and forth means everyone except me dodges.

Belleg Shusia di Tersia has cast [Horn of Souls].

When Summoned Creatures strike the same target, they inflict additional damage.

Raising an index finger means only the ranged positions attack.

Ainar Fenelin has cast [Focused Shot].

Erwen Fornachi di Tersia has cast [Rupture].....

Raising the index and middle fingers together means both ranged and melee attack.

And then...

Shhh.

Raising the middle finger like this.

----!!!

--!! -

--!! -

--!

They each use their ultimate skill to unleash their maximum Focused Fire.

However...

'Is it because I can't hear anything?'

It definitely feels a bit dull, but the Raid proceeded smoothly nonetheless.

The Ancient Beast has cast [Earth Tremor].

Phase 1 ended.

The Ancient Beast has cast [Horn of the End].

We also safely passed Phase 2, where it summons a near-army's worth of Summoned Creatures.

The Ancient Beast has cast [Return].

There were no major problems in Phase 3 either.

If I had to pick a dizzying moment, it would be when Ainar was hit by lightning and got Stunned, nearly causing us a major setback.

'18 minutes to get this far is plenty of time.'

And so, 18 minutes after the battle began, we entered the final phase, Phase 4.

The Ancient Beast's HP is below 20%.

It slowly recovers its lost Divine Power.

Phase 4 has two major characteristics.

The first is that the 'Divine Power' it had sealed by wiping out the island's monsters recovers over time.

And the second is...

The Ancient Beast has cast 'Power of Annihilation'.

The first Divine Power it uses is always locked onto the 'Cursed One'...

Wow, Power of Annihilation right from the start?

Surprisingly, the most powerful Divine Power was used right from the beginning.

A target-type Instant Kill Skill with no casting time, impossible to dodge or block.

---!

A formless mist bloomed from the Beast's body and silently moved towards its target.

Ah, the target, as I said before, is already decided.

The one who got the 'Cursed One' title by delivering the Last hit to the boss on Skull Island was none other than...

the Guild Master, Iria Adnus.

Of course, I didn't plan this Raid with the intention of using the Guild Master as a sacrifice.

It's not like I'm some kind of vicious Barbarian.

Anyway, the first Divine Power isn't fully recovered, so its effect is very minimal.

Well, it gets stronger later on.

"......!!!"

Anyway, even if she gets hit by that, she won't 'instant death' right away.

It's just that something she has will be 'annihilated'...

"...?"

I wonder what it could be.

What did the Guild Master lose after being hit by that?

A regenerable body part?

Her gear?

Or perhaps a part of her Essence?

Hurriedly turning to check on the Guild Master, I let out a silent gasp without realizing it.

'Ah...'

Lava, meteorites, lightning, and more.

Was it because there were many things acting as a light source nearby?

A Halo of Light, reflective as a mirror.

'So that can happen too...'

Her hair was annihilated.

--- Chapter 812 ---

Chapter 812: Hidden Story (4)

The Guild Master's hair disappeared.

Not just his sideburns or any specific part, but all of it.

It had 'disappeared' so cleanly that his scalp was smooth and shiny.

'...

That turned out well.'

I forced myself to look away and find the positive side.

You know how it is.

When two people look at a cup that's exactly half-full, one will think there's not much left, while the other thinks there's still this much left.

In the grand scheme of life, the latter mindset is more beneficial.

Well, in this case, the expression 'turned out well' was actually fitting.

'It's a bit unsightly, but... if it just ended with his hair, it's a huge gain, isn't it?'

No matter how I thought about it, losing his hair was a much smaller penalty than losing the equipment he was wearing, or having some of his Essence's skills or stats removed.

Hair will just grow back, after all.

However, I would absolutely hate to go bald myself...

'Behel-Laaaaaaaaa!!'

I roared as I charged forward.

My companions, including myself, couldn't hear it anyway.

But at least that bastard would.

[ГўВҖВ” ГўВҖВ”!!! !.]

Indeed, as if in response to my war cry, the beast opened its maw wide and roared.

Well, I couldn't hear the sound, but.

'Ugh, saliva...

I always feel this way, but getting splattered with saliva is more disgusting than blood.

But there was no time to wipe my face, so I charged on with it dripping down.

And....

KraaaaaashГўВҖВ”!!!

After blocking another one of the beast's basic attacksГўВҖВ”a creature several times my sizeГўВҖВ”with my Shield.

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has used [Aegis Dragon Armor].ГЈВҖВҚ

I activated the use effect of the armor I obtained outside the Fortress Wall, not in the Labyrinth.

ГўВҖВ”!

Instantly, the beast's pupils dilated.

It must have been surprised.

As if a Viking Warrior suddenly appearing before it, larger than itself, wasn't enough.

ГўВҖВ”!!!

Who would have imagined I'd smash a Greatsword down on the crown of its head?

'I hope it likes this, though.'

Because the reflected damage from the Dragon Armor is proportional to the damage I've accumulated from taking a beating for eighteen minutes.

ГЈВҖВҢReflecting accumulated damage.ГЈВҖВҚ

I'm just returning what I received.

***

It didn't take long for the Beast raid to come to a complete end.

After we unleashed all our focused fire for three minutes on the Beast, which was on the verge of death due to the Dragon Armor's damage reflection, it soon lost its strength and collapsed to the ground.

However, if I had to point out one thing that happened during that process...

ГЈВҖВҢThe Ancient Beast has cast 'Divine Power of Mutation'.ГЈВҖВҚ

Just a tick before it was defeated, the beast used its Divine Power one more time.

And surprisinglyГўВҖВҰ.

"....!!!"

The Guild Master was chosen as the target again.

Unlike the first time, he was chosen randomly this time, not fixed...

..

Does this even make sense?

Getting picked twice was strange enough, but the effect was also truly unbelievable.

'...

He turned into a woman.'

The Guild Master with the shiny head's bone structure became slender, and he was given all the external features of the female gender.

To put it simply, he had become the female version of Iria Adnus...

"ГўВҖВ”? ГўВҖВ”!!!"

I'm curious what he's trying to say.

Swish, swish.

When I gestured to her, Raven seemed to understand and dispelled the magic affecting all of us.

"Wh-what is this situation...! Ah, ah, first my hair... and now my, my, my chest, why is it like this...!!!"

"That's not the only thing that's changed."

At my reply, the now-female Iria Adnus took out a mirror to check his reflection and was utterly shocked, but I didn't have time to console him at length.

"Calm down. Your hair will grow back."

"That's not the problem...!!"

"Are you discriminating against women right now?"

"No, this isn't discrimination, it'sГўВҖВ”!!"

"Don't worry. You'll return to normal after some time."

"... Is that really true?"

"Yes, I swear it on my Warrior's Honor."

I considered pretending not to know and teasing him, but I figured he'd just be a nuisance throughout the expedition if I let him be, so I quickly confessed the truth.

After all, we have things to do now that the hunt is over, don't we?

"Oh, I can hear again!!"

"But. Is it really over, meow...?"

"It doesn't seem to be moving..."

"More importantly... the Guild Master..."

"Don't say it. He can hear you..."

The members looked at the fallen Beast with doubt, and a few were slightly anxious.

I can't say I don't understand.

"You can lower your guard. It's all over."

"But why... isn't the corpse disappearing, meow...?"

Ah, that? It's simple.

For some reason I don't know, this 'Beast' thing is classified as an Unranked monster.

Even in the game, you didn't get any Experience Points for killing it, and its corpse wouldn't disappear even without casting Distortion Magic.

Except for when it dropped an Essence.

"Ah... So that means no Essence dropped, huh..."

Hearing my explanation, Misha licked her lips in disappointment.

Well, it's not like I wasn't disappointed either.

The Beast's Essence could have been Misha's graduation Essence if she got it.

'If the Essence had dropped this time, I could have immediately fed her a linking Essence...'

Of course, this doesn't mean we have to come back to this island several times to try again.

That would be incredibly time-consuming, and besides, since the dual-wielding frost swordsman is a core part of my strategy, there are multiple types.

Type A, which uses the Beast's Essence as its core.

Type B, which uses other Essences as its core.

'There's also a Type C, but its potential is low, so it's best to put it on the back burner...'

This game has such a low Drop Rate for Essences that you have to be flexible with your build and grow through various combinations like this to reach the Gate of the Abyss.

'It's a shame... but it's not a complete loss.'

It's not as good as an Essence, but this thing's corpse is valuable enough on its own.

For example...

"What are you all doing! Get to skinning it!"

As for its hide, its entire body has performance comparable to Drake leather, which is a Tier 4 material.

"D-Drake leather, you say? The one that's famously expensive...?"

"I heard that for Drakes, only the hide on their back is usable, but to think the entire hide of this huge monster is comparable..."

"Then how much is it worth...?"

Well, if we sell it all, we should get enough money to buy one or two Third-Rank Essences, I think...

But it's too early to be surprised

"Be especially careful when skinning the head. That part is on par with Ogre leather."

"Huk...! Are you saying it's a Tier 5 material...?"

"And for the bones, make sure to take the skull. If you make a sword from it, it'll be as hard as Adamantium."

"... Now I see, this thing really was a treasure."

It was a colossal monster, not a size that one or two people could butcher, but with a truckload of skilled explorers, the looting was finished quickly.

The skull bones.

The hide.

The heart and various other byproducts that Raven set aside, saying she wanted to research them.

After we packed everything we could, I saw Arta, who had just been staring blankly at the process.

"Ah, right, she was here too."

Her expression was strange, so I quickly approached and spoke to her.

"Is something wrong?"

"... It's strange."

"What is?"

"If this Beast was truly the cause of the apocalypse... all of you should have died here. No, even if you didn't die, you should have failed. I came along in the first place to find a clue to stop the apocalypse by observing that process."

What, so you were just watching from the back, thinking we were all going to die?

"... So what are you trying to say?"

"... That it's just too strange...! My research was on conceptualizing time to derive coordinates. And that research succeeded, bringing me here to today, 'The Final Day'."

"I get it, so get to the point."

"If you all were victorious... then why is today The Final Day?"

Having said that, Arta stated with conviction, not doubt.

"I'm sorry, but the Beast you just defeated... is not the cause of this apocalypse."

Hmm, how strange.

The Mechanical Beast Arta in the secret base across the sea said this was the cause.

But another Arta with the same name is saying it's not.

"Then what is the cause?"

"That's what we need to find out now."

"How?"

"That powerful Magic Wave I mentioned earlier... at first, I thought it was coming from the 'Beast' you spoke of, but it wasn't. I can still feel that wave even now that the Beast has been defeated."

"So you want to go there and check it out?"

"Yes, that's right. I don't have much time left."

"Understood. Let's depart immediately."

There was no time or reason to hesitate.

I had anticipated that something hidden would appear after we dealt with the Beast.

It also seemed like she had a specific place she wanted to take us to.

"How much time is left?"

I checked that first, but time was tighter than I'd thought.

"Well... for some reason, the time has decreased drastically since that Beast appeared..."

Ah, it's because of [Song of the End].

Does this mean I should send her far away during the next raid?

The thought crossed my mind, but I wondered if I'd ever use this Hidden Piece again.

To be honest, I still don't really know the conditions for her to appear.

In the first place, I don't understand why [The Final Day] was even used.

Therefore...

"I know everyone's tired, but hang in there a little longer. We're moving at full speed from now on."

After quickly packing up all the spoils of war, we swiftly headed in the direction Arta was guiding us.

And...

"This is the place...?ГўВҖВқ

"Yes, I can feel a very strong Magic Wave from here. Let's go in!"

Surprisingly, the place we arrived at was a cave with a Portal.

'There shouldn't be anything hidden here.....'

Honestly, I was a bit puzzled.

Because I was confident that I had thoroughly searched this cave while playing the game.

"It's here...! Just a little further this way."

Soon, Arta announced that we had almost reached our destination, which made me tilt my head in confusion once more.

And for good reason...

"Is this really the place...?"

I thought there might be a hidden space I didn't know about, but the place we arrived at was where the Portal was.

"Let's hurry inside!"

Still, since we were here, I moved with Arta to where the Portal was.

And...

WhooooooshГўВҖВ”!!

Before long, the ominously crimson Portal came into our view.

"Huh...?"

Upon discovering the Portal, Arta immediately let out a dazed sound.

And...

"... A Portal? Why is a Portal here...? Why? What, what went wrong? This can't be..."

She seemed extremely confused.

No, Arta fell into a complete panic and started muttering to herself.

"W-wait a minute..."

Soon, Arta began to back away like a frightened person, and so I intervened.

"What's the problem? It's just a Portal."

At my question, Arta's expression grew even more bizarre, and then her eyes widened as if she'd realized something.

And...

"... I will ask just one thing."

She continued, her voice much calmer than before.

"By any chance, is this place I'm in... a Labyrinth?"

At that question, I too was dumbfounded.

What is this situation now?

--- Chapter 813 ---

Chapter 813: Hidden Story (5)

NPC.

Non-Player Character.

In game terms, Arta was clearly an NPC.

No, to be precise, a Hidden PieceГўВҖВҰ

In the words of this world, should I call it 'Gabrielius's Arrangement'?

Anyway, that's not what's important.

"By any chance, is this place I'm in the Labyrinth?"

I was genuinely surprised by Arta's words.

It was only natural.

Whether a Hidden Piece, an Arrangement, or an NPC, anything belonging to the Labyrinth could not be free from the 'law'.

In short, monsters cannot cross through a Portal to move to another floor.

The same went for monsters or NPCs that could hold a conversation.

Not only could they not move between floors, they even believed this world within the Labyrinth to be the 'real world'.

[It has been a long time since I've seen a living being.

Where have you all come from?]

The Vampire of the Bloodstained Fortress was like that.

[You curs of the Empire, consumed by greed.

Today, I shall punish you.]

The Knight of the Apocalypse I met at the White Temple was also like that.

[...

This place is a Labyrinth.

I was exploring this Labyrinth when I met you.]

[The Labyrinth... what is that?]

The Vampire I met again on the Underground First Floor.

The Duke, presumed to be the original version of Cambormir, was not much different in this regard.

That was why, when I spoke of the history of the 'current time', I intentionally made no mention of the Labyrinth.

But what was with this guy?

[As I introduced myself earlier, my name is Arta.]

[I am a Chief Researcher at the First Magi-Tech Research Institute!]

[Haha, it's not a boast, but... above me, there are only the Deputy Director and the Director.]

It certainly seemed like he believed this world within the Labyrinth was his own.

"What do you mean, is this place the Labyrinth?"

"It's just as I said. Please answer me, is this very place where I'm standing inside the Labyrinth?"

This guy knew of the existence of the 'Labyrinth'.

Well, I'd have to find out if the Labyrinth he knew and the Labyrinth I knew were one and the same.

"... What is the Labyrinth?"

"May I take that to mean you know nothing about the Labyrinth?"

Hmm, well...

For some reason, I had a hunch that if I just said yes, the conversation would end right there.

So, I deliberately gave a meaningful answer.

"Well, that depends on your answer."

With those words, I gave off an air of someone who would absolutely not show his cards first.

Arta stared at me for a moment, then quickly continued speaking.

"The Labyrinth is an Artificial Dimension created through the Power of Origin. We can create the laws of the world. Within the Labyrinth, anything can be newly created, and we can freely move to any place we wish to go. And the 'Portal' was a means of transportation within the Labyrinth devised by our Empire."

Arta's rapid-fire explanation was difficult to understand, but despite that, a chilling feeling coexisted, making my heart drop for some unknown reason.

"... The Empire created the Labyrinth?"

"That is correct. His Majesty the Emperor desired, through this Labyrinth, an eternal Life, absolute Divine Power, and a perfect, flawless world like a paradise, eternally controlled beneath him."

"......"

"Because God is a being with far too many flaws."

It was confusing.

Honestly, I didn't quite understand even as I was listening.

I couldn't tell if what this guy was saying was true, or if it was just part of his lore.

However, there was one thing I became curious about.

It might be a very trivial thing to ask in this situation, but...

"But why call it a Labyrinth?"

"... I cannot understand the meaning of your question."

"No, I mean, if you wanted to create a world like paradise, why is its name 'Labyrinth'?"

"I do not know why you are curious about that, but I will answer you."

Arta continued without the slightest hesitation.

"There is always an answer at the end of the Labyrinth."

"......?"

"It is an old proverb that has been passed down since long before my time. And His Majesty spent his entire life searching for an answer to save this world. In accordance with His Majesty's will, this grand project to build a paradise through an Artificial Dimension was given the name 'Labyrinth'."

"......"

"On the day the Sweat of Knowledge you have shed leads us to the end of this Labyrinth, we will finally head toward an eternal paradise. This is part of a speech His Majesty gave when he visited our Research Institute. Does that answer your question?"

"... It's enough."

"Now, please give me your answer."

Arta looked at me and asked once more.

"Is this place inside the Labyrinth?"

Well...

Honestly, I still didn't know for sure.

No, if anything, I was even more confused than before.

But...

"Yes, it is inside the Labyrinth."

A fact was a fact.

After I let the affirmative answer out.

A long silence followed.

Arta was silent, his mind seemingly complex, and I left him undisturbed.

And after a moment..

"......"

"......"

What broke the long silence was neither me nor Arta.

It wasn't the tactless Ainar, nor was it Raven with her thriving academic curiosity.

Beep-beep-beep-beep, beep-beep-beep-beep-

An alarm sounded in a regular pattern.

The sound originated from within Arta's clothes.

As I naturally turned my gaze, Arta also ended his thoughts and opened his mouth.

"It means the time to return is imminent."

"I see."

I answered as if indifferent, but inwardly, I was pondering.

Should I leave him be and talk more during the remaining time?

Or should I put the thought I had a while ago into practice and forcibly prevent him from returning?

Of course, the latter option was one I intended to pursue inevitably if no special situations or rewards appeared while I had him with me.

'Well, it is that kind of situation.'

The only thing that had changed from back then was me.

At that time, I was focused only on rewards and events, but not anymore.

It was now judged to be far more important than such rewards.

The 'story' this guy held.

It might even be helpful in uncovering the secrets of this world.

'For that reason, knocking him out would be-.'

Just as I finished my internal conflict and was about to make a decision.

"Stop."

Arta suddenly cut off my thoughts.

As if he could hear my thoughts.

For a moment, I wondered if he had a Magical Tool for mind-reading or something similar, but thankfully, that wasn't the case.

"When observing the future, I sometimes meet people with eyes like yours."

"ГўВҖВҰI wasn't thinking anything."

"Haha, if that's the case, then I'm relieved. I have experienced this many times, and accordingly, I am prepared with various countermeasures for different situations."

Was he telling the truth?

I couldn't know, but him saying it to my face like that was quite effective.

It made me much more hesitant.

'What should I do...?'

The internal conflict I thought was over deepened once again.

If this were a game, I wouldn't have needed to think for long.

Whatever happened, I could just try again later-.

"How about you let go of your greed and use this little remaining time usefully? For all of us, both you and I."

At that moment, Arta interrupted my thoughts again, speaking as if making a suggestion, and I just honestly revealed my true intentions.

Right, honesty is a Barbarian's virtue, isn't it?

"I don't know about you, but what's in it for us?"

"Because I know why today is 'The Final Day'."

"... What?"

"I didn't know before, but thinking of this place as inside the Labyrinth made it easy. Why something like this happened."

"... Fine, let's talk."

After much thought, I decided to choose the peaceful method.

After all, his tone and expression had changed 180 degrees, as if his previous clueless attitude was just an act...

There were many things that gave me pause before jumping at him.

For one, his claim of having countermeasures didn't seem like a lie.

Unless I was certain of a way to break through those countermeasures, it was risky to make a move when I didn't even know what hidden cards he had.

Therefore...

"Since we don't have much time, let's be fair. How about we ask one question each?"

"Fine."

Even the method of conversation was decided.

Coincidentally, it was a method of conversation I was very familiar with.

From Auril Gavis, to Lee Baekho, the Round Table, and so on...

For some reason, this kind of situation had happened often since long ago.

The people here all wanted a lot, but none of them wanted to lose a thing.

...

Not that I was any different.

'It's a shame I don't have Twisted Trust...'

After using the last charge last time, I had been trying to keep a spare, but it was such a rare item that I hadn't been able to get one in advance.

'Well, I don't even know if it would work on him in the first place.'

So, that was enough of that regret.

"Then I'll go first-."

"No, I'll go first."

"... Please be quick."

And so began my turn.

The first question was already decided.

But I didn't know what kind of shocking answer might come back...

[Continue what you were about to say.

Why is today 'The Final Day'?]

From now on, it would be safer to conduct the conversation using the 'Ancient Language' for security.

Fortunately, as if he understood my intention, Arta turned off his translation device and answered.

Ah, of course, it wasn't the answer I wanted.

[Ask another question.]

[What?]

[Do not worry.

I do not mean that I will not answer, but simply that we should delay the order a bit.]

[Why must we do that?]

[Because it is highly probable that only then will you be able to properly understand what I am saying.]

I was suspicious that he might be playing some trick, but it didn't seem like he was just saying it, so I simply accepted.

[Alright, then I'll ask again.

What is the name of 'His Majesty' you mentioned earlier?]

It was a question I threw out, wondering if the name of the Immortal King or the Dawn King might come up.

[I have never heard that name before...]

But unfortunately, the name of 'His Majesty' he mentioned was one I had never heard before.

'The dynasty name is different too, so is it completely different from the Rafdonia Kingdom?'

Anyway, that was the first question.

[Then it is my turn.]

This time, it was his turn to ask a question.

As always, I listened to the question with intense focus.

After all, in conversations like this, the question itself is information.

What would this guy be most curious about?

Finally, his mouth opened, and the answer revealed itself.

[Bjorn Yandel, have you ever personally seen a being from another dimension, or perhaps heard of such a being?]

The moment I heard that question, my mind went blank.

Because there was nothing.

It was only natural, not even a guess came to mind.

[Please answer.

It is a very important matter.]

...

Why on earth was this so important to him?

--- Chapter 819 ---

Chapter 819: Lifeline (3)

Dimensional Collapse.

If one were to name the worst disaster you could experience while playing Dungeon & Stone, it would be the first thing to come to mind without a moment's hesitation.

The greatest terror of this calamity is one thing.

You never know what will come next.

When a Dimensional Collapse occurs, the divisions between floors lose their meaning, and the portals that once connected them lose their function.

It feels as if every tile on the map turns into a random portal.

In the game, it was a single press of a directional key; in reality, with every step you take.

The surrounding space changes, and you are transported to a random location in the Labyrinth.

That's whyГўВҖВҰ

Thump-!

Considering the nature of a Dimensional Collapse, it wasn't entirely nonsensical.

For it to be right before my eyes like this.

The familiar stone gate appearing.

Thump-!

This was it.

ГўВҖВҳ...

The Gate of the Abyss.ГўВҖВҷ

The dream and hope of all the Evil Spirits summoned to this world, the very thing Lee Baekho had burned his entire life trying to open.

ГЈВҖВҢThe Character has discovered the Gate of the Abyss.ГЈВҖВҚ

As it appeared before my eyes at such an unexpected moment, my mind went blank.

It wasn't that I couldn't think at all; rather, it was closer to a feeling of being unable to focus as all sorts of thoughts swirled around.

Thump-!

Even if it's random, how could this happen?

From the First Floor to the Tenth Floor, in this wide, vast Labyrinth, what are the chances of landing exactly in front of the Gate of the Abyss?

Thump-!

Is this some kind of Hidden Piece I don't know about?

Never mind that, what would happen if I took a step forward and opened the gate right now?

Thump-!

What about the Witch of the Earth who said not to open it?

What about the Royal Family, who was clearly hiding some secret?

But that's not what I wantГўВҖВҰ

Thump-

'It'sГўВҖВҰ meaningless anyway.'

All the thoughts I was having were pointless.

The area before the Gate of the Abyss is filled with all sorts of traps.

The moment I cross the tile's boundary, I have to be prepared for annihilation.

It's right to thank the heavens just for landing on a safe tile out of thousands.

Yes, it should be like thatГўВҖВҰ

Chapter 820: Lifeline (4)

[Just a moment, let's watch a little longer.

The voice of Raven, asking once more, was tinged not just with unease, but with a hint of fear.

Fearing the unknown is human nature.

"Could it be part of Gabrielius's arrangement?"

Well, there wasn't much of a conversation to begin with.

[...]

[...]

--- Chapter 823 ---

Chapter 823: TheГӮВ Lifeline (7)

The conversation that followed between two intelligent beings was peaceful and efficient.

I asked, and he answered.

No tricks, no games.

"Then let me start with this. I once saw a diary you wrote. At the end of that diary, it said, 'Today, we die.' What happened after that?"

A dark tale, told in a voice as calm as still water.

"We all decided to die together and took the drug made by Tarvian. But for some reason, I didnГўВҖВҷt die. It seemed the resistance I built up from taking various modified drugs during my soldier days saved me."

Their plan to die together on the same day had failed.

Because Ormi Noark survived.

"There was no drug left... So I left the house where my comrades slept and just walked. The demons we were looking for didnГўВҖВҷt appear, but that didnГўВҖВҷt matter. The flames burned my skin, and the heat destroyed my lungs. I thought IГўВҖВҷd soon be able to join them."

But if he had really died then,

this story wouldnГўВҖВҷt have come this far.

"Even so, I survived."

"How was that possible?"

--- Chapter 828 ---

Chapter 828: Every Man for Himself (5)

At first, I didn't think it was that serious.

After all, areas shrouded in deep darkness were a dime a dozen in the Labyrinth.

Due to the lack of any distinguishing features, I couldn't yet pinpoint which map I was on, but I figured I would find out after a little exploring.

But what in the world was going on?

ГўВҖВҳI have no idea.ГўВҖВҷ

Despite having moved quite a distance from the starting point, I still had no clue.

I hadn't seen any monsters that could serve as a clue, and the terrain hadn't changed from the initial spot.

Of course, even if a monster did appear, IГўВҖВҷd have no way of knowing if it was one caught in the Collapse or one that originally lived here.

Trudge, trudge.

As I continued to move slowly and explore, I honestly started to feel a bit confused.

ГўВҖВҳTerrain that is just an endless dirt floorГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҷ

ГўВҖВҳBecause IГўВҖВҷve explored most of the areas below the Seventh Floor myself.ГўВҖВҷ

As I walked with a frown, the Special Mage chimed in.

ГўВҖВңUmГўВҖВҰ wherever this is, it doesnГўВҖВҷt seem particularly dangerous, so wouldnГўВҖВҷt it be better to rest in one spot?ГўВҖВқ

At a glance, his words were reasonable.

We grow by hunting monsters and making discoveries.

I didn't have to look far.

ГўВҖВҳWellГўВҖВҰ setting all that aside, my curiosity is also a big factor.ГўВҖВҷ

Chapter 831: Eternal Hall (3)

[Dragon Fear]

A signature skill inherent to any creature whose species is ГўВҖВҳDragon,ГўВҖВҷ a symbol of their kind.

In truth, this skill is impossible to obtain through normal means.

That is because dragons do not yield an Essence.

When successfully defeated, a dragon collapses, leaving its body behind and offering the most high-grade byproducts as a reward, no different from gold and treasures.

One might think that casting Distortion Magic could produce an Essence, but for some reason, it never works on them.

ГўВҖВңHeheheГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰ.ГўВҖВқ

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has cast [Dragon Fear].ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢA penalty is applied differentially according to the gap in status with the target.ГЈВҖВҚ

The effect itself is also a considerably inferior version.

Based on my current level of 8, Rank 3 monsters, judged to be at level 7, would be paralyzed for five seconds.

No matter how large the level difference, I could not obtain an overpowered effect like ГўВҖВҳinstant deathГўВҖВҷ.

ButГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰ

ГЈВҖВҢThe energy of the earth weakens noticeably.ГЈВҖВҚ

Chapter 832: Eternal Hall (4)

Eternal Hall.

The structure of this place is quite simple.

Flame, Earth, Water, and Wind.

There are a total of four types of fields, and in each field, primeval spirits of the corresponding attribute guard the paths like old masters.

AndГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВҳSomewhere on the map, thereГўВҖВҷs a Spirit Gate.ГўВҖВҷ

We ended up here by chance due to a dimensional collapse, but in truth, nothing would have changed even if that wasn't the case.

That's because you always fall into a random location when you enter through the Spirit Gate anyway.

The strategy for this map is to explore it and use the Spirit Gate to get back out.

ГўВҖВҳNo, actually, thereГўВҖВҷs not much of a strategy to speak of.ГўВҖВҷ

In a normal situation, you could just not leave and hold out until the Labyrinth closes.

Well, since the Spirit Gate itself is a reward, that usually doesnГўВҖВҷt happen.

"Follow me closely!"

After we began our search in earnest, we gradually picked up the pace.

At first, we walked at a brisk pace, and then, once we got a bit more used to it, we started running as if in a march.

Thud!

Now, we were running at a full sprint.

I summoned a great number of Light Orbs to widen our field of vision as much as possible.

"This path is blockedГўВҖВҰ!"

"We will detour to the right."

If an abnormality was spotted in the distance, we immediately changed direction and ran again.

AndГўВҖВҰ

"It's blocked againГўВҖВҰ!"

We repeated this over and over.

It was a method we could use because we were in the Eternal Hall.

Normally, widening the field of vision so drastically would attract the attention of monsters.

Usually, it's common practice to secure just enough visibility to avoid discomfort.

The situation earlier, when we were caught by the Earthen Body, happened because we had minimized our visibility, not knowing where we were.

"Hwarururuk! ГўВҖВҰLook over there! ItГўВҖВҷs fireГўВҖВҰ!"

As we continued our search for over three hours, a change finally occurred in our surroundings.

There was no need to secure our vision with Light Orb magic; a blazing red land was visible even from far away.

ГўВҖВҳIt finally appeared.ГўВҖВҷ

Hiding a satisfied smile, I led the group toward the fire zone.

We then moved along the boundary between the fire and earth fields.

A safe road of sorts.

Primeval spirits rarely appeared along this boundary, so I always moved along it whenever I came here.

AndГўВҖВҰ

"Guide, do you feel anything?"

"Still nothingГўВҖВҰ My Luck Detection hasn't picked up anything since that stone from before either."

"I believe I told you not to do a half-hearted job and give a half-hearted answer."

"Th-that's not it, what am I supposed to do when it's the truth!"

"HmmГўВҖВҰ."

"But is there even a point to this? If there was a portal, I would have felt it long ago."

"When I tell you to do something, you do it. Is that so difficult?"

I periodically used the guide, who possessed the talent for guidance, to scan the area like a radar.

The Spirit Gate I was looking for was also considered a portal, so it could be detected by the talent for guidance.

Though, unlike the normal floors of the Labyrinth, there was the prerequisite of being within a certain radius.

ГўВҖВҳShould I go inside after all?ГўВҖВҷ

Not much time was left before the eight-hour stabilization period, which guaranteed safety, would be over.

Therefore, it was time to make a move.

"Follow me. We need to search inside there as well."

When I pointed to the fire zone, the survivors looked disgusted.

"Inside there, you sayГўВҖВҰ?"

"We can feel the heat even from this far awayГўВҖВҰ."

"Don't worry. It won't be that hot."

After I briefly explained the Orb of Fire, the group's opposition quickly subsided.

However, there was one thing they couldn't accept.

"But will there really be any survivors in thereГўВҖВҰ?"

ГўВҖВҳHmm, thatГўВҖВҷs trueГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВҷ

He had a point.

Thwack!

The guide, who had been hit on the back of the head, frowned and looked up at me.

"Hey, why did you suddenly hit me! Am I not right? There's no way anyone could be in a place like thatГўВҖВҰ."

"You, you seemed to be doing a half-assed job as a radar earlier, and now you're talking back?"

"There are no absolutes in the Labyrinth."

"No, even soГўВҖВҰ."

"What if there are survivors in there? What will you do then? Can you take responsibilityГўВҖВҰ?"

"Wh-what do you mean by responsibility? I just said one thing."

"Didn't you just say there would absolutely be no survivors?"

The guide, having lost the logical battle, bit his lip in frustration.

But it seemed his anger hadn't subsided.

"I'm grateful that you saved us, but I can't stand this kind of treatment! Besides, didn't you just pocket what I found with my Luck Detection earlier?"

Just as the guide, who had been showing signs of it, dared to try and rebuke me.

"Anton!!"

Whoa, that startled me.

"That's enoughГўВҖВҰ."

"ButГўВҖВҰ."

"I said, that's enough!!"

The thunderous shout came from the hammer-wielding warrior, who was from the same clan.

"I will apologize on his behalf, Viscount Yandel."

"I accept."

"Thank youГўВҖВҰ?"

"No need to thank me, aren't we both warriors?"

Of course, it is a man's duty to accept an apology from a warrior.

Besides, this guy was from the same Black Bear Tribe as Bear Uncle, so I felt a strange sense of affinity for him.

"HahahaГўВҖВҰ."

Leaving the awkwardly laughing hammer-wielding warrior behind, I headed towards the fire zone, and perhaps because of the incident with the guide, the entire group followed without a word of complaint.

Hwarururuk~!

"ГўВҖВҰ?"

Suddenly, the priestess muttered something and then shouted.

"The Viscount was right!"

"WhatГўВҖВҰ?"

I couldn't understand it at all.

"S-survivors!!"

Why would there be survivors here?

***

I had told the guide that there might be survivors, but to be honest, I hadn't been serious.

I just needed an excuse to enter the sea of fire.

"It-it's true! I can feel the presence of magic from that direction!"

FwoooooooshГўВ”ВҖ!

A group of eight explorers, having raised a protective barrier with divine power, were walking through the flames, supporting the injured.

"To think there were actually survivorsГўВҖВҰ."

The guide, who had insisted that there couldn't be any survivors, stared at them with a dazed expression.

If I were to look in a mirror, I would probably have a similar expression.

The truly surprising thing didn't end with just 'there are survivors'.

As I checked the faces of the survivors, I saw familiar faces here and there.

"The Armin ExpeditionГўВҖВҰ?"

The very same clan I had journeyed with on the underground first floor.

It wasn't that I saw wrong from a distance.

I had met those guys several times in the city and had been interacting with them periodically.

ГўВҖВҳWhy are they hereГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВҷ

To meet such welcome faces here!

"Bahellaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!!"

I couldn't help but shout the name of the ancestral spirit, and with that, the group over there also noticed our presence.

"Th-this powerful and vigorous roarГўВҖВҰ?"

"Over there! There are people over there!"

Since I was even using Giant Form, it wasn't difficult for them to recognize me from afar.

"Baron YandelГўВҖВҰ No, Viscount YandelГўВҖВҰ!!"

"Oh my godГўВҖВҰ!!"

The group over there seemed quite exhausted, so I quickly ran over, and my temporary companions who followed behind took care of the Armin Expedition, who were on the verge of dying.

"Stay still! We'll help you!"

The priestess and the male priest took the injured who were being supported and began to treat them.

"You've been using protective magic to the brink of running out of mana. Stop and rest now. We'll take it from here."

The mage from the Armin Expedition, who was on the verge of collapse, finally let go of his staff and fell.

That side will take care of itself without me telling themГўВҖВҰ

Thump.

I approached Armin, the leader of the expedition.

"V-Viscount."

He looked up at me, on the verge of collapsing.

"It's not aГўВҖВҰ a dream, is itГўВҖВҰ?"

It was clear that a lot had happened, and I could feel how incredibly difficult it had been for him.

Hah, to ask me to look after the injured first in this state.

Then againГўВҖВҰ this guy was the leader.

"It's not a dream, so don't worry."

"Th-thank goodnessГўВҖВҰ."

Armin coughed and sputtered, his voice not coming out due to his parched throat.

Long words were unnecessary.

"You've worked hard."

I, who understood the weight on his shoulders better than anyone, just quietly patted his shoulder.

And after about five minutes had passed.

Armin seemed to have regained some of his energy, and he finally asked me a question.

"ГўВҖВҰWhat on earth happened to you? I never imagined I would see you here."

"I'm in a similar situation as you."

"YourГўВҖВҰ your clan membersГўВҖВҰ?"

"Don't worry too much. We were just separated due to some circumstances. Those guys will overcome it just fine."

"I see."

Armin nodded, and I asked him in return.

"And you?"

"ГўВҖВҰWe are all that's left."

"I see."

No wonder I didn't see a few familiar faces.

A sigh escaped me at the bitter thought, but showing it to this guy wouldn't be right either.

I changed the subject.

"You guys were dropped here too?"

"YesГўВҖВҰ The flames engulfed us as soon as the stabilization period began. Everyone was exhausted, but we mustered our strength and kept walking."

"ГўВҖВҰFor six hours? It's a miracle you all survived until now."

Armin suddenly fell silent at my words.

I belatedly realized that I had misspoken.

ГўВҖВҰDammit.

So someone had died on the way here.

"We should have just held our ground in one spot. If we had, we would have lasted for at least eight hours."

"But at the time, we didn't know how much longer the stabilization period would last, so we judged that escaping this sea of flames was the priority."

"Of courseГўВҖВҰ it was a misjudgment. If we hadn't met you, Viscount, we would have all died hereГўВҖВҰ."

His long and pitiful words of self-blame.

I shook my head firmly.

"No, that's not true."

"ГўВҖВҰ?"

"In the first place, if you had gone just a little further, you would have been able to get out of the flames."

"I-is that soГўВҖВҰ?"

"And if you hadn't come this far, I wouldn't have been able to meet you either. Your judgment was absolutely not wrong. Do you understand?"

Did my words comfort him?

Or did they instead poke at his wounds?

I couldn't know the emotions hidden deep within him, but Armin lowered his head as if holding something back.

And after about ten minutes had passed.

By the time Armin and the other injured had been taken care of.

I cautiously brought it up.

"Alright, we'll start moving again."

"YesГўВҖВҰ? Again from here? There are still people who can't even stand properlyГўВҖВҰ."

"If someone can't stand, someone else can carry them."

"ButГўВҖВҰ."

"You never know. There might be more survivorsГўВҖВҰ."

I had a conscience, so this time I couldn't say it with confidence.

It was a miracle we met them, so there was no way there would be more.

I swear, there will be no more survivors.

But surprisingly, the reaction wasn't bad.

"IndeedГўВҖВҰ The Viscount is the same as everГўВҖВҰ!"

"Though it may be a small contribution, we will join you in your cause."

The Armin Clan, who had a deep friendship with me, was naturally favorable to whatever I said.

"Yes, maybe there are more!"

"The more people we have, the better our chances of survival!"

The existing temporary companions were also positive, perhaps because they had the example of the Armin Clan.

And was he influenced by that atmosphere?

"I'm sorry about before. I won't be careless and will try to spread my senses with more careГўВҖВҰ."

Even the guide who had shown resistance towards me obediently backed down and faithfully performed his role as a radar while we traversed the fire zone.

And did his efforts reach the heavens?

"There! I feel the energy of a portal from that direction!"

Finally, the target was captured on the radar.

--- Chapter 833 ---

Chapter 833: Eternal Hall (5)

Clink, clink.

I moved through the flames, rolling a moderately sized stone in my hand.

It was the stone the Guide had found for me earlier using his [Luck Detection] skillГўВҖВҰ Naturally, it wasn't an ordinary object.

Well, it wasn't anything too amazing either.

'It's a bit awkward to sell...

Yes, I'll just use it myself.

They say you shouldn't even give elixirs to your own family.'

Having made my decision, I used the strength of my index finger and thumb to crush the stone.

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has destroyed the ГўВҖВҳEarth SoulstoneГўВҖВҷ.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢEarth Resistance is permanently increased by +3.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢSoul Power is permanently increased by +10.ГЈВҖВҚ

It wasn't an effect I could feel dramatically, but it was the kind of effect that would be a lifelong asset.

But perhaps because this was all I found despite using the [Luck Detection] skill, I honestly wasn't very satisfied.

The only saving grace was that even if other side gains were few, I had found the one most important thing.

...

Well, I'm not sure how this will turn out either.'

ГўВҖВңTh-That must be it!ГўВҖВқ

When the Guide, who had been running at the very front and leading us, stopped and pointed ahead, those following behind began to murmur.

It was only natural.

That which no Explorer could fail to recognize had appeared right before our eyes.

ГўВҖВңA portal...?ГўВҖВқ

A portal shimmered within the blazing flames.

Of course, it was different from the portals one normally saw.

A portal of dazzling colors, a mix of red, blue, green, and gray.

Whoooosh~!

It's the 'Spirit Gate'.

The only exit to escape the Eternal Hall, andГўВҖВҰ

'also a passageway.'

As I passed the Guide and approached the portal, the man behind me warned urgently.

ГўВҖВңBe careful! We don't know where that portal might leadГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

For me, who knew the details about that portal, it was a very amusing warning.

ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҰAh! Do you happen to know something?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңRoughly.ГўВҖВқ

The portal shimmering before me now.

The 'Spirit Gate' is a door to anywhere.

Below the ninth floor.

A place you have visited at least once in person.

As long as these two conditions are met, this portal will transport a player to their desired location.

Rifts and Hidden Fields are, of course, possible as wellГўВҖВҰ

'It can even take you to a floor that has been sealed off.'

Of course, in that case, the Labyrinth closes based on the ninth floor's closing date, and there are even a few Hidden Pieces that can only be found by using thisГўВҖВҰ

But that's not what's important right now.

In the first place, it's meaningless to consider the closing date during a Dimensional Collapse.

ГўВҖВҳWhat would happen if I took it nowГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВҷ

All the information listed above applied in 'normal situations'.

In nearly ten years of playing [Dungeon & Stone], I had never once ridden it during a Dimensional Collapse.

'But to come all this way and not take it feels a bit wrong, doesn't it?ГўВҖВҷ

I soon made my decision.

ГўВҖВңAfter I enter the portal, when its color changes, all of you follow me in.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң... Yes?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңDon't worry. It won't be that dangerous. That portal allows you to go anywhere within the Labyrinth.ГўВҖВқ

After I briefly explained about the Spirit Gate, the wary looks that regarded the unidentified portal greatly diminished.

But perhaps another question had arisen.

ГўВҖВңThen is there a need to take the portal? The collapse will just start again once the stabilization period is over anyway.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThe stabilization period is a minimum of eight hours, not necessarily exactly eight hours.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSo you've judged that you can gain enough profit within that time.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңRight. And in the first place, according to the ancient text I read, you can receive a blessing just by taking that portal.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңA blessingГўВҖВҰ ah, you mean the baptism!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYeah, that.ГўВҖВқ

For reference, the Evil Spirits who came over from [Dungeon & Stone]... no, the players, simply called the baptism an 'achievement'ГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВңThen we must take it!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңDon't worry! We'll follow right behind you!ГўВҖВқ

The survivors' gazes changed 180 degrees.

It seemed the talk of an achievement was the deciding factor.

After all, it was safe to say that no Explorer disliked bonus stats.

Tsk, they're all crazy for elixirs.

ГўВҖВңAnyway, I'll take it that there are no objections thenГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

I slowly extended my foot into the portal.

ГўВҖВңWell then, see you in a bit.ГўВҖВқ

Now, where would be the best place to go?

***

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has entered the ГўВҖВҳSide PathГўВҖВҷ.ГЈВҖВҚ

***

Side Path.

To be honest, this map didn't have any particularly impressive features.

That's because in the game, a map simply unfolded when you passed through the Spirit Gate.

All you had to do was choose your desired location.

ButГўВҖВҰ

'So this is what it's like in realityГўВҖВҰ'

This wasn't the first time I'd experienced the gap between 2D dot-game graphics and reality.

The 'Room of Balance' I went to using a restrained wish in Larkaz's Maze was like that, as was my visit to the Temple to erase an Essence.

Many differences exist between the game and reality.

Just like this.

ГўВҖВҳSo thatГўВҖВҷs the mapГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВҷ

A pitch-black space reminiscent of outer space.

When I casually lifted my head, I could see a huge plate divided into floors far in the distance.

'The very top one must be the ninth floor...'

I get that, but how am I supposed to get there?

The moment I had that thought and lowered my head to check my body.

ГўВҖВҳWhatГўВҖВҷs this now?ГўВҖВҷ

A strange flag was grasped in my hand.

I think I have a rough idea of what this is for.

'Is it the type of thing where if I plant this, the destination gets locked there?ГўВҖВҷ

Anyway, that wasn't important.

Whoooosh~!

My whole body glowed blue as if it were a will-o'-wisp.

My hard-earned equipment was gone, and I was completely naked.

In that state, I was floating gently in the air like a spirit.

In fact, it was so natural that I didn't even feel the sensation of my body floating.

It was as if I had a new organ.

Even though I wasn't using my feet, I instinctively knew how to move.

'Whoa, that surprised me.'

The moment I thought, my body shot forward.

The speed was abnormally fast, but it didn't feel particularly fast.

It was as if I myself had become some existence that transcended the laws of physics.

ГўВҖВҳSo this is how you do it.ГўВҖВҷ

Having gotten a rough feel for it, I headed for the highest of the floors visible in the distance.

The entire landscape of the ninth floor's Grave of Stars, seen from afar as if looking down at the ground from thousands of meters in the air.

'...

That's a bit of an exaggeration.'

Only the outline of the map was visible; the entire ninth floor was covered in a dark fog.

That was only natural.

I had never been to the 'Grave of Stars' in the first place.

'I guess going outside the fortress walls doesn't countГўВҖВҰ'

Well, I had sort of expected this, so I wasn't too disappointed.

Still, a few areas revealed themselves as holes in the fog.

It seemed they were the areas I had passed through during the Dimensional CollapseГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВҳSo the special fields are separate, on the side.ГўВҖВҷ

The independent spaces of the ninth floor were floating gently like satellites, and one of them had a very large area not covered by fog.

It was the 'Eternal Hall' where I had just been.

Well, I did wander around a lot there since I arrived during the stabilization periodГўВҖВҰ

Whoooosh~!

My interest piqued, I quickly moved to that area.

I had felt it when looking down from above, but unlike reality, it was extremely gloomy.

It was like watching a black-and-white film.

The flames of the fire zone that had once blazed crimson were now distinguished by shades of gray, and time itself seemed to have stopped.

ГўВҖВҳAs expected, I can't enter the fog-covered areas.ГўВҖВҷ

I tried once, but as expected, it was impossible to cross the fog.

It felt like an invisible barrier was blocking me.

WhooooooshГўВҖВ”!

So I moved along the path that wasn't covered by fog.

It was a route I had explored for over six hours until my tongue was hanging out, but the time it took to pass through it all and reach the location of the 'Spirit Gate' was but a fleeting moment.

For reference, my companions were gathered in front of the portal, frozen like statuesГўВҖВҰ

What happens if I set this as my destination?

Can I ride the Spirit Gate an infinite number of times?

As I was indulging in such useless imaginings, I belatedly realized.

'No, wait a minute...

This is insane!'

This wasn't just simply showing me a map.

If it were, my companions shouldn't have been in front of that portal.

ГўВҖВҳThenГўВҖВҰ is this all liveГўВҖВҰ??ГўВҖВҷ

Goosebumps crawled all over me.

My spiritualized body couldn't actually get goosebumps, but that's how it felt.

And why wouldn't it?

ГўВҖВҳWith this, I can find them, can't I?ГўВҖВҷ

With this, it's possible.

To find my companions who were separated from me during the Dimensional Collapse.

ГўВҖВҳГўВҖВҰTo think the Spirit Gate had a feature like this.ГўВҖВҷ

Thinking about it again, it was unbelievable.

My top priority was my companions, so that's all I was thinking about, but the potential danger of this live function couldn't be described even by the word 'immense'.

And why wouldn't it be?

It wasn't because of trivial things like being able to secretly peek under a Priestess's skirt, or secretly witness raiders in the act and catch them, or finding out which clan was sucking up honey whereГўВҖВҰ

'If we were in the middle of a war, just coming here would let you know who was where at a glanceГўВҖВҰ'

If one had ill intentions, it would even be possible to find and assassinate someone.

Take the Dragon Slayer, for example.

If that bastard had the chance to take this, he would have found me long ago and popped up right in front of me.

Whoooosh~!

Having discovered the immense potential of the Spirit Gate, I quickly headed for the eighth floor.

I planned to scan everything from the top down.

'This place is small.'

The eighth floor's Land of Dawn was extremely small compared to the vast ninth floor.

No, it was beyond small; the satellites existing at the same altitude as the eighth floorГўВҖВҰ to be precise, the Hidden Fields, were far larger.

'The eighth floor has a lot of Rifts, so there are many of them.'

The Rifts on the eighth floor were all shrouded in fog, so there was nothing to see.

The only thing that was somewhat impressive was the group of dozens of explorers gathered in the grassy field that could be considered the entrance to the Land of Dawn.

They were split into three groups, keeping some distance from each other.

It seemed their stabilization periods had coincidentally overlapped, and they were spending time together.

'I don't see any familiar facesГўВҖВҰ Pass.'

To be honest, I had been an Explorer for quite some time, so I did occasionally see people I was acquainted with.

But I couldn't just plant the flag for their sakes.

If it were someone I was as close to as the Bear Uncle, I would have seriously considered it, but none of them were like that.

'So this is how it is here.'

The seventh floor was divided into two chunks.

The Dark Continent and Ice Rock.

The two floors were separated like islands with a slight gap, and since I had wandered all over the place here, the opened area was much larger than on the upper floors.

Well, still, more than a third of it was covered in fog.

Labyrinth exploration inherently requires moving along efficient routes, so it couldn't be helped.

ГўВҖВҳThe continent is large, so there are many people tooГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҷ

There were quite a few Explorers experiencing a stabilization period on the seventh floor.

As I flew, looking down from above, I would spot a group every few minutes.

'...

They probably don't even know there are other survivors so close by.'

It felt as if I had become an omnipotent god.

Of course, it was just a feeling.

It was impossible to give a heads-up to the group of survivors camping nearby, or to let them know that a monster was targeting them from the darkness.

I hadn't actually become a god.

'Next.'

After a quick scan of the seventh floor, I headed down to the sixth floor.

Because one travels by ship in the Great Sea, most of the area was covered in fog, butГўВҖВҰ

'...

This place is surprisingly fine.'

There were no tidal wave-like high waves.

No, if anything, the sea looked much calmer than usual.

So I headed to the place where the Lord of Tears is summoned, but for some reason, there was nothing there.

Even though I was sure I had come to the right place.

'Did that bastard get caught up in the collapse too?'

For now, that was the most likely hypothesis.

The seventh floor I had just looked at was already teeming with monsters from other floors.

'Then... that means that bastard is somewhere on another floorГўВҖВҰ'

For some reason, I felt a shiver run down my spine again.

The Dimensional Collapse alone was a headache, but now I had to worry about that monster too.

'...

Well, I haven't run into it yet.'

Reminding myself of my purpose, I quickly continued my search of the sixth floor.

My separated companions might be spending their stabilization period on one of these islands, so I searched meticulously.

'Oh.......ГўВҖВқ

As I was searching each island at the speed of light, I found a familiar face for the first time.

'...

This is absurd.'

It was Lee Baekho's party.

--- Chapter 834 ---

Chapter 834: Grudge (1)

Rudria Island.

It wasn't quite 'Grand Rock,' the name for the easternmost region of the Great Sea, but it was a tropical island located somewhere on the outskirts.

Beyond the sandy beach, under a palm tree, a group of nine explorers was gathered.

They looked far too peaceful.

'Rek Aures.'

The hulking human man, the very image of a Tank, must have gone for a swim, as he was wringing out his wet shirt.

'Velveb Ruinzenes.'

The old man of twilight was using a fallen tree as a chair, sitting on it and reading a book.

'Jaina Flyer.'

The Priestess in black robes, as if wanting to avoid mingling with them, was leaning against a tree a short distance away with her eyes closed.

'Lee Baekho.'

That blond bastard with the rude-looking features was sitting in front of a campfire.

No, he wasn't just sitting there.

He was smiling about something and blabbering on.

'Lee BaekhoГўВҖВҰ!'

There were five other people in their group whom I had never seen before, but unfortunately, I didn't have the luxury to pay attention to each of them.

BeeeeeeeepГўВҖВ”!

This sudden reunion paralyzed my reason, and a strange ringing filled my ears.

My spiritual hand seemed to tremble.

I calmly closed and opened my eyes.

But just like the day I first opened my eyes in this world, nothing changed.

ГўВҖВҳГўВҖВҰLee Baekho.ГўВҖВҷ

ItГўВҖВҷs Lee Baekho.

The one who killed James Carla and Bersil Gourland.

The one who, on the day I founded the Anabada Clan, crushed my resolve, which I had ignited with the promise to never feel that damn feeling again, with a smiling face.

ГўВҖВҳRight, so this bastard entered the Labyrinth too.ГўВҖВҷ

He had completely disappeared since that day, making me wonder where he had vanished.

Just as I suspected, he was scurrying around in the shadows, looking for something to scavenge.

Like a cockroach.

ThumpГўВҖВ”!

A wave of revulsion washed over me.

If I had my way, I would plant this flag right here and now, then jump through the Portal.

ButГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВҳI can't.ГўВҖВҷ

I desperately suppressed the urge.

It wasn't just because of the realistic reason that fighting that party with my current members gave me a low chance of victory.

'Oh shГўВҖВҰ'

ГўВҖВҳI canГўВҖВҷt.ГўВҖВҷ

This flag is the only item that will allow me to meet my comrades who fell during the Dimensional Collapse again.

But to use it just to land a blow on that bastard?

What if, because of that, my comrades, whom I could have saved, were swept away in the collapse and died?

I would surely come to despise myself.

To be consumed by hatred and miss the opportunity to save them when I had the chance.

ГўВҖВҳHooГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҷ

Letting out a silent sigh, I barely managed to tear my gaze away from Lee Baekho.

If I looked at that face for even a second longer, I felt my resolve would waver.

So I shifted my gaze to the surroundings.

ГўВҖВҳГўВҖВҰFive people.ГўВҖВҷ

Lee Baekho's party had grown by five people while I hadn't seen them.

Just knowing who they were would be a great help in the future.

I carefully examined the faces of the new party members.

First, the little kid with an innocent face, grilling something over the campfire.

'He can't be a minor.

Is it some kind of magic?'

An unidentified person wearing a mask.

'He's about 170cm tallГўВҖВҰ I can't even tell if it's a man or a woman from the figure.'

A swordsman standing before the campfire, listening intently to Lee Baekho's words.

It's a new face, but judging by his frown, it doesn't seem like he's hearing anything pleasant.

Come to think of it, this was only natural.

There was no way that super psycho-sociopath, completely lacking in social skills, would say something pleasing to others' ears.

Anyway, next isГўВҖВҰ?

An elf with skin so dark it was clearly black even in monochrome.

Could it be a Dark ElfГўВҖВҰ so, a Drow?

But I heard they went extinct hundreds of years ago.

'It's not like he just got a tan, rightГўВҖВҰ?'

Doubts arose, but since this wasn't something I could figure out right now, I passed and moved my gaze to the side.

ГўВҖВҳFinally, someone I know.ГўВҖВҷ

A Dwarf with hand-axes hanging from his waist like a bunch of keys.

His nickname wasГўВҖВҰ

'What was it again?'

Anyway, he was a promising talent of the golden generation, but for some reason, he butchered the previous DumokaГўВҖВҰ I mean, the previous Dwarf Tribe Chief, and fled to join Orculis.

A criminal.

His public reputation was much higher than the corpse collectorГўВҖВҷs, but I've never actually seen him fight, so I can't be sureГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВҳHe must be the last one.ГўВҖВҷ

The ninth member of Lee Baekho's party was a Beastkin, lying spread-eagled on the beach, away from the others.

It was a far cry from romantically becoming one with nature and gazing up at the stars.

Because there were liquor bottles rolling around him.

ГўВҖВҳDrinking in the LabyrinthГўВҖВҰ Is he a bit crazy in the head? I guess thatГўВҖВҷs why he hangs out with a bastard like Lee Baekho, right?ГўВҖВҷ

Anyway, the important thing is that I have no idea who this guy is either.

'In the end, I only clearly recognized one personГўВҖВҰ'

At this point, it was so absurd I was flabbergasted.

I hate to admit it, but to be hanging out with Lee Baekho, they must have some level of skill.

But it's this difficult to even recognize them?

'Where the hell does that bastard Lee Baekho find these people? Is there some kind of dark part-time job site?'

Chapter 838: Grudges (5)

ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҰDo as you please.ГўВҖВқ

The more I thought about it, the more my head ached, so I decided not to worry about this issue any longer.

What does any of that matter?

I don't do good deeds expecting favors in return.

To live in this harsh world.

ГўВҖВңUnderstood. IГўВҖВҷll inform the others. You heard him! Rest well!ГўВҖВқ  

With my orders relayed, the explorers finally relaxed and began resting.

The third wave had been lucky.

No Rank 1 monsters appeared, and only two Rank 2s.

We hadnГўВҖВҷt been dropped into strange fields with bizarre effects, and weГўВҖВҷd even gained three survivors.

But it wasnГўВҖВҷt an easy victory.

That single casualtyГўВҖВ”  

It had only been avoided thanks to everyone fulfilling their roles: mage, priest, vanguard, archer.  

ГўВҖВҳThe stabilization period is still far off.

For now, let them restГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҷ  

I then gathered three specific people.  

ГўВҖВңElГўВҖВҰ what was your surname again?ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңMaria.ГўВҖВқ  

The priestess.  

ГўВҖВңElbain.ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңAhaha, to be called by name! That must mean you trust me now!ГўВҖВқ  

The special mage.  

ГўВҖВңAnd youГўВҖВҰ sorry, what was your name again?ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңA-Anton Finders.ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңRight, Anton.ГўВҖВқ  

The high-grade radar with [Luck Detection].

With the three gathered, I told them plainly.  

ГўВҖВңWeГўВҖВҷre going out to scout. YouГўВҖВҷre not too tired, right?ГўВҖВқ  

Honestly, I couldГўВҖВҷve gone alone.

But who knew? There might be dangerous monsters trapped here by the dimensional collapse.  

ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷm tired, but I wonГўВҖВҷt shirk my duty!ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңSince itГўВҖВҷs about finding survivors, IГўВҖВҷll come tooГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңM-My legs are sore thoughГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңThatГўВҖВҷs serious! Let me fix that!ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңW-Wait, what are youГўВҖВ”Aaaagh!! Fine! IГўВҖВҷll come! Just stop!!ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңGood! Muscle pain must be walked off anyway!ГўВҖВқ  

And so, with everyoneГўВҖВҷs ГўВҖВҳconsent,ГўВҖВҷ we set out to scout.

Checkpoints, drawbridges, outer walls, city streetsГўВҖВҰ  

The path I had once carved through with blood and sweat, I now passed easily.

I didnГўВҖВҷt even need to lift a hand.

The Bloodstained Fortress only spawned undead.  

ГўВҖВңBeheeeellaaaaaa!!ГўВҖВқ  

Just one [Wild Surge] wiped them all out in a single blow.

Boom! Boom! Boom!  

At the drawbridge, thousands of Dead MenГўВҖВҷs heads exploded.

It was a gruesome spectacle.  

ГўВҖВңSo weak, truly pitiful.ГўВҖВқ  

This was what I called Heavenly Demon Barbarian Mode.  

ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҰExcuse me?ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңNothing. Just talking to myself. Or were you eavesdropping?ГўВҖВқ  

ГўВҖВңS-SorryГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ  

Anyway, after sweeping through the outer city, Anton suddenly shouted.  

ГўВҖВңWait! [Luck Detection] is picking something up!ГўВҖВқ  

In the center of a dried fountain stood a statue.

As expected, his skill was useful.

ГЈВҖВҢCharacter has obtained ГўВҖВҳTear of the Goddess.ГўВҖВҷГЈВҖВҚ  

A one-time-use Sacred Artifact.

ГўВҖВҳPerhapsГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҷ
