--- Chapter 842 ---

Chapter 842: Regression (4)

Tear of the Goddess.

The very item that played a decisive role in defeating the Rank 5 monster, the vampire duke Cambormir, back in my bare-bones Barbarian days.

In truth, I only use this item because there's no way to take it out with me; it's not something you'd normally waste on a mere Rank 5 creature.

"Aren't you going to check what it is? I could feel a divine power from it at just a glance..."

To the Priestess's curious question, I gave an awkward smile and vaguely said I'd look into it later.

Of course, I know exactly what this thing is.

When used, it inflicts area-wide damage to the undead, and if drunk, it can even heal injuries as severe as severed limbs.

That's why, in the early days, if the main character's arm got cut off and created a handicap, I often used the method of coming here to reattach it.

But...

'When you think about it, that's nothing.'

Damage? To be honest, it's not that amazing.

Even a mere Rank 5 monster like the vampire, who appeared as a Guardian, couldn't be killed in one shot.

Well, its healing power isn't bad.

As for healing, a Top-grade Potion can easily replace it.

The true value of this item lies elsewhere.

ГЈВҖВҢThe Tear of the Goddess has been destroyed.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢ[Grace] descends upon the location of destruction.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢBy [Grace], all of the vampire duke Cambormir's resistances are temporarily sealed, and he suffers great damage.ГЈВҖВҚ

The sealing of all resistances.

This single line option is this item's true value.

Even if it's temporary, it means you can completely wreck a monster's defenses.

ГўВҖВҳEven with Distortion Magic, I don't think I can take this out to the city...ГўВҖВҷ

Just having it on me is reassuring.

It will be a great source of strength in overcoming the calamity known as the Dimensional Collapse.

It will serve as a trump card when facing a powerful foe that is difficult to overcome.

Well, it's best if I never have to use it.

But things in this world don't always go as you wish.

'Alright, I've secured the most important thing...'

I considered withdrawing at this point and returning to the main group, but I just continued the search.

Returning suddenly from here would be a bit strange, and besides...

'I'm curious.'

Who could possibly be in the Boss Room?

My curiosity as a gamer is stirred.

If Cambormir is in there, I'd like to tie him up and have a little chat.

'I might even encounter the 'original', like Ormi Noark whom I met at the hut.'

With that thought in mind, I continued the search.

I passed through the city streets, the inner wall's Underground Prison, and finally reached the last chapter: the Demon Worship Hall.

Contrary to my worries, I didn't see a single monster from the outside that seemed to have been swept in by the Dimensional Collapse.

'If I knew it would be like this, maybe I should have just come alone.'

I only brought the Priest and the Mage to have a minimal response ready in case of an unexpected situation.

As for the radar... well, he was just sort of an extra.

"This should be the last place. According to the book I read, a Death Knight should appear as a Guardian beyond this point..."

"A Death Knight...?"

Well, this time, it seems like that might really be the case.

The doorknob at the entrance to the Boss Room was an ordinary color.

When Cambormir appears, even the color of the doorknob is different.

Therefore.

ScreeeechГўВҖВ”

I boldly opened the old door and entered the Boss RoomГўВҖВ”.

"Huh?"

Having entered the Boss Room without much thought, I froze in an instant.

[12......?]

What the hell is this thing?

In the center of the Boss Room, there wasn't a Death Knight, but a winged pig sitting there blankly.

***

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has entered the range of [Gluttony].ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢ'Distortion' is applied to all living beings within the radius.ГЈВҖВҚ

***

The Boss Room, where a Death Knight or a Vampire should have been.

Inside, there was only a single monster.

"It... doesn't seem to be a Death Knight."

"Hmm, the information I read was from a reliable book. How strange...!"

"What should we do? It's a monster I've never seen before...?"

The trio who followed me calmly assessed the situation, ready to enter combat at any moment.

And as for me...

"Stop!!"

I used all my strength to stop them from reaching for their weapons.

"....?"

They all seemed dumbfounded, but it couldn't be helped.

I recognized this one, too.

"Guiltigos......."

A Rank 1 monster that appears on the Tenth Floor.

It goes without saying, but it was a foe that the four of us couldn't possibly face, even if we died and came back to life.

Chomp, chompГўВҖВ”!

It seemed to be in the middle of a meal.

A Rank 3 monster was impaled on its front paw... or more precisely, on the claw of its front paw.

ГўВҖВҳSo Guiltigos uses its claws like a fork.ГўВҖВҷ

While I made a mental note of this new fact...

I shared the information I knew with my companions.

Quickly, summarizing only the most important facts.

"Guiltigos is a Rank 1 demon-type."

"Gasp!"

"And it will never attack first, unless you attack it or get too close. Understood?"

"U-Understood."

That it doesn't attack Explorers first, but has a trait of tearing apart and eating all other monsters.

That it grows stronger the more monsters it eats, so each individual's strength varies.

I didn't bother sharing such extraneous information.

That wasn't what was important right now.

"If you understand, we will now slowly walk backward and leave this place. Do not provoke it, not even by accident. Got it?"

My companions did not reply.

But sometimes, silence is an answer in itself.

Shuf, shuf.

Judging by the sound of careful footsteps instead of words, it seemed everyone was of one mind.

ThumpГўВҖВ”!

Amidst the suffocating tension, we safely exited the Boss Room.

We had spotted it near the door, so we didn't even have to take many steps, yet for some reason, I felt completely drained.

The situation ended when I carefully closed the door, as if telling it to go on with its meal.

However, I still didn't feel safe.

It was only after passing through the Demon Worship Hall and the Underground Prison and arriving in the city streets that we let out the sounds we'd been holding back.

The Priestess was the first to speak.

"T-Truly, it was a demon of horrifying form... My hands and feet are still trembling."

I was dumbfounded.

Before I said it was Rank 1, she had been watching it with interest.

The second was the radar.

"W-What was that! A Rank 1 monster! Why in the world was something like that in there...!!"

It seems he was so out of his mind that he didn't get a good look at the surroundings.

"There was equipment and bones nearby, presumably belonging to explorers. It must have been swept in here along with them."

Of course, I don't know when that happened.

It could have happened during the collapse, or it might have fallen in there during the first or second stabilization period.

It wasn't particularly surprising.

Not every explorer would have had as peaceful a day as we did during the third wave.

"It was truly a surprising experience... but thinking about it calmly again, a question arises."

The third to speak was the Special Mage.

"A monster eating another monster... How is such a thing possible...?"

"Ah, that."

To explain that, I'd have to start with its passive.

[Gluttony]

This passive skill is an aura-type that applies Distortion to all living things within its radius.

In the case of monsters, their corpses remain even after death.

'In the case of a living person, they spit out a Magic Stone or drop one of the Essences they possessed at a low probability.'

And...

'There's also the effect of growing stronger by eating them.'

As expected of a Rank 1, it's a very peculiar skill in many ways.

I didn't bother telling them, since it felt like a hassle.

"Guiltigos... When I return to the city, I must look into it."

By the way, this guy keeps setting up flags.

Does he have that much to do when he gets back to the city?

"Anyway, nothing happened, so that's good. We'll end the search here and head back."

Having encountered a Rank 1 monster, there were no objections.

Afterward, we quickly returned to the main group for personal maintenance.

In other words, we took a break.

However, I'm not sure how much time passed after that.

I obtained a clue about my scattered companions from an unexpected source.

"You saw Misha...?"

This news came from one of the three survivors who had joined our group during the last wave.

Though I couldn't say whether this was good news or bad news.

"Can you stand by those words?"

"Yes! I can guarantee it! Miss Karlstein was wandering around, just like we were!"

"---- Alone?"

What in the world happened?

***

I spoke with him more afterward, but there was no particularly helpful information.

Before joining our group, while being swept about in the collapse, he had only happened to run into Misha once by chance.

"Hmm, but how can you be so sure it was Misha?"

"When we met, she asked me first! She asked if I had seen Viscount Yandel! Thinking back, her appearance was identical to Miss Karlstein's! And the monsters struck by her dagger would freeze!"

Certainly... with that much circumstantial evidence, it does seem like it was Misha.

"And? What happened then?"

"That's all. I told her I hadn't seen him, and we parted ways right after as if we were being chased. There were monsters everywhere, you see..."

"... I see."

After ending the conversation and sending him away, my thoughts deepened once more.

How did Misha end up separated from the other members?

It seems she was left behind alone and wandered around looking for me throughout the third wave.

Is she really still aliveГўВҖВ”.

Thump!

My heart kept pounding as dark imaginations encroached upon my mind.

Should I start looking for Misha from the next wave?

But that's impossible with this number of people.

Should I change my plans now, abandon them, and move alone?

"Bjorn."

"Ah."

Ainar, who had approached me unnoticed, pulled me from my spiraling thoughts.

"I heard the story. That Misha seems to be alone."

"Yes, just as you heard."

"What are you going to do?"

To that question about the future, I asked one back in return.

"What do you think I should do?"

A type of question I would almost never ask Ainar.

But this time, I wanted to hear her opinion.

A weak, unconscious desire to shift even a little of the weight of the choice onto someone, anyone.

But Ainar, as always, was clear.

"Well, I don't care either way! It's fine if we stay like this, and if you decide to abandon everyone and go search, I'll follow you."

Simply put, whatever.

For some reason, it reminds me of the trauma from meeting Hyunbyeol.

"So in the end, you're telling me to decide?"

"Because I trust your choice."

"Hoo...! Should I be happy about this or upset..."

Still, thanks to her, my head cleared up a bit.

A choice is both a right and a duty.

If I've always enjoyed the right to choose for others, then I must also fulfill the duty when things are tough.

Yes, so...

"There is no change in plans. We will overcome the Dimensional Collapse with these people, just as we are."

I shook off my last lingering attachments and made my decision.

Who knows? Misha will probably survive on her own, right?

Of course, that wasn't the sentiment behind my decision.

I simply judged that this was better for both of us.

If Misha is still wandering around during the next wave, staying in one place might actually increase our chances of meeting.

In fact, didn't the guy who brought me news of Misha meet us and join us while he was wandering?

Okay, then.

Decision made.

"I'm going to rest now, so you should rest too, Ainar."

"Understood!"

After sending Ainar off, I lay down on the floor, spread-eagled.

And then, how much time passed?

Zzzzzzzzt!!!

Hearing that sound, which now gave me PTSD just by listening to it, I shot up.

"Everyone, get up!"

"The collapse is beginning...!!"

"Hurry and gather in the center!!"

Now, it's the fourth wave.

--- Chapter 843 ---

Chapter 843: Wave (1)

The early part of the fourth wave went smoothly.

A series of manageable fields appeared one after another, so there were few instances of unkilled monsters piling up.

We reached what could be called the mid-to-late stage without any major incidents.

However, if I had to point out a problem, it would be this.

ГўВҖВңWe should have seen at least one by now, but this time, not a single hair has been seen.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWell... I suppose that just means the number of people has decreased that much.ГўВҖВқ

Not a single survivor was seen wandering in the collapsing Labyrinth.

We had found three during the last wave.

It seemed the cause was that the number of living explorers itself had dwindled as we entered the fourth wave...

...

ThumpГўВҖВ”!

Anxiety and impatience tried to grow, but there was nothing I could do right now.

'MishaГўВҖВҰ will be fine.'

'And my other companions, too...'

They will surely be fine.

So, I'll trust them and do what I have to do.

ГўВҖВңBehellaahhhhhh!!ГўВҖВқ

It was then, as I let out a war cry, swung my hammer, and raised my shield to block the enemy's assault.

ГўВҖВңHm?ГўВҖВқ

A strange phenomenon occurred amidst the monotonous, repetitive actions.

A blue light suddenly burst from the shield and began to flow into me...

Fwoooosh-

What's this? Did someone give me a buff or something?

That was my first thought, but in the end, that assumption was wrong.

Fwooooosh-

A light of azure hue.

This sensation, as if the shield and I had become one.

This isn't a buff.

Yes, this is...

ГЈВҖВҢThe Barrier of Aegis has reached level 7.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢAll effects of this artifact will increase by a total of 40%.ГЈВҖВҚ

After Soul Link, the Barrier of Aegis, which had been steadily accumulating its own experience points under the surface, had finally reached level 7.

So, to break it down by the numbers...

[Top-grade Indestructibility]

[Immunity to all damage when 'Guarding' with the shield]

[When Threat Level is 500 or higher, all monsters will attack the wielder first.]

These options have fixed values, so they remain the same.

[Increases all of the wielder's defensive stats by 20%]

[When using Aura skills, the radius and effect are doubled.]

The stat increase would now be 28%, and the radius and effect increase would be 2.8 times...

[All magic damage received by the wielder and allies under an Oath within a 10m radius is reduced by 30%, and they are immune to Rank 5 or lower magic.]

The magic immunity part is a fixed value, but the option written in the first part is enhanced.

'A 14m radius and 42% magic damage reduction.'

In other words, if I use Scales of Greed now, the 42% will be multiplicatively applied to the 50%, resulting in a final reduction of 71%.

...

Am I done with magic now?

I could probably get the reduction rate to 100% if I tweaked my setup a bit more, but it's not really meaningful.

Just as there's a limit to the Blade Resistance of the Evolving Shell, there's also a cap on magic damage reduction.

With more time, I could reach a value close to the cap with just Soul Link and ComradeГўВҖВҷs Burden, the eighth-stage effect of the Immortal Engraving...

'Anyway, level 7 already...

It feels strange.'

My own level is 8, after years of struggling and rolling around in the Labyrinth.

Somehow, it feels like the value of all that hardship is fading.

'Well... it was a game where leveling up slowly was hard, but if you had the ability, it was instantaneous...'

Besides, my expeditions haven't exactly been ordinary.

The Underground First Floor, outside the Fortress Wall, and now a Dimensional Collapse.

Having gone through these big events one after another and gained experience, reaching level 7 already is, in a way, a natural result.

'I wonder what will be faster, this thing reaching level 8, or me hitting level 9...'

Honestly, I've been killing monsters so frantically that, at some point, it became impossible to calculate my experience points.

If I were to make a rough guess, I think I'm almost at the end of level 8...

'I suppose I've pretty much killed all the worthwhile monsters on the seventh floor and below.'

Of course, if you count the elite monsters that only appear in certain rifts and hidden fields, there are still quite a few left, but they aren't creatures you can hunt just because you want to.

Suddenly, I feel envious of the era decades ago, called the Golden Generation.

Back then, there were so many high-rank explorers that they formed expeditions in the hundreds to explore even the ninth floor.

Compared to these days, where even exploring the eighth floor has been stalled for years, the difficulty of leveling up must have been much lower.

'Perhaps that's why their levels were all inflated...'

The executives of Orculis are a prime example.

Having been active during the Golden Generation, their levels are high, and the rank of the essences they possess is also high, but their essence combinations themselves are of a very low quality.

Well, I can understand.

It was an era where the strength of the group was more important than the individual, so even with such flimsy essence combinations, there wouldn't have been much of an obstacle to reaching the upper floors.

Zzzzzzt!

As soon as I heard the familiar sound of something tearing, I ended my train of thought and checked my surroundings.

Because after this sound, the transfer begins.

ГўВҖВңGather up...!ГўВҖВқ

Even without any specific command, the explorers who had been spread out on a wide front, actively reducing the number of monsters, expertly gathered in the center.

Because after teleporting, monsters might appear in the center.

They've now developed a know-how for these dimensional collapses.

FlashГўВҖВ”!

Soon, my vision flickered like a TV turning off, and a new space appeared.

Swooooossshh-!

A coastline with crashing waves.

From that alone, I could guess where we were, but the sixth floor is exceptionally vast.

As I hurried to check our surroundings, there were explorers who recognized this place before I did.

ГўВҖВңRudria Island! It's Rudria Island!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, you're right!ГўВҖВқ

Rudria Island.

An island located in the far east of the Great Sea, just outside the boundary of the Grand Rock... and the very island where Lee Baekho's party was discovered resting when I checked through the Side Path.

Surprisingly, the location is also identical.

What kind of fateful coincidence is this?

On a hunch, I quickly turned my head, and behind me, I could see the traces of a campfire that Lee Baekho's party had made while camping.

That empty tree stump over there.

Just a day ago, Lee Baekho was sitting right there.

But only for a moment did I feel the hand of an unknown fate in this strange coincidence.

I shook off the feeling and focused on the present.

ГўВҖВңSomething's coming up from under the sea...!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat in the world are those? They don't look like murlocs.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңA trident...? I-It's a Speardipper! A Rank 3 monster!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat? How can there be so ma...!ГўВҖВқ

A group of Speardippers, advancing inland in neat rows and columns like an army.

At a glance, there seemed to be well over dozens of them, and not even all of them had revealed themselves yet.

'Is it because this is where Lee Baekho was staying...?'

I couldn't even guess why these creatures, which should be in the deep sea, had appeared here, and in such a large group.

But trying to find a reason for something that had already happened was meaningless.

Especially during a dimensional collapse.

ГўВҖВңPrepare for battle! Stay sharp and maintain formation!ГўВҖВқ

However, just as I pushed the questions aside and was about to charge toward the beach.

They say misfortune never comes alone.

Kuuuuuuung!

A vibration originating from the overgrown inland area traveled through the grains of sand.

A somehow hair-raising sensation.

I whirled around and saw a giant monster crushing palm trees as it approached from behind.

It was letting out horrifying screeches.

[Gweeeeeeehhhhk!! Gwehk!!]

A bipedal monster that kept spewing something pitch-black from its gaping maw, as if it were sick to its stomach.

The moment I saw its hideous form, I recognized it.

ГўВҖВңWh-what is that...?ГўВҖВқ

First, it belongs to Rank 2.

However, it doesn't have a specific individual name.

That's because, unusually, it has no common variant and only appears as a Guardian.

[GweeehkГўВҖВ”! Li-light...! Gweeeeeeehk!]

A rift from the Seventh Floor's Dark Continent.

The Guardian of the Mausoleum of Valantis.

ГЈВҖВҢThe Fallen Star, Valantis has cast [Eclipse].ГЈВҖВҚ

If I had to be precise, 'Valantis' itself could be considered its individual name.

Well, that part doesn't matter.

[E-eat...! GweeeeeeehkГўВҖВ”!!]

A legion of Rank 3 monsters swarming from the beach.

A Rank 2 Rift Guardian approaching from the land.

ГўВҖВңAll of you, focus only on the beach. I will take care of that one!ГўВҖВқ

Even as I gave the order so quickly it could be considered instantaneous.

ГўВҖВңBehellaahhhhhh!!!ГўВҖВқ

And even in that moment, as I let out a powerful roar and blocked the creature's path.

ThumpГўВҖВ”!

I couldn't help but think to myself.

'No wonder it's been so easy until now...'

That this time, great sacrifices would be inevitable.

***

As expected, the chaotic battle that began on Rudria Island left us with too many scars.

During a dimensional collapse, the option to 'flee' is erased.

No matter how much damage is incurred, there's no choice but to stand and fight...

Zzzzzzzzzzt!

Even the luck of the fields was not on our side.

On Rudria Island, we somehow managed to hold on without any casualties, but...

ГўВҖВңIt's a transfer...! Huddle together!ГўВҖВқ

The next area we transferred to was the Panthelion Ruins.

A field belonging to the Seventh Floor's Dark Continent, where various types of mechanical-type monsters poured out, ranging from Rank 6 down to Rank 3.

[Gweeeeeeehhhhk!!]

As the one solely responsible for marking Valantis, I couldn't know exactly how bad the situation was, but...

ГўВҖВңHey! Priest! Over here! Over here, please...!ГўВҖВқ

Chapter 844 ГўВҖВ“  Wave (2)

--- Chapter 845 ---

Chapter 845. Wave (3)

The place where we spent our fourth stabilization period was the Second Floor, the Beast Den.

Aside from the slight smell, it was a pretty good floor to spend the stabilization period.

For one, being a lower floor, the difficulty was low.

As long as a Priest used Evil-Banishing Proclamation, the creatures that appeared in the Beast Den could not even get close.

Well, among the monsters swept up by the Collapse and dropped here, there might be some that could break through the barrierГўВҖВҰ but for that, we just had to take turns standing night watch.

"What will you do?"

At Armin's question, I took a moment to think.

There were two options.

Either search the surroundings.

Or lie down on the floor and recover my stamina.

I did not think for long and quickly made a decision.

ГўВҖВң... I should get some sleep.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI see. Understood. Please rest."

Right, let's just rest this time.

Everyone was tired in many ways, and there were not any particular events to look out for on this floor anyway.

'If I had to hope for something, it would be finding another group of survivors during the search...'

Honestly, if we could find them, it would be a great help.

After all, we had suffered quite a lot of losses this time.

If we did not replenish our numbers somehow, the burden would surely increase as the waves continued, so even if the chances were slim, searching was essential.

However...

ГўВҖВңThen I will gather the explorers who have the energy and begin searching the area. Please rest, Viscount.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң... I'll leave it to you."

It should be fine to leave it to others for a day.

Honestly, positions like rogues or scouts had been having it easy all this time, had they not?

They needed something to do too.

ГўВҖВңApologies for interrupting your rest! We are selecting members for a search party. Those whose names are called, please step forward. Creed Allen, Anton......."

Listening to the explorers who could not help but have energy left being called out, I found a spot, lay down, and fell asleep.

And...

Grrrrrrrrrr! Snort! Snort!!

This time, I did not dream at all while I slept.

I woke up feeling as if I had just closed my eyes for a moment, and I could hear murmuring from my surroundings.

What the hell are they chattering about? While everyone's sleeping.

I took out my watch from my clothes and peeked with my eyes slightly open.

[06:35]

So I slept for a little over six hours...

The stabilization period lasts for at least eight hours, so I can still rest easy for another thirty minutes.

So I tried to get a little more sleep...

Agh, really.

With all the whispering around me, there was no way I could sleep.

ГўВҖВңOh, just now...? I think he woke up.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңH-here, you go talk to him.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң... Ahem, let's wait a bit longer, just in case.ГўВҖВқ

Hearing the whispering voices clearly now, I figured I would not be able to fall back asleep, so I just sat up.

"Gasp...!"

"The Viscount is awake!"

ГўВҖВңHey, what has been going on?ГўВҖВқ

Perhaps because I had just woken up, my voice came out a bit gruff.

ГўВҖВңMore than ten!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң... I see.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes. To explain briefly, a total of three clans naturally gathered and joined forces during the Collapse, but suffered heavy losses during this wave. 'He dragged it out on purpose.ГўВҖВҷ

Because the more anxious they became, the clearer the power dynamic between us would be.

ГўВҖВңI know what you're worried about. However, with a terrible accident like this Dimensional Collapse, according to the Labyrinth Disaster Act established by the Royal Family and the Guild, I have a duty to rescue them and overcome this disaster together.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңEnough."

ГўВҖВңYou all, you're not planning to cause any trouble, are you?"

ГўВҖВңNo! That will never happen! If you trust us, we will surely live up to that trust!"

ГўВҖВңSo they say.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen, I'll be counting on you from now on."

ГўВҖВңYes, Viscount! Thank you for accepting us!"

The place where we held a simple funeral before I fell asleep.

My words of comfort would not bring the dead back to life.

Clench.

There was nothing to do but try my best.

ГўВҖВңWhat?ГўВҖВқ

Within the sunken darkness of the labyrinth.

***

ГўВҖВңThey are knights from the Royal Army!ГўВҖВқ

A group of knights with the insignia of the royal family engraved on their chests.

Chapter 846. Wave (4)

Contrary to my expectations, the Knight at a crossroads made a quick and rational decision.

"I yield."

ГўВҖВңHuh?ГўВҖВқ

He said it so coolly that for a moment, I thought I had misheard him.

But I had heard him correctly.

"The nineteen members of the 4th Company have made every effort to rejoin the main force, but we have determined that it is difficult to fulfill this duty due to unavoidable circumstances. From now on, in accordance with Article 41 of the Military Law, we will exercise autonomous judgment and transfer command authority as requested by Viscount Yandel for the survival of the battalion."

"Ah..."

I had even been planning to use my title to crush any resistance.

ГўВҖВҳDid he just need a justification...?ГўВҖВҷ

Now I see I was just shadowboxing by myself.

"You are just as I have heard. It is embarrassing, but I entrust myself and my unit to you. We will never be a burden or fail to pull our own weight."

"Well... to be honest, I'm not worried about you being a burden... but are you really okay with this?"

At my question, the man tilted his head.

"You ask a strange question. Anyone who lives in Rafdonia knows. If something happens in this Labyrinth, there is no one more trustworthy than you."

Uh... what should I say to this?

In this unexpected situation, hearing such direct praise from him felt a bit awkward.

Besides, it felt wrong to treat him carelessly like before.

'What the, turns out he's a pretty decent guy?' I thought...

'This bastard, could he have been aiming for this?'

The Third Royal Knight Division, Fourth Company Commander Saul Andreakora.

He was truly a man with fearsome social skills.

I must never let my guard down.

"Alright, as long as you don't cause any trouble, I promise I will take responsibility for you and look after you without discrimination."

"That is sufficient."

"Armin!"

Having decided to accept the group of Knights, I handed them over to Armin.

Armin, as if accustomed to it, completed their background checks and proceeded with the tutorial.

ГўВҖВҳ...

Somehow, the number of people has grown immensely again.ГўВҖВҷ

From the fourth Wave, 13 people died, leaving 26 of the original members.

To that, add the 14 survivors found by the search party.

On top of that, the final group of Knights numbered 22 in total.

It was a group of nineteen Knights mixed with a total of three rescued survivors, like the Guild Master.

'A total of 62 people...

At this point, it's safe to say we've reached our maximum capacity.

If any more people join, we'll run out of space.'

ГўВҖВҳThis feels a bit bewildering.ГўВҖВҷ

Just a little while ago, I was most worried about having too few people.

But what about now?

I merely sent out a search party, had a good sleep, and woke up to find our numbers had exploded to the point where we couldn't take anyone else.

That's why I felt extremely strange.

'Things are suddenly going this... well...?'

A sense of unease bloomed for some reason.

Of course, since the last Wave was incredibly difficult, it could be that a little luck is on our side this time.

But...

'Still, something feels off.'

I have a habit where my heart races and my breathing quickens the better things go.

So I checked everything over once more.

Just in case I missed anything.

I scanned the roster Armin made to check if a Hans had secretly infiltrated, and then I spoke with the newcomers to hear their stories.

What had happened to them to survive until now.

Whether they had seen anything strange along the way.

It was an action taken to gain even a little peace of mind, but as I went around listening to their stories, I made an unexpected discovery.

"When the Collapse began, you were on Arps Island...?"

"That is correct."

Listening to Company Commander Saul's story, I found myself frowning without realizing it.

The reason was very simple.

The Royal Army had even announced they would occupy three islands where Magic Stone farming was good.

"Arps Island isn't exactly a good place for gathering Magic Stones, is it?"

"I do not know the detailed circumstances myself. In the first place, things started happening not long after we moved."

Saul said they were lucky, nonetheless.

After all, Arps Island was one of the few islands unaffected even after the Lord of Tears was summoned.

'This doesn't feel like a coincidence at all...'

With that thought, I asked Saul for more details about various things.

At first, Saul seemed reluctant to speak in depth about internal military affairs, but when I pressed him with my title of Viscount, he eventually relented and answered faithfully.

And the result.

"Hmm...?"

From entering the Labyrinth to the moment the Collapse occurred.

I was able to grasp the general movements of the Royal Army.

Initially, the Royal Army had moved according to plan.

The nearly 1,700 soldiers of the Royal Army split into three groups, occupied three islands, and began farming Magic Stones.

But...

"An order suddenly came down? To move to Arps Island?"

"... That is correct."

"And you don't know where the military units stationed on the other two islands went?"

"I have not heard anything separately about the other units. However, would they not have remained there?"

No, that's definitely not it.

I remember the resentful cry someone shouted when the Lord of Tears was summoned and they were swept away by the waves to the center.

[Royal Army! Where in the world is the Royal Army!! If a situation like this breaks out, you should come find us!!]

There wasn't a single Royal warship there.

'They were probably on a safe island.

Just like the group this Saul guy belonged to.'

Just in time, by chance, coincidentally.

The circumstances were hard to accept with such words.

In fact, at this point, I might as well confirm it.

I don't know what they were aiming for by pulling something like this, but...

'It wasn't that bastard, Lee Baekho.'

The one who summoned the Lord of Tears was the Royal Army.

***

The Royal Family summoned the Sixth Floor Lord.

Why?

For what reason?

What benefit did they hope to gain by summoning that monster?

Unfortunately, I wasn't given time to ponder the matter deeply.

The stabilization period had already ended.

Zzzzzzt!

The empty air split with a familiar tearing sound.

"Everyone, get into formation!"

And...

FlashГўВҖВ”!

The fifth Wave began with a flash before my eyes.

'It's already the fifth...

Well, as I said, that's if we're lucky.

"Still, we've come a long way."

It's not like the end is in sight, but we've at least reached a stage where we can start imagining it.

Therefore...

"BehelГўВҖВ”laaaaaaaaaaah!!"

The creatures that appeared had a high average Rank, and it was a region with tricky Field Effects, but there was still a positive aspect.

Isn't there a saying that it's better to get the beating over with first?

It's better for it to come early than when the monsters have piled up and we're low on stamina.

Just like this.

ГЈВҖВҢYou have defeated a Frost Wraith.

High-Rank creatures poured out, but they were immediately cleared away, and experience points piled up.

A large group of over 60 people.

Above all, the addition of the group of Knights who could use Aura was decisive.

Shhhk!

Aura that sliced through monsters over Rank 4 like tofu.

As is the case with most Knights, since their damage can be covered by the cheat-like skill called 'Aura,' they fill in their weaknesses with defensive Essences and heavily armed armor.

Simply put, they could function as off-tanks.

'If I had to point out a weakness, it would be that high defense often means they're slow...?'

That too didn't really matter much right now.

ГЈВҖВҢCharacter has cast [Dragon Fear].ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢCharacter has entered a Special Zone.ГЈВҖВҚ

Ah, none of the three dropped an Essence.

Zzzzzzt!

The early part passed without incident, despite starting with a Ninth Floor field in an Eighth-Floor rift.

Zzzzzzzzt!

"Confirmed deaths are three. The current total number of survivors is fifty-nine!"

Their deaths weren't particularly incomprehensible.

In the Labyrinth, a single small mistake can take your life.

The fact that only this many died amidst a chaotic battle with Rank 3 and Rank 4 monsters pouring out was proof of how well we had endured this Wave.

'The field luck wasn't on our side this time...'

Was it a side effect of our increased numbers and improved combat power?

The difficulty of this Wave, in absolute terms, was quite high.

Even the location for the stabilization period was not good.

Prime Land.

A field located in the center of the Grave of Stars on the Ninth Floor.

"What will you do?"

"We'll skip the search. It's not like we're short on people, and the chance of not returning from a search is high."

"And increase the number on night watch. The monsters that appear here will most likely break through [Evil-Banishing Proclamation]."

"I will handle it as you've said."

"Ah, and... call the Priest too."

"Yes."

As we did during every stabilization period, we called the Priest and lit a fire.

There were no sobbing people like last time.

That's because the three who died this time were all people who had joined alone after being separated from their original groups.

There was no one around who had a particular connection to them.

But still, we all gathered and observed a moment of silence before the blazing fire.

"Grant them mercy, warmly embrace and guide their souls, and give rest to the weary."

The time when the fifth Wave ended.

I thought before the blazing flames.

Fwoosh!

I wonder how many more times we will have to light a fire.

Chapter 847: Wave (5)

Ground.

We spent the fifth stabilization period in a primeval land.

Other than constantly fighting off monsters that approached our camp, nothing else of note occurred.

We did not send out a search party, and unlike the group of knights from before, no survivors came looking for us on their own.

Zzzzzzzzt!

Starting in a good field, the Second Floor's Land of the Dead, we fought off the flood of monsters as we were continuously transferred from place to place.

AndГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВңFour dead! The current number of survivors is 55!ГўВҖВқ

The wave ended, leaving four dead.

The overall difficulty seemed lower than the previous wave, yet we ended up with one more casualty.

It was hard to say it was just bad luckГўВҖВҰ

Everyone was probably just exhausted.

For days, we had been going through this Dimensional Collapse, repeating the cycle of sleeping and fighting, sleeping and fighting.

ГўВҖВңWhat will you do this time?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWe will search. We have lost a fair number of people.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңUnderstood.ГўВҖВқ

Chapter 848: Wave (6)

Golden Ghost.

A member of the Seven Peaks, whom I met on Skull Island during this round of exploration, leading to a rather ill-fated connection.

The notable point is that 'Bear Uncle' is 'presumed' to be bound by a slave contract and exploited by that clan.

'But to meet that fellow here...'

Of course, it must be a coincidence.

After all, I don't even know where I'll be in just a few hours.

This isn't an environment where I can just go and find someone because I want to.

Just as it was for me.

'The Misha or Anabada Clan I wanted to see don't show up...?'

Leaving my disappointment behind, I head in the direction where the survivors are stirring.

Honestly, the name Golden Ghost has a negative image, but I should at least meet him first.

I don't know what kind of news I might hear.

"Ah! You've arrived, Viscount."

"What do I need to know?"

"There's nothing other than what I've already told you. He said he wouldn't speak to anyone but you, Viscount."

"Is that so?"

I pushed past Armin and stepped forward.

And I saw the Golden Ghost fellow in front of me.

The man was waiting for me quietly, having led an even larger group than ours as if to show off his numbers.

"We've been seeing each other often lately, haven't we?"

I didn't bother mentioning that he had tailed me on Skull Island.

Without physical evidence, bringing it up would just be a waste of time.

"Indeed. It seems I am fated to have a connection with you, Viscount."

"So, you were looking for me?"

"There are many listening ears. How about we both have our people step back?"

"Fine."

Afterward, I had my group move back as he suggested, and he did the same.

However, as if that wasn't enough, he took out a Magical Tool from his coat and activated it.

It was a design I'd never seen before, but I didn't need to ask what kind of Magical Tool it was.

It was obviously a Magical Tool for noise control.

"Looking at you, you seem to really like secrets."

"Haha, I prefer to call it being cautious."

"So? If there's nothing else, spit it out."

We weren't close enough to make small talk, so I quickly got to the point, and he, knowing my personality, revealed his intentions bluntly.

"I will hand over Abman Urikfrit."

What's his game?

On Skull Island, he'd adamantly refused when I tried to take Bear Uncle, even at the cost of a huge loss.

"We will even tear up the contract he has with our clan, making it a clean break. How about it?"

So I wouldn't even have to pay off Bear Uncle's debt...

The moment I heard the offer, my nerves grew sharper than usual.

A deal that's unilaterally good for one side is nothing but a scam.

"What are the conditions?"

"Just give me twenty people of Third-Rank or higher. It's fine if a few Fourth-Rank Explorers are mixed in."

For a moment, my mind went blank, and it took me a while to grasp the point of his words.

"?"

What kind of madman is this?

"I refuse. In the first place, it's not like I can send them just because I tell them to go."

When I flatly refused without much thought, he merely smacked his lips.

"Hmm, that is true."

As if he had expected my refusal, the man nonchalantly brought up a new offer.

"Then how about this? We will join Viscount Yandel's group. Ah, of course, the condition of tearing up Abman Urikfrit's contract remains the same."

"You're saying you'll give me command authority?"

"Not entirely... How about something like... collaborators? It's clear we're both in a tough spot."

"Sorry, but if you join, our numbers will be too large. I'll only take up to twenty people."

"Hmm, that's difficult. They are all people who have contracts with me, so abandoning them recklessly would be a great loss."

"Then our conversation ends here."

"No, not at all."

Golden Ghost then shook his head and looked at me.

Looking into his eyes, I could tell instinctively.

The previous offers were all bait, and what he was about to say now was the real point.

"Isn't the real charm of a deal in finding a middle ground through mutual compromise?"

I just stared at his smirking face without replying, and he, perhaps feeling awkward, stopped smiling and continued.

"After experiencing the Collapse, I personally think the appropriate number of people is up to seventy. Any more than that, and they just get in the way instead of helping."

"So?"

"I'll also make a concession and reduce my current group of fifty-six to forty. So, how about you, Viscount, also reduce your numbers to about thirty?"

So, this was it.

I was wondering what he was going to say.

"You're telling me to abandon my people? To make room for yours?"

"I'm saying we should cull the herd and keep only the good ones. To increase our chances of survival."

"Do you think that makes any sense?"

"Why wouldn't it?"

The Golden Ghost fellow spoke as if he genuinely didn't understand.

"Do you owe them a debt, Viscount? Or do you have some obligation to save them? Why on earth are you letting personal feelings get in the way in this situation? Are you worried about your reputation later?"

Personal feelings...

While I felt a strong repulsion to his suggestion, a part of me also found myself somewhat convinced by Golden Ghost's words.

Logically speaking, doing as he said would indeed increase our chances of survival.

"Think about it. What if the roles were reversed? If abandoning you alone, Viscount, would increase their chances of survival, what choice do you think they would make?"

I had no particular rebuttal to that either.

I, too, am the type of person who doesn't blindly believe in the intangible value of 'humanity'.

No, to be precise, I'm the type who doesn't consider humanity itself to be all that noble.

In fact, in some ways, I think the choice Golden Ghost mentioned is more human.

The will to survive is an instinct, after all.

In the face of survival, the line between good and evil is bound to blur.

"Besides, I'm just saying we should thin the herd, not that we should openly abandon them. We just need to adjust the formation a little. If we place the useless ones on the outside, they'll disappear on their own before the next Collapse is over. There'll be no need to tarnish your reputation, Viscount."

"..."

"Even if they were to learn the truth, who would blame you, Viscount? The ones who were left behind might, but those who survived would surely be grateful for your decision."

There was a strange power in the Golden Ghost fellow's words.

It was as if he understood the true nature of humanity perfectly and was using that as a basis for persuasion.

'Tsk, for a guy who looks like a swindler, he sure talks well.'

Even though a guy I disliked was saying things I disliked, I still found myself agreeing with some parts.

Ah, but of course, that didn't mean my answer would change.

"I refuse."

I had already finished contemplating that part long ago.

"...?"

The hero of the city, known as the Giant.

The Chief of the Barbarian Tribe.

A titled noble of Rafdonia.

I'm not the one who chooses anymore.

I'm the one who gives the choices.

That when that time came, they would discard him like a worn-out shoe.

Uh... no, wait a minute.

So...

At my question, which sought to deny this terrible reality, he trembled as if insulted and turned his back, as if not wanting to speak another word.

I stared blankly at his retreating back.

Hans P.

That guy is Hans P.

That fact torments me more than anything.

Chapter 851: A Choice of Three (1)

[Twisted Patience].

A skill that greatly increased Natural Regeneration in exchange for amplifying pain when hit.

However, it was a bit of a stretch to define it simply as an enhancement-type skill.

It had a curse function, you see.

Just like this.

ГЈВҖВҢThe characterГўВҖВҷs Pain Resistance decreases by 300.ГЈВҖВҚ

While this skill was active, the Pain Resistance of enemies within the radius also decreased.

Though not as much as the userГўВҖВҷs own, which was a whopping 1,000.

In truth, it didnГўВҖВҷt mean much.

That was because monsters had a common trait of their fighting spirit growing stronger and becoming more ferocious the more pain they felt.

You could even see it as a buff.

Unlike us fragile ГўВҖВҳnon-monstersГўВҖВҷ.

ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҰOof.ГўВҖВқ

The moment my Pain Resistance dropped, I could clearly feel the change throughout my body.

Drip, drip-drip-

The falling raindrops.

Just a moment ago, it had only felt like a slight itch, but now I could feel it distinctly.

To the point where I could perceive in real-time which part of my body it touched and in which direction it flowed.

Clearly.

SizzleГўВҖВҰ

A distinct burning pain bloomed.

That was only natural.

The Pain Resistance from the Corpse Golem had only been 70.

And yet, thanks to that stat, I had been able to pull off the crazy feat of continuing to fight while chugging potions.

Grrr-

Ugh, it hurt so much I was going crazy.

Of course, it wasnГўВҖВҷt yet to the point of losing my mind.

My Mental Power stat was quite high, after all.

ButГўВҖВҰ

I couldnГўВҖВҷt help but wonder.

The answer to that question came faster than I could have imagined.

Not the body of a tough Barbarian Warrior, but the weak and frail body of the modern person, Lee Hansoo.

ГўВҖВңShГўВҖВҰ itГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

Why all the in-game characters threw such a fit after being hit by that skill.

The ГўВҖВҳFearГўВҖВҷ status effect would sometimes pop up out of nowhere.

For those with low Mental Power, including fighting spirit, if that state lasted too long, they would sometimes desert the battle.

With a message like this popping up.

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has abandoned the battle due to excessive pain and is leaving the area.ГЈВҖВҚ

I remembered seeing that message for the first time back when I was researching the Fairy Archer.

I had been so flabbergasted when the main tank, a Dwarf, suddenly deserted the front line and dived into the sea.

Well, it wasnГўВҖВҷt a raid where the tank was crucial, so we managed to finish it safely in the end.

ГўВҖВҰSo that was why.

When your whole body feels like itГўВҖВҷs on fire, how could you resist the urge to jump into the water?

Yes, not an easy thingГўВҖВҰ

[ГўВҖВҰKieeeeeeeeeeeeeeekГўВҖВ”!]

Chapter 852: A Choice of Three (2)

Chapter 858: Terrible News (1)

The starting point was the First Floor's Crystal Cave.

--- Chapter 863 ---

Chapter 863: Terrible News (6)

Strictly speaking, it wasn't an alliance.

From the very beginning, Duke Kealunus had made it clear.

"Full acceptance...?"

That the residential areas would be separate.

That they would only explore the Labyrinth together.

That their identities would be thoroughly verified to prevent any criminal activities within the Labyrinth.

No matter what countermeasures were added, the truth remained unchanged.

The points listed before were just steps to soften the blow; Duke Kealunus was aiming for a complete merger.

"Are you out of your mind?"

The moment I unconsciously muttered those words, a brief silence fell before the surroundings grew noisy.

Some chastised me for such insolent words to the Chancellor.

Some said that while they wouldn't demand I use honorifics, I should still maintain some courtesy.

There was even one who insulted all non-humans in general.

And another who snickered in a very low voice, saying I must be on edge after my clanГўВҖВҷs downfall.

But none of that mattered.

But...

Thwaaack-!

Now was not the time to bicker with such small fry.

I slammed my fist on the table, using just enough force not to break it, and silence once again descended upon the meeting hall.

"......!"

"!"

Everyone just wore surprised expressions at the sudden event; this time, no one scolded or insulted me.

The reason was simple and primal.

"Say it again, Duke Kealunus. You're going to let those Noark bastards into the city?"

As if he had predicted such a situation.

"It is not yet a confirmed matter. I am merely presenting an agenda item for now."

At my words, Duke Kealunus said nothing and just looked at me, and from his gaze, I realized it too.

"It's already... been settled."

As if waiting for my words, the Duke opened his mouth.

"In fact, it was they who contacted us first. They knew that if they missed this opportunity, only ruin awaited them, so they made concessions on many fronts."

Those concessions were likely the things the Duke had mentioned earlier.

Differentiation of residential areas, identity verification, and stricter punishment for crimes, and so on.

"..."

"Furthermore, I, as the Chancellor and a noble who also wishes for the good of the kingdom, am merely presenting a path. If you truly do not approve, you may exercise your right."

Simply put, he was telling me to use my precious vote.

But from my perspective, it just sounded like mockery.

My vote of opposition wouldn't change the outcome.

The very fact that such an agenda was brought up in an official setting like the Royal Family council meant that all behind-the-scenes agreements had already been made.

"Then, may I continue the discussion?"

After that, I made my displeasure known by not answering and sitting back down with a thud.

And...

"Attendees who agree with this agenda, please turn over your coins."

Afterward, each noble attending the Royal Family council exercised their precious right, and with that, the policy direction of Rafdonia was decided.

Bang, bang, bang!

The motion for a complete end to the war with Noark and their full acceptance was passed.

***

After that, there were a few more agenda items.

"That is correct. Once the number of Explorers is replenished, we will no longer need the help of such traitors, will we?"

"Ahem, I must disagree. Do you truly believe they became traitors of their own free will? Only by minimizing discrimination and providing active support can they be reborn as perfect subjects of Rafdonia..."

Duke Kealunus continued to lead the Royal Family council, discussing the details with the attendees, sometimes debating to find a point of agreement.

How much more time passed like that?

"It seems we have mostly wrapped things up. We shall conclude this meeting here."

At last, the long, long Royal Family council came to an end.

Afterward, I requested a private meeting with Duke Kealunus, but my request was denied on the grounds of fatigue.

It was late at night when I left the Palace of Wisdom.

Thud, thud.

The procession of wagons leaving the palace disappeared, leaving the night street empty.

I walked slowly to clear my head and get some fresh air.

'What will happen from now on...'

After the motion passed, I had sat with my arms crossed and eyes closed in displeasure for the rest of the meeting, but my ears had been open, so I had a firm grasp of the subsequent discussions.

To summarize the important points:

1.  Massive support for newbies.

Rafdonia decided to pour all its remaining national power into training new Explorers.

Tax exemptions.

Increased budget for the ExplorerГўВҖВҷs Guild.

To reduce the shock to the city, the Noark Forces would be confined to Bifron for the time being.

They even said they would activate the Dimensional Plaza there so those people would enter from that location?

'I can't argue with that.'

Newbie support, Noark integration.

After all this is done, in three months, the Labyrinth will open.

Simply put, it meant that by then, I too had to finish recruiting members and be ready for the expedition...

"Ah, greetings to Viscount Yandel...!"

The Hero's Plaza in the Imperial Capital Karnon.

Once when I entered the Underground First Floor.

The Mage Tower.

"ГўВҖВ”tering at this hour. If you have business, you must return after daybreak."

Hey, what's he on about.

The one who made me come all the way to this Mage Tower.

The community of Evil Spirits.

--- Chapter 866 ---

Chapter 866: Influx (1)

The ability to revive the Ghost Busters.

No, beyond that, the chance to become the ruler of that space.

ГўВҖВҳThis might be quite tempting.ГўВҖВҷ

In truth, I didn't care much about the latter.

Just the thought that the community could be revived made my heart race without me knowing.

It was only natural.

How disappointed had I been after Auril Gavis unilaterally shut down the server?

There was a kind of stress that could only be relieved by having modern conversations periodically, and not being able to do that was truly frustrating.

It felt like being forced to quit smoking.

Sven Parab, an Evil Spirit, was in my acquaintances category, but revealing my own otherworldly origins for such a reason would be ridiculous.

ГўВҖВҳBut to think it could be revived...?ГўВҖВҷ

I had to do this, no matter what.

It was more than just stress relief.

The moment I restored it, I could communicate with my companions on the Underground First Floor through Sven Parab.

ThumpГўВҖВ”!

The thought made my heart pound, but I suppressed my excitement as much as possible and kept my expression neutral.

After all, a poker face was basic for negotiations.

"... If you have no particular use for it, perhaps I could take it and do some research."

"Hold it right there."

?

Hey, you think you can just get something like this for free?

No matter how much you try to act like it's nothing, your eyes are already full of desperation.

Without further ado, I listed my conditions.

"Join the Anabada Clan. Promise to enter the Underground First Floor. Grant me full authority over the spiritual space and share all research findings."

"... Excuse me?"

"If you agree to these conditions, I'll hand it over."

"Ah..."

"I'll tell you now, there are no other options."

I took a firm, non-negotiable stance, and the rest was up to the GM.

". I will do it."

I had succeeded in recruiting a puppet boss and a Mage.

***

After the recruitment was settled, I moved on to the next matter.

In truth, I was just as curious about this as I was about the gem.

"So, what exactly was that alarm?"

The very alarm that had blared not long after I took this gem.

Was it really just a coincidence?

"Ah, right, there was that."

As if he had completely forgotten, the GM exclaimed softly and began to explain yesterday's alarm.

"To give you the conclusion first... it seems the alarm did go off because we st... no, because we discovered this item."

"...'Seems'?"

"Then that's not a conclusion, but a guess, isn't it?"

I tilted my head and pointed it out, but he, now used to this, continued without any particular reaction.

"When I arrived at the Mage Tower, there were only a few schoolmasters there. Ah, and there was one member of the Rose Knights."

"Only a few?"

"Yes. By the time I arrived, it was already late. The forces that had gathered early had already departed, and we were told to monitor the situation from here and decide whether or not to provide support."

From what I heard, the masters gathered at the time were all confused about what was happening, and the remaining Rose Knights member provided no explanation.

"So they said they waited for a few more hours, completely in the dark..."

"Just as dawn was breaking, the Mage Tower Lord and the Rose Knights returned. From their tense expressions and the way they were watching the Royal Family, it was clear something had gone wrong."

"And then?"

"The assembly order was soon lifted. Afterwards, I cautiously asked one of the masters from the vanguard whom I was friendly with about the situation, and he said they had been to the underground sewers."

"What? The sewers?"

At this point, it was safe to say it wasn't a guess but a fact.

There was no way that many coincidences could align.

"But why did you use that phrasing?"

"Let's just say... it's a habit."

"Fix it quickly. That kind of attitude isn't good for getting by in life."

It was sincere advice, but he showed no reaction this time either, and so the conversation moved on to the next topic.

"But... where on earth did you get those coordinates? It really looks like the Mage Tower and the Royal Family collaborated to hide it away..."

...

How much of this should I tell him...

After a moment of thought, I quickly made a decision.

'We're in the same boat now, aren't we...?'

So I revealed most of what had happened in the Labyrinth.

Honestly, I also wanted to tell someone and ask for their opinion.

"[Go to the spatial coordinates I'm about to give you. I will hide something there that will be of help.]"

And that something really did exist.

That's what made it all the more confusing.

Was the Arta I met not an NPC?

Maybe that was really the case.

Because no hidden piece in the Labyrinth could affect the outside world, only the Labyrinth itself.

"Uh... so, to summarize..."

The GM's reaction after hearing my whole story was subtle.

"You're saying that a person named 'Arta' whom you met in the Labyrinth gave you those coordinates? Saying he would hide something helpful for you, Viscount?"

"That's right. And for your information, as I said before, he claimed he was from the past."

"Hah..."

The GM seemed to be pondering whether I was being serious or not.

It wasn't that I didn't understand.

It was just that unbelievable of a story.

But perhaps he sensed I wasn't joking?

"If what you say is true, Viscount, then it must be one of two possibilities."

After thinking for a short while, the GM finally spoke.

"Two possibilities?"

"Yes. The first is that Arta truly used an ancient magi-tech device to enter the Labyrinth dimension from a past timeline."

Hmm, I'd already thought of that, so I'll pass.

When there are even Evil Spirits who have crossed over from other dimensions, it wouldn't be impossible for a being to enter the Labyrinth directly.

Although, for some reason, I felt that possibility was low.

"What's the second?"

"It could be a phenomenon caused by the nature of the Labyrinth."

"Explain it simply."

"You've been outside the fortress walls, Viscount, so you must know. The world beyond the walls is very similar to the Labyrinth, a world filled with monsters."

Ah, now I see what he's getting at.

"The Labyrinth was clearly created in imitation of reality. The same could be true for the 'Arta' you met, Viscount."

Arta had been talking about the 'original version'.

"It might be difficult to understand, so let me elaborate. In actual history, Arta succeeded in time travel and met 'A' from the future. For him, Arta buried a treasure deep underground even before the Mage Tower was built. However, for some reason, 'A' was unable to find that treasure."

...

"The Labyrinth recreated that event based on history, and you, Viscount, experienced that recreated event from the position of 'A'."

I was grateful for the detailed explanation, but it wasn't the novel idea I had been hoping for.

After all, I had thought of that possibility myself.

No, in fact, I believed this was the most likely possibility.

The Labyrinth's hidden pieces always had an original that served as their motif.

But if there was one thing that bothered me...

"Then what about the Royal Family or the Mage Tower? Didn't they already know this was buried here?"

"Uh... I'm not sure about that. But isn't it possible there's some conspiracy or secret history we don't know about? Perhaps they didn't know the exact location..."

What nonsense.

Just a moment ago, he was making a fuss about how it could be a treasure hidden by the Mage Tower or the Royal Family.

Despite opening up and asking for advice, I hadn't gained much.

"Tsk."

It seems I have no choice but to find out myself.

***

I had expected it, but reviving the community using the gem took quite some time.

Even though I gave him the gem to research as he pleased, there had been no significant progress yet.

However, it wasn't a total loss.

"From now on, I intend to call this gem the 'Fragment of Origin'."

He had given the gem a name.

And a meaningful one at that: 'Fragment of Origin'.

"... Is it related to the Power of Origin?"

Having explored Arta's Secret Base, it was hard for me to think he had just chosen a cool-sounding name.

So, I persistently questioned him about the reason for giving it such a name, and after a moment's hesitation, he honestly revealed the truth.

"Someone I know once gave a similar name to an object like this."

"Who is this person...?"

"His name is Auril Gavis. He is an incredible Mage, not well-known to the public."

Right, that old man.

After all, the original owner of the community was that very same old man.

The GM had received authority from that old man to manage it, and for some reason, the old man had unilaterally shut down the community's server.

'Fragment of Origin, you say...?'

In any case, hearing this gave me a lot to think about.

First, the fact that it's called a 'fragment' suggests it's not in a complete state...

Well, if I gather all these fragments, will I gain the Power of Origin and be able to create a new Labyrinth or something?

I don't know yet.

But if I keep researching, I might be able to figure something out.

Anyway, I'll set this issue aside for now.

Swish.

I slowly tilt my head back and look up.

No, to be precise, I look at the black spire that soars endlessly into the sky.

"Oh...! It's really tilted!"

"Even seeing it up close, if it's this much... isn't that pretty serious, meow?"

The Mage Tower, which had always stood tall in the same spot, was now slightly tilted, like a certain tower in Europe.

It was probably the effect of the collapsed tunnel below.

Of course, people were completely unaware of that fact, so the case of the tilting Mage Tower became quite a hot topic.

Thanks to that, the number of tourists increased, revitalizing the surrounding commercial district...

"Wow, there are so many people... Can't you get a little bigger, meow? So I can sit on your shoulder."

"You think I can?"

I chuckled and ruffled Misha's hair.

The Mage Tower plaza was currently packed with a huge crowd.

However, they weren't just here to see the tilted Mage Tower.

There was another event today.

"Oh, they're coming...!"

Amidst the murmurs of the crowd, a long procession approached from the distance.

A procession like that of criminals bound by ropes.

"It's those Noark curs!"

"Damn them...!"

"Die! Die!"

The crowd hurled curses, while the Noark citizens, protected by soldiers, walked down the main road as if being escorted to prison.

This was the method of 'influx' that the Rafdonia Royal Family and Noark had finally agreed upon.

"Because of you, I lost my home and my family!"

"Die!"

Officially, Noark had been defeated.

They had surrendered completely, and now they were on their way to the Imperial Capital to be judged.

And...

'They will be sentenced to serve the nation in the form of labor.'

The citizens were ecstatic watching them being dragged away like criminals, but I knew this was all a well-written script.

Was that why?

"Diiiiie!!"

A man from the crowd rushing into the procession didn't seem like a coincidence at all.

Shunk!

A dagger plunged into the stomach of a Noark man whose arms were bound.

But was this perhaps his first time stabbing someone with a knife?

"Huh, huh huh..."

The one who was stabbed stood still, while the one who did the stabbing looked dazed, letting go of the dagger and stumbling backward.

An expression that seemed to say, "What have I done?"

The Noark man with the knife in him trudged toward the other man.

"Huh...!"

"Kyaaah...!"

As the large-framed Noark man, who looked like he could kill several grown men in an instant even with his hands cuffed, approached the man, the onlookers cried out in alarm.

Ah, but of course, what they expected didn't happen.

Thud.

The fierce-looking Noark man did not get angry at the man who stabbed him.

He didn't complain about being unfairly treated, asking what they had done wrong.

He just knelt and shed tears endlessly.

"Sorry... I'm so sorry..."

...

"I did it because I wanted to live, too... Because I was born in that underground den... Because they told me I had to fight..."

The Noark man cried and begged like a child, and the dazed man, stunned once more, blankly asked his age.

"Seventeen... I'm seventeen..."

"Seventeen, with that build?"

It was hard to believe, but looking closely, I could see youthful features here and there.

"You're... the same age as my son."

"S-sorry..."

"Why, at such a young age..."

Seeing the numerous scars carved into the large body, the man was once again at a loss for words.

In Rafdonia, it was an age where one couldn't even enter the Labyrinth.

But in Noark, that was the norm.

In a city where strength was everything, beyond the reach of society's systems and rules, would anyone care about age?

"I'm sorry... I wanted to live... For causing trouble..."

The Noark man pressed his forehead to the dirty ground and wept in apology, and the other man could do nothing more.

Soon enough, the soldiers who had been observing restrained the man holding the knife by grabbing both his arms.

And...

Click, click!

The reporters captured that moment with their magical tools.

--- Chapter 868 ---

Chapter 868: Influx (3)

Are they insane?

At first, that was my thought, and it seemed absurd, but after thinking about it, it wasn't an incomprehensible phenomenon.

I mean, it makes sense, right?

A game where people go missing?

Isn't that the kind of urban ghost story that would pique one's interest?

No, it wasn't even a ghost story.

It was something that was actually happening.

It was entirely possible for the number of newbies to explode as the rumors spread.

However, the problem wasГўВҖВҰ

'This isn't an issue that can be viewed so simply.'

Why hadn't something like this happened until now?

Even if time on Earth flows much slower than it does here, almost twenty years have passed since the first Evil Spirit from Earth arrived.

And yet, not a single person knew the game was connected to the 'disappearances,' and no such urban ghost story even existed.

Simply putГўВҖВҰ

'Someone must have been intentionally blocking the information.'

But that information suddenly spread.

Could this really be a coincidence?

Of course, the possibility exists.

For instance, someone might have been nearby when they were clearing the game, and a witness account could have spread.

Or perhaps someone had been tenaciously investigating the disappearances.

Such cases were entirely possible.

After all, Hyunbyeol herself started the game because of my disappearance, didn't she?

HoweverГўВҖВҰ

'Something feels offГўВҖВҰ'

In the first place, who made the cheat version?

A game that automatically sends you to another world upon clearing it.

Could an ordinary person modify the code to do that?

Well, I don't believe it's that sloppy.

It would be far more convincing to think that the bastards who sent us here had a change of heart and distributed the cheat version themselves.

'ThenГўВҖВҰ is the current explosion of new players also their doing?'

For now, that seems to be the highest probability.

But I just can't figure out their intention.

Since the Gate of the Abyss showed no signs of opening no matter how many people they sent, did they decide to switch to a swarm offensive?

"Hah, how would I know what those bastards are thinkingГўВҖВҰ"

"Pardon?"

"It's nothing, just talking to myself."

In any case, hearing more from the GM, there were many pitiful aspects to the situation.

Not only did the urban ghost story spread, leading to more people trying the game, but the cheat version was also modified, and now there was even a 300x difficulty mode?

So, even ordinary people with no knowledge of the game could easily clear it on their first try and were being kidnapped one after another into this worldГўВҖВҰ

'My goodnessГўВҖВҰ'

I myself went through hell after stepping on a trapГўВҖВҰ

My heart feels heavy.

Is there anything I can do?

'It feels like a waste to just let them die.'

The Tribe Chief of a tribe.

A Viscount of Rafdonia.

The Captain of the Anabada ClanГўВҖВҰ

***

Anyway.

Chapter 869: Influx (4)

Secret Security Force.

A state institution directly under the Rafdonia Royal Family.

However, the institution's own influence had waned somewhat.

After all, the glory of the old days was long gone.

Roles like infiltration, espionage, and operations had been transferred to the Rose Knights, and the authority to investigate nobles had also passed to Mozlan with its establishment.

In fact, it was safe to say that its main duty now was to find and execute Evil Spirits...

That was probably why its name ended with 'Force' instead of a more prestigious title.

In its heyday, its name was the Secret Security Bureau.

'They said everything changed when the era shifted with the Dawn King.'

That was why I was a bit lost when I first came to this world.

This world, over 150 years after the time setting of [Dungeon & Stone], had many differences from the one I knew.

For example, the Rose Knights.

In the game, a group with that name didn't even exist.

"Secret Security Force..."

Astarotta muttered, looking at me as if organizing his thoughts.

"You don't actually intend to catch and kill the Evil Spirits, so are you planning to gather them to form a private group?"

Yes, just as I'd expect from a smart guy, he immediately grasped my intentions.

Of course, I would have to correct a slight misunderstanding.

"I have no intention of forming a private group."

I said frankly.

"I'm just a little upset. You're accepting the Noark bastards because you're short on Magic Stones, so why can't you accept Evil Spirits?"

""

"Considering that Noark is swarming with Evil Spirits, suppressing them is not just meaningless, it's almost a futile effort."

"Hmm, that's hard to deny."

Surprisingly, Astarotta nodded without much protest.

"It seems you ultimately want to continue the previously failed 'Evil Spirit Integration Policy', but that's impossible, so give up."

"Why is that?"

"His Majesty does not wish for their civilization to contaminate this world."

Astarotta drew a firm line.

It wasn't difficult to understand what he meant.

Democracy, revolution, religion, science, and technology...

From the perspective of ruling this city, the way of thinking of a Modern Person would certainly be unwelcome.

'Though it pisses me off that he himself created all sorts of things like elevators and board games.'

In any case, I perfectly understood what he meant.

No, in fact, I came here expecting him to say just that.

So, I presented the answer I had prepared.

"That's all the more reason why I must have the Secret Security Force."

"Yes, I knew you wouldn't give up so easily. Go on, tell me. What is your reason?"

"Because if it were me, I could control and prevent everything you're worried about beforehand. If there are any who don't listen to me and try something foolish, I'll just send them to the execution block, and that will be that."

"Well, that might be possible. But from our perspective, the current situation is sufficient, so there's no reason for us to allow such a variable."

Rather than dwelling on the feasibility of my plan or things like that, Astarotta went straight to the core of the issue.

"Now, tell me then. Why should we grant your request?"

Astarotta and the Dawn King were not my parents.

Meaning, they weren't beings who would give in just because I threw a tantrum asking for something.

However, there was no need to worry about this problem.

Coincidentally, I had a free pass.

"Didn't His Majesty say it before he fell asleep? That you should do your best to grant and cooperate with whatever I request from now on."

"That was regarding the mission to kill the former Chancellor during the war."

"Oh, is that so? I suppose His Majesty woke up in the middle of his sleep to tell you that?"

I spoke in a tone anyone could recognize as sarcastic, but he remained silent, offering no response.

And that, to me, was the answer.

"What? So that never happened, and you were just daring to presume His Majesty's thoughts on your own and arbitrarily distort his command?"

""

Astarotta chose silence once again.

It was time to deliver the finishing blow.

"That day, when I came here, the Dawn King agreed to ask five favors of me."

The first favor concerning the 'Chancellor' was already done, leaving four more.

However, the next favor had not yet been given.

But one thing was certain.

"So what are you trying to say?"

Tch, so impatient.

"The Dawn King clearly wants something from me. And for that purpose, he agreed to support me completely. So you should cooperate with me."

"Hah, even without knowing what the next command is?"

"It's a 'favor', not a 'command'. I'd appreciate it if you were careful with your words. Or are you planning to interpret His Majesty's words however you please this time as well?"

After I finished speaking, Astarotta answered after a long pause.

"I will think about it."

It was practically an approval.

'They must be plotting something again...'

Pfft.

And...

"And if we did, would anything change?"

He asked mockingly.

Floor Lord, Dimensional Collapse.

Hyunbyeol was the only person who knew.

Not the strong Barbarian Warrior, Bjorn Yandel, but the incredibly weak Korean man, Lee Hansoo.

"I see."

At her words, I nodded without saying anything else.

Actually, I had a hunch.

Perhaps she saw her old self in Ragna.

But to show this much loyalty for that reason meant that Hyunbyeol had indeed changed a lot.

"Let's talk about the Secret Security Force. From what you've told me, Oppa, it seems like it's basically confirmed. What are you going to do after that?"

I explained my future plans to Hyunbyeol in their entirety, and Hyunbyeol, who had once worked in a public institution, helped fill in the details my plan was lacking.

But as we went on, a question seemed to pop into her head.

"Can you really do all this on your own, Oppa?"

"No, I don't think I can."

"What? Then what are you going to do?"

"I'll have to start looking now. But don't worry. There's someone who can do it much better than I can."

".....?"

"?"
