--- Chapter 872 ---

Chapter 872: Novice (2)

The long-awaited day to enter the Labyrinth.

Having quickly finished this round's Coming of Age Ceremony, I led the young warriors to the Dimensional Plaza myself.

For reference, there were over several hundred young warriors.

It was because the Labyrinth had been closed, so there was a backlog of warriors who had completed their Coming of Age Ceremony but hadn't yet finished their first expedition.

'It must be the same for the other tribes.'

Ah, of course, I didn't see any other tribes on the way to the Dimensional Plaza.

They would likely enter the Labyrinth through the plazas in the zones adjacent to their respective Holy Lands.

"The Labyrinth! I see the Labyrinth!"

"The dimension of sacred battle!"

"Bjorn son of Yandel, the great warrior, has led us to the Labyrinth!!"

As the Dimensional Plaza came into view, the young warriors began to get extremely excited.

'Cute.'

The Barbarian tradition was to wander a strange city before finally entering the Labyrinth, but I got rid of all that in my time.

Besides, even if that tradition remained, it would be meaningless in the current environment.

'Finding my way is easy since all the buildings have collapsed.'

Zone 7, where we were, was the area that suffered the most damage in the last war, and with the added misfortune of the Labyrinth's closure, it hadn't been properly restored.

"There are surprisingly many people."

The plaza was more crowded than I had expected.

Since the number of people settled in Zone 7 was small, I thought most would have gone to an intact plaza.

"Wow! It's Bjorn son of Yandel!!"

"I told you! I said we'd see him if we came here!"

"There are a lot of Barbarians over there, too?"

They chose this place to see me?

I wondered what kind of mindset that was, but seeing the plaza teeming with people felt like I was looking at its old self.

Well, only in appearance, that is.

'There are only real newbies here.'

More than seventy percent of the people gathered in the plaza were newbies who didn't even have proper gear.

Twenty percent were lower-rank explorers who had some gear and a team but had likely never been above the Fifth Floor.

The remaining ten percent were just above that.

'MidГўВҖВ‘ranks.'

But they were probably at the lower end of the mid-ranks.

Most of them would mainly explore the Fifth Floor, with only a few on the Sixth Floor.

From the Seventh Floor upwards, you could say there was almost no one.

It was highly likely that those of that level were called by the state in the last round, entered the Labyrinth in secret, and died away from home.

'So I've become a high-rank explorer, relatively speaking...'

A sudden thought occurred to me.

Perhaps this was why retired explorers were recently coming out of retirement one after another, trying to return to the industry.

'If they can just secure a spot on the Fifth Floor, they won't have to worry about taxes for the rest of their lives.'

A kind of cash cow, I guess.

If you form a clan and successfully occupy an empty hunting ground, you could live like a landlord with a steady income.

In fact, for that reason, ever since the Labyrinth appeared, certain islands on the Fifth and Sixth Floors have always been inaccessible to regular explorers due to clans fighting over interests.

"Then I'll go on ahead, Tribe Chief!"

"I'll achieve great things like you, Tribe Chief, and return!"

"Bahella!"

The newbie Barbarians, unable to wait any longer, let out a war cry and entered the Labyrinth.

So, I was about to join my companions.

"Hm?"

An young warrior staring blankly at the open Labyrinth portal caught my eye.

Right, his name was...

"Vidal, third son of Kizel."

An young warrior who finished his Coming of Age Ceremony today, and one of the few who didn't call out the name of his ancestral spirit when the ceremony began.

Simply put, a player from Earth.

'This attitude is a bit troublesome.'

I know he's not used to the name yet, but he needs to adapt quickly.

This cruel world never waits for us.

"Vidal, third son of Kizel."

After clearing my throat to make my presence known and calling his name again, the kid looked back at me, startled.

"Ah... T-Tribe Chief?!"

"What are you doing here? All the other warriors have entered the Labyrinth."

The kid was so flustered he couldn't say a word.

Still, it was a relief that he at least recognized he shouldn't look suspicious.

Well, the information I hammered into them helped with that.

Since I started leading the Coming of Age Ceremony, the number of incidents where someone was exposed as an Evil Spirit and executed has dropped to zero.

But paradoxically, those execution events also served as a device to make clueless newbies realize that this world is hostile to us.

Therefore...

'Oh, looks like there are no Evil Spirits this time!'

'As you all know, the moment you see an Evil Spirit that steals bodies, you must kill it!'

I always said these things during the Coming of Age Ceremony to convey information to the newbies.

But did he not understand it well?

"Are you perhaps afraid of entering the Labyrinth?"

Despite the Tribe Chief's question, the kid just trembled, unable to answer.

If he had any sense, he would know that his behavior itself looked suspicious.

'Not that I don't understand.'

I suddenly remembered the day I first entered the Labyrinth.

Actually, I was similar to this kid.

Watching the Barbarians enter the Labyrinth full of excitement, I, on the contrary, wanted to run away.

Because what I had done was a game!

Actually go into the Labyrinth and fight monsters? Did that even make sense?

Although I rationally knew I had to do it, the decision wasn't easy.

In the end, it was only after gaining courage from my conversation with Ainar that I could take my first step into the Labyrinth.

So this kid will need it too.

A warm hand to push his back, so he can take that first step more easily.

Just like this.

Thwack!

When I smacked him on the back, the kid shrieked in surprise and then looked up at me with angry eyes.

"Aargh!! Why, why did you hit me!"

"Your eyes look much better now."

Yes, this is better than being frozen.

It suits a Barbarian, too.

I gave the stick first, so now it's time for the carrot.

"I understand being nervous. Entering the Labyrinth is always a daunting task. I was the same."

The kid said nothing.

He just looked at me as if suspecting I was baiting him.

Hmm, this kind of suspicion is good.

"Vidal, third son of Kizel."

"..."

"Believe. In the strong body you possess, in the unyielding intellect of a warrior, and in the reality that it's better to die fighting than to starve."

""

"If you believe in just these three things until the very end, you'll be able to survive for a long time."

That was my advice, and I didn't bother to hear his reply.

I just patted the kid's back more gently than before, then lost interest and left.

"Then I'll be on my way! I'm hungry!"

Indeed, I never looked back at the kid and headed to the spot where I was supposed to meet my companions.

When I returned to the plaza to enter the Labyrinth, he was nowhere to be seen.

I wonder if he went in.

Or did he turn his back and run?

A brief curiosity arose, but I didn't dwell on it.

"What are you doing? Not going in?"

Well, if we're fated, we'll meet again.

***

[You have entered the First Floor, Crystal Cave.]

***

The most familiar place to all explorers.

The First Floor, Crystal Cave.

The brilliant glow of the crystals.

A cave shining with a soft light.

Those on their first expedition feel an unknown eeriness here, but those with some experience feel a sense of stability instead.

Because as you continue to explore, you learn that there's no place as safe as the First Floor.

It's a place where one naturally feels at ease...

Thump~!

My heart is pounding.

Not a pleasant throb of anticipation, but a low pound, like when having a foreboding thought.

Thump-!

What is this? Something like PTSD?

It's clearly a normal scene on the First Floor, but my heart keeps pounding anxiously.

It feels like at any moment, a static sound will fill the air and the whole world will collapse.

"Bjorn? Are you okay?"

"Ah... I'm fine. I was just lost in thought for a moment."

It felt like something was wrong with my mind, but I tried my best not to show it and moved on.

Well, it's probably because this is my first expedition since that incident.

I'll be fine soon.

'I will be fine, right?'

A sense of anxiety arose again, wondering if this would continue, so I quickly checked my surroundings.

I saw the members of Team 1 who had entered the Labyrinth with me after forming a Binding Spell.

Misha.

Bear Uncle.

Garton Cultner from Armin's expedition.

Malid Kevron from Tony Tooth.

And even the navigator helmsman Jackson, whom we hired as a mercenary.

Team 1, composed of six people in total.

Ainar was dispatched to Team 2, where Armin is the Team Leader.

They are short on vanguards there.

"If everyone is ready, we'll be starting soon."

I soon began the expedition and picked up the pace, but we couldn't go as fast as I had thought.

The reason was very simple.

Because more than ninety percent of the people this round were newbies.

"Wow, it's my first time seeing the First Floor this crowded..."

The newbies who had just entered were blocking the paths, acting as obstacles.

Usually, more than ninety percent of the entrants would head straight to the upper floors, but this time it was the complete opposite.

The newbies had no intention of leaving the starting point.

Instead, they looked around in wonder, found companions to move with,

or shouted when they saw me.

"... It's Bjorn Yandel!!"

"... Am I your friend?"

"Wow! He answered me!!"

Phew, this isn't easy.

The atmosphere is so different from usual that it's hard to adapt.

'If the Noark people were here too, it would have been even harder to adapt.'

The fortunate thing was that the Noark people's right to explore the Labyrinth had been postponed.

They waged a media war on the level of brainwashing, but there's still a considerable percentage of opposition, I hear?

'Next month at the earliest, three months from now at the latest.'

For that reason, time is gold on this expedition.

It makes sense, right?

No matter what kind of institutional arrangements are in place, in the Labyrinth, fists are closer than the law.

When the Noark people are running rampant in the Labyrinth, I too will have to move more cautiously than usual.

'Besides, those Noark bastards are at least mid-rank explorers.'

This is also a very frustrating part for me.

In reality, from the Sixth Floor and above, the majority will be from Noark rather than Rafdonia.

In other words, the time I can do whatever I want is running out.

"Phew, I can breathe now that we're in the Outermost Area."

"Then I'll pick up the pace again."

After leaving the central area, which could be called the starting point, the blocked paths were wide open, so we really picked up speed.

Well, even so, I missed first place.

Shwaaaaaaaa-

The portal was open, as if to prove someone had already passed through.

It was something I had expected.

The newbies were clinging to us so much as we left the central area.

In fact, the starting position wasn't good either,

[The character has entered the Second Floor: Goblin Forest.]

[The character has entered the Third Floor: Path of the Pilgrim.]

It's a bit disappointing.

Tsk, I wanted to get some free experience points before the Noark bastards swarmed in.

'Even if there are no high-rank explorers, I was too late.'

I arrived on the Third Floor on the afternoon of the third day.

Considering that the first opening usually happens on the afternoon of the second day, it was absurdly late.

Is that why?

"Ah, you're here! We were just starting to worry that something might have happened."

"Sorry. The explorers on the First Floor were clinging to us."

Still, was luck on my side?

Before we arrived, Team 2 had already obtained one 'Nameless Fragment.'

But...

"It's no good."

From then on, luck wasn't with me.

No matter how I look at it, it's impossible for now.

No, actually, it'll probably be the same next time.

'We don't have enough Mages.'

Even if we had arrived on time, the result would have been the same.

With our current members, even opening the portal to the Underground First Floor is difficult.

'I just wanted to check if I could open the portal or not...'

Right, let's do this next time when we're more prepared.

Rushing things often leads to mistakes.

I soon set aside my disappointment and set up camp.

After all, I hadn't slept properly because I was digging for fragments.

The plan was for everyone to get a full day's rest and then continue the expedition...

Trudge.

In the end, I failed to get proper rest too.

Trudge.

There was an unknown uninvited guest who found our camp.

Do they not know the unwritten rule that approaching another team's camp within the Labyrinth is considered a hostile act?

Or do they not care because they intend to be hostile?

'It would have been better if it were the latter...?'

Sadly, they belonged to the 'former' group.

"Stop, don't come any closer! Huh? I said don't come closer."

"We're not really causing you any harm, are we? We're getting tired too, so we'll just rest here for a bit and then leave! Huh? Or what, should we get hurt resting by ourselves in this dangerous place?"

'They' strode fearlessly into our territory as if Misha's attempts to stop them while she was on watch weren't even worth listening to.

Incomprehensibly bold and violent.

They, who cannot be controlled even by the strength of a giant.

"Kyaaaaaaaaaaah!"

"Oh my god, oh my god, oh my god!!"

Their name was... newbies.

ГЈВҖВҢYour character has entered Paradise Island.ГЈВҖВҚ

--- Chapter 874 ---

Chapter 874: Paradise Island (1)

Paradise Island.

Even in the Labyrinth, a place full of bizarre incidents and mysteries, a one-person entry Rift was a rare sight.

However, that didn't mean a one-person clear was mandatory.

It just meant you couldn't enter with your companions.

The total capacity for the Rift was thirty people.

Each participant would enter this island through one of thirty portals opened across the entire Fifth Floor.

Simply put, it was a Rift that had to be conquered by cooperating not with companions you trusted, but with unfamiliar explorersГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВңIt does feel like IГўВҖВҷve come to a resort.ГўВҖВқ

Perhaps it was because the Canyon of Wraiths, where I had been just a moment ago, was a grim place where the screams of restless spirits echoed.

The sudden change in atmosphere upon entering the Rift felt strange.

The darkness of the labyrinth that had limited visibility had long since vanished without a trace.

Warm sunlight shone down.

The white sandy beach danced in the light, reflecting the sunГўВҖВҷs rays.

It was the same for the clear, emerald-colored sea over there.

ГўВҖВңI wonder what expressions theyГўВҖВҷre making.ГўВҖВқ

Suddenly, I unintentionally thought of the companions I had left behind in the Great Demon Realm.

They must have been quite startled when the portal closed after only I had entered.

Well, that surprise probably didn't last long.

As they were all experienced veterans, they would have quickly realized why this had happened.

Paradise Island was quite famous, after all.

Ah, not in a good way, of course.

Flop.

With nothing to do until the full quota of participants was met, I simply lay down on the sand and enjoyed a sunbath.

And how much time had passed?

ГўВҖВңH-HelloГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВқ

Opening my eyes, I looked in the direction of the voice and saw an explorer a short distance away, looking at me with cautious eyes.

ГўВҖВңAre youГўВҖВҰ a person?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat else would I look like?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhew! What a relief! Do you happen to know whatГўВҖВҷs going on? For some reason, the companions who were supposed to follow me in haven't arrivedГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

It seemed this guy didn't know what kind of Rift Paradise Island was.

He also didn't seem to recognize me yet.

ГўВҖВңDonГўВҖВҷt worry. Your companions are probably fine, waiting for your return.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңUmГўВҖВҰ does that mean this is a Rift that only one person can enter at a timeГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIf you know anything, please tell me. IГўВҖВҷll reward you handsomely.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңForget the reward. Just sit down somewhere and wait. ItГўВҖВҷll be a while before it starts.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВқ

With those words, I closed my eyes again.

The man tried to say something more, but seemingly exhausted by my complete disregard, he shut his mouth and sat down nearby.

And how much more time had passed?

ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҰWhat is this! Wake up! ItГўВҖВҷs suddenly gotten dark all around!ГўВҖВқ

Waking up to the sound of his startled voice, I slowly stretched and loosened my body.

The island had become as dark as the middle of the night.

It wasn't particularly surprising.

ГўВҖВңLooks like everyoneГўВҖВҷs gathered now.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңGatheredГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYouГўВҖВҷll see when we get there. Follow me.ГўВҖВқ

Saying that, I headed towards the center of the island, from where a colorful radiance was flowing.

The man, though confused about the situation, quietly followed behind me.

And thenГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs other explorersГўВҖВҰ!ГўВҖВқ

In a clearing where a giant crystal emitting a multicolored light was located.

ГўВҖВҳStill only about half have gathered.ГўВҖВҷ

ГўВҖВңLook, two more have arrived!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңDonГўВҖВҷt be on your guard, come over here! IГўВҖВҷll explain the situation all at once when everyone has gatheredГўВҖВҰ Huh? V-Viscount YandelГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВқ

The female MageГўВҖВҷs eyes widened as if she recognized me.

At this, the man next to me also widened his eyes.

ГўВҖВңYou were Viscount YandelГўВҖВҰ? I had a feeling when I saw you were a BarbarianГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIs that important right now?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAhГўВҖВҰ my apologies.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWith the Viscount here, we have even less to worry about! We can just think of it as relaxing for a bit before leaving.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңRelaxing before leaving? What does that mean?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAhahaГўВҖВҰ Please wait a moment. I was planning to explain everything about Paradise Island once everyone is here. Oh, IГўВҖВҷm Ibella Rianelin.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAlend Daren. As you can see, IГўВҖВҷm an archer. I can also handle close combat and serve as a Scout.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHaha, you donГўВҖВҷt need to introduce yourself like that. ThereГўВҖВҷs no need to fight in this Rift.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң...?ГўВҖВқ

The two exchanged brief introductions.

Since I didnГўВҖВҷt feel the need, I just found a spot a little away from the group and sat down.

Alend, who had followed me this far, glanced at me and then joined the group.

Well, the company of a friendly-looking female Mage was probably far more appealing than that of an unfriendly Barbarian.

ГўВҖВңEveryone, your attention for a moment, please!ГўВҖВқ

As a little more time passed, almost all the explorers who had seen the light from across the island had gathered.

Ah, the reason I say almost all and not all is simple.

There were 29 people, not 30.

ГўВҖВңFor some reason, one person hasn't arrived yet, but since we don't know when they'll come, I'll start the explanation. This place is called Paradise Island, and it's a very unique type of Rift! You donГўВҖВҷt need to fight.ГўВҖВқ

Exhibiting a very gentle leadership, Ibella explained ГўВҖВҳParadise IslandГўВҖВҷ so clearly that even a child could easily understand.

However, was there someone who understood even less after hearing the explanation?

A fierce-looking axe warrior scowled.

ГўВҖВңSo, what youГўВҖВҷre saying, missy, is that if we just hold out for ten days, an escape portal will open and weГўВҖВҷll get a reward, is that it?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes, thatГўВҖВҷs right.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThen arenГўВҖВҷt we in big trouble? The one person who hasnГўВҖВҷt arrived yet. That person might already be dead.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңUmГўВҖВҰ I donГўВҖВҷt think thatГўВҖВҷs likely, but even if it were, it wouldnГўВҖВҷt be a big problem. No, to be blunt, itГўВҖВҷs actually to our advantage.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAdvantageГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSince some of you may already know about this island, IГўВҖВҷll just be honest. When ten days have passed, the fewer people are left, the bigger the reward each individual gets.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat?ГўВҖВқ

At IbellaГўВҖВҷs explanation, the axe warrior looked around in bewilderment.

He probably got the gist of it.

A reward is given to all survivors.

However, the fewer the survivors, the greater the reward.

How dangerous these two simple rules were.

In fact, when I was active in Stone Iven, I used to call Paradise Island ГўВҖВҳBattle Royale IslandГўВҖВҷ.

ГўВҖВңBut donГўВҖВҷt worry too much. Just think of it as a comfortable rest before weГўВҖВ”ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes. Of course. Do you see over there?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңViscount Yandel is right there, so what could be the problem? No one would harm another person just to get a slightly better reward.ГўВҖВқ

At IbellaГўВҖВҷs words, the explorers who had been secretly anxious showed expressions of slight relief.

And the same was true for the axe warrior.

Well, it wasn't an incomprehensible sentiment.

HoweverГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВҳWell, I wonder about that.ГўВҖВҷ

Because among all those playthroughs, it had only happened once.

The time everyone managed to survive.

***

Well, it was not a difficult request.

ГўВҖВңThe only good raider is a dead raider.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң...ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAnd if you commit murder for a reward, youГўВҖВҷre no different from a raider.ГўВҖВқ

Was it helpful that I had clearly stated my position?

Only then did the explorers who had been watching me nod their heads in relief.

ГўВҖВңWhat a relief. After he said that much, I doubt anyone would have any foolish thoughts.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңTrue, who would do such a thing when you get a reward just for staying put. It's not like we're Noark scavengers.ГўВҖВқ

An atmosphere that had visibly softened.

Within it, the explorers engaged in conversation, befriending strangers, and naturally, groups began to form.

ГўВҖВңEveryone, where are you going?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWe canГўВҖВҷt just sit around like this for ten days, can we? We plan to scout the area first. A Rift is bound to have valuable things hidden in it.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAhГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

The Men's Party for short, left the central area.

AndГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВңLady Ibella, weГўВҖВҷre also going to do some scouting.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAs far as I know, there isnГўВҖВҷt anything that valuable hidden hereГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYou never know. Besides, IГўВҖВҷm still worried about that one person who hasn't arrived. If we find them, we'll explain the situation and, if possible, bring them here.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, yesГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

Thus, the second group also followed the Men's Party and left the central area.

The second group was characterized by having ten members and a good balance of men and women.

ГўВҖВҳIГўВҖВҷll call them the Social Party.ГўВҖВҷ

After the Social Party left, a total of 14 people remained.

Here, there was a third group.

It was the group centered around Ibella.

ГўВҖВңMiss Ibella, is it alright to just let them go like this?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat do you mean?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThe one person who hasn't been found yet. We don't know what kind of person they are.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңStill, theyГўВҖВҷll be moving in a group, so I doubt anything will happen, right? Ah, perhaps you all are alsoГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNo, we plan to stay here until the Rift ends. As you said, Miss Ibella, this is the safest place. We donГўВҖВҷt want to attract any unnecessary suspicion either.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSuspicionГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷm worried that those who left might stir up some trouble. And when that happens, we will have to be the ones to punish them. If even the bare minimum of law disappears, these ten days will be hell.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYouГўВҖВҷre right, Mr. Tehan.ГўВҖВқ

Although it seemed centered around Ibella, a closer look revealed that a man named John Tehan also had considerable say and presence.

ГўВҖВҳTheyГўВҖВҷd be good as the Vigilante PartyГўВҖВҰ Anyway.ГўВҖВҷ

If they were the third groupГўВҖВҰ

The fourth was me.

To be precise, it was the type that, like me, didn't belong to any group and maintained their individualism.

Ah, the number was the smallest, at just four.

ГўВҖВҳSighГўВҖВҰ whatГўВҖВҷs going to happen now?ГўВҖВҷ

Honestly, I thought it would be best to just spend ten days here safely and then leave.

ГўВҖВҳShould I have just locked them all up in the central area and not even given them the chance to do anything else?ГўВҖВҷ

That thought came to me belatedly, but I let go of the lingering regret.

Yes, this was just about right.

I had already given them a minimal warning, hadnГўВҖВҷt I?

If someone were to cause trouble despite that, they were likely the type to do similar things on a regular basis.

It would be better to nip the bud here.

ГўВҖВҳI also lacked the justification for complete control.ГўВҖВҷ

If something were to happen, then I would have a reason to intervene.

Of course, it would be best if nothing happened.

ГўВҖВңDid your scouting yield any results?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңLady IbellaГўВҖВҰ!ГўВҖВқ

Hmm, no, that might be a bit of a misstatement.

ГўВҖВңWho is that on your back?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWe found him during our search. He seems to be the thirtieth explorer we were looking forГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHeГўВҖВҷs already deadГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes. Please gather everyone. We must find the culprit.ГўВҖВқ

So, all-out survival failed again.

Still, I didn't expect it to be on the first day.

--- Chapter 875 ---

Chapter 875: Paradise Island (2)

Paradise Island.

For the thirty explorers who set foot on this island, there was only one goal.

Survival.

Even if they did nothing in particular, as long as they succeeded in surviving for ten days, the Rift would be cleared.

The explorers who participated in the Rift could then return to their respective places, each with their own reward.

However, the surprising thing wasГўВҖВҰ

'That there's nothing.'

On this island, where 'survival' was the only objective, there were absolutely no monsters or traps that could threaten the explorers' lives.

Strictly speaking, it wasn't that there were none at all.

But it was more accurate to consider them nonexistent.

As long as one didn't do anything foolish and simply rested on this island, they were beings one would never encounter.

You could say they had no effect on survival.

Moreover, this island lived up to its name, with clean water and delicious fruits scattered everywhere, so there was no need to worry about starvation.

To put it simply, one could rest comfortably for ten days, and when the time came, they could return with a decent reward.

It could be called an island that was literally a paradise on earth, butГўВҖВҰ

'There's no way such a place exists in the Labyrinth.'

This island was far from a paradise.

To prove that fact, there was no need to even mention the things I experienced while playing the game.

Because even just looking at the current situation, wasn't it obvious?

A time before even the first day had ended.

One casualty had already occurred, and because of this, the 'survivors' were suspicious of each other and had prepared to defend themselves.

Just like this.

"First, there are fifteen suspects. The rest of us can prove to each other that we haven't taken a single step away from here."

The man who had established himself as something like the vice-captain of the Vigilante Party, John Tehan, scanned the Social Party and the Men's Party, who had gone out on an expedition, with a sharp gaze.

Needless to say, those who received such a look were not about to stay silent.

"The same goes for us! When we discovered this man's corpse, the ten of us were in one place, and no one had left their spot until then."

"That is your claim. Please understand that we cannot rule out the possibility that all ten of you conspired together."

"By that logic, your claim is also just your claim."

"Viscount Yandel was also here. Do you really think the Viscount would participate in such a criminal act?"

At Tehan's words, which clung to me, the leader of the Social Party could not offer any rebuttal.

But the absence of a rebuttal did not mean the absence of a self-defense.

They say the best defense is a good offense, right?

The leader of the Social Party was a seasoned explorer.

"Ahem, in that case, it would be more reasonable to view them with more suspicion than us. After all, there are only five of them."

If you can't break the arrow, it's the stance of a wise explorer to turn its direction.

"What do you mean by that! Suddenly dragging us into this!"

"Wouldn't it be much easier for five people who just met today to come to an agreement than for ten people to do so? Furthermore, about half of our group are women."

"Indeed... that is a reasonable argument."

"Damn it! What kind of bullshit is that! What's the difference between ten and five, and what do women have to do with it!"

The Men's Party, characterized by their rough looks, were going crazy, but the momentum had already shifted.

Because they were the minority.

"Would it be alright if we checked the belongings of the five of you?"

"Bullshit! Just try and touch them!"

"Why are you hiding them?"

"If it were you, would you just obediently show everything?"

"I would. Especially to prove my innocence."

"Fuck, what did we do to be suspected? Fuck off, if you want to check our pockets, you all have to do it too! Otherwise, it's not happening."

"As you heard, the estimated time of death for that man was about four hours ago. I believe our innocence has been sufficiently proven, but?"

With those words, the Men's Party drew their weapons as if to say there was no room for negotiation, and the atmosphere became uncontrollably hostile.

"Now, now, everyone, please calm down a bit."

As the situation escalated, Ibella, who had been watching quietly, stepped forward.

"After examining the corpse, I found quite a few strange things. So before we suspect each other and draw our weapons, how about we talk about that part first?"

"Strange things, you say..."

"First of all, this man. No matter how much I look, I can't find anything that could identify him."

"That's because the culprit..."

"In the first place, why did the culprit kill this person? If the motive was his gear, there are no signs of anything being taken."

"Ah..."

Simply put, it wasn't a robbery-murder for gear or valuables.

So why did this man die?

"It could be that the culprit is someone who knows about this island."

At the opinion someone muttered, the explorers' eyes sharpened.

Because if it was someone who killed for the island's reward, they wouldn't be satisfied with just one person.

We would all be targets.

"Well. We can't be sure yet, since it hasn't been confirmed if this man was truly innocent either."

"Huh? What do you mean by that?"

"This man might have tried to harm the 'culprit' first, and the culprit might have just fought back in self-defense."

"But if the situation was that innocent, there would be no reason to hide it, would there?"

"That... can all be explained by what just happened. With what could he have possibly proven his own innocence?"

It's easier to conceal than to prove.

It was a statement that made sense in many ways, and perhaps because of the incident that had just occurred, the other explorers couldn't offer any particular rebuttal to that point.

"Then what about the identification badge? Why was it taken?"

"That... I don't know either. But we still lack clues to confirm anything."

-she was about to say.

Ibella's words, as if intending to say so, were cut off in the middle.

"Bahella!!"

I was starting to get bored.

***

A silence descended upon the sudden shout.

However, no one pointed out my impulsive action.

"It seems the Viscount has something he wishes to say."

After a brief pause, Ibella's summary focused everyone's attention on me.

The Men's Party, the smallest group, was looking at me with very worried eyes.

They must have judged that if I took their side here, there would be no way out.

"You all..."

As I finally opened my mouth, the concentration heightened to the point where I could feel people's ears pricking up.

As it was my statement that could be a game-changer, everyone was holding their breath and listening intently.

I felt a little sorry about that part.

I had no intention of presenting a new logic, nor did I plan to.

In the first place, why is logic even necessary?

"Why are you all so stupid?"

"... Pardon?"

"Can't we just check everyone's belongings!"

"But it's a reasonable procedure to first check those who are suspicious."

Reasonable procedure, my foot.

That's something only weaklings do.

"Did you just call me stupid?"

"Pardon? No, that's not what I meant..."

"What do you mean, no! Didn't I just say it wasn't reasonable!"

"So, that definitely doesn't mean you're stupid."

"... You're even lying?"

I couldn't believe it.

Only trash like raiders deceive barbarians.

Ah, right!

"You were the culprit, weren't you?"

As I strode forward, hammer in hand, the man flinched and stepped back.

"Wh-what are you talking about! It's not me! I've been here the whole time...!"

"Raiders always claim their innocence."

"... Belongings! Then check my belongings! It will prove my innocence! If I were the culprit, wouldn't I have that identification badge!"

"Oh, is that okay?"

"Yes! Please check... but put that hammer down first..."

"Hmm, if you insist. I suppose there's no reason I can't."

And so, very peacefully, the first member for the search and seizure was decided.

His name was Matt Grail.

The explorer rank written on the identification badge from his pocket was 5.

He voluntarily laid out everything he had in front of everyone, but no suspicious items were found.

AndГўВҖВҰ

"You, why are you looking at me with those eyes?"

The next turn belonged to a member of the Social Party whose eyes I happened to meet.

"I-I, what kind of look did I give..."

"Suspicious. It seems a search is necessary."

"What's with that dissatisfied expression? Could you be the culprit?"

However, from the second time onward, there was no need to lift the hammer of peace.

It was thanks to Ibella, who had roughly understood the situation, taking care of the traffic control on her own.

"I know it's uncomfortable for everyone, but let's cooperate. It seems the Viscount intends to check everyone. This might actually be better. Since everyone's belongings are being inspected, it's not unfair."

"... I understand. However, the rest of you, please watch each other thoroughly. Someone might try to hide something during this."

After that, it was a smooth process.

Each explorer came forward in order to have their belongings inspected, while the rest watched each other to prevent any funny business.

AndГўВҖВҰ

The second, the third, the fourth.

The inspection of belongings proceeded quickly.

"Hmm, what's this? You're clearly a man, so why do you have a woman's underwear..."

"Th-that's my wife's! Everyone, don't look at me like that! It's just that I feel like things go well when I carry it around, so I always keep it with me!"

Pass.

It was a little suspicious, but the explorer industry is particularly weak to superstitions and jinxes.

In the first place, even in modern times, there were quite a few sports athletes with strange routines because of such jinxes.

"This is... a Firefly Ring?"

During the seventh search, I flinched.

"Yes. But is there a problem...?"

"Of course there's a problem. For safety, I'll hold onto this for now."

"... Pardon?"

"Just kidding."

Wow, how does this guy have something so precious when he's only Fifth-Rank?

Afterward, I rummaged through his belongings much more thoroughly than usual, but unfortunately, there was nothing suspicious in the Firefly Ring owner's bag.

'Tsk.'

If he had been the culprit, I could have rightfully beaten him to death and taken the ring.

The eighth, the ninth, the tenth.

As the search continued, I was able to grasp some information about the explorers who had entered Paradise Island.

Isn't there a saying that you can tell what kind of person someone is by looking at their room?

It was a similar context.

An explorer's bag inevitably reveals the various characteristics of that individual.

'This one has a lot of spare daggers, so he probably has at least one throwing skill...

Three high-grade potions? Is he rich?'

Someone with many family photos cares for their family that much, and having a lot of empty space in a bag is also a piece of information.

Usually, professional raiders who plan to carry a lot of spoils of war tend to keep their bags empty.

That's why the explorers had reacted so sensitively to the act of revealing their bags.

"Nothing strange this time either."

Ibella, who was assisting with the search beside me, spoke with a tone of disappointment, but I actually wasn't expecting anything.

I didn't believe an identification badge or a murder weapon would turn up just by doing this.

'Unless he's an idiot, he would have thrown it away a long time ago.'

Ah, of course, that doesn't mean what I'm doing right now is meaningless.

My goal was to understand each individual through their belongings.

I had already found a few slightly suspicious guys, so once the search was over, the real deduction would beginГўВҖВ”

"Uh...?"

It was during the nineteenth search.

"Th-this person, why does he have two identification badges?"

For the first time, a person with two identification badges appeared.

Checking the personal information, one of them was indeed his own.

The problem, however, was the other identification badge.

"Height 165, male, brown hair, warrior..."

All the personal details written on the second identification badge matched the deceased.

"Uh... th-this, why is this here..."

The cold gazes of the survivors gathered on the owner of the backpack.

John Tehan.

The man who had been the first to take the lead, saying that those who had gone on the expedition should be searched.

"This crazy bastard, so this is why you drove us into a corner earlier!"

The Men's Party, who had been through a rough time, even took out their weapons and surrounded him, but...

Surprisingly, he made no excuses.

No, to be precise, he couldn't.

"Ugh, ugh...!"

His face turned pale blue, and in an instant, John Tehan's entire body melted away.

Chiiiiik!

At the unannounced tragedy, everyone watching was at a loss for words.

Yes, this is Paradise Island.

--- Chapter 876 ---

Chapter 876: Paradise Island (3)

In truth, I had thought so.

The first victim, whose name was unknown.

No, I know his name now after checking his identification badge, but anyway.

[This man might have tried to harm the 'culprit' first, and the culprit might have fought back in self-defense.]

I had predicted there was a high chance that Ibella's hypothesis from a moment ago was correct.

It makes sense, doesn't it?

A tragedy caused by a fight between two explorers who didn't know much about this island.

It was something that could easily happen.

After all, they entered a Rift, only for the portal to close before their companions could follow, leaving them stranded alone in an unfamiliar place.

They probably doubted if the other person was even real.

And even if they were, they wouldn't have been able to trust them.

After learning about this island in the central area, they would have hidden the fact, fearing they might be held responsible for the murder.

ButГўВҖВҰ.

ГўВҖВңThat wasn't it.ГўВҖВқ

The death of John Tehan proves it.

The 'culprit' is clearly harming people with malicious intent.

AndГўВҖВҰ

'It would be right to assume that the culprit knows quite a lot about this island.'

Just as I had roughly organized my thoughts, someone shouted while looking at John Tehan, who had turned into a pool of blood.

ГўВҖВңKingГўВҖВҷs BloodГўВҖВҰ! ItГўВҖВҷs KingГўВҖВҷs Blood!ГўВҖВқ

KingГўВҖВҷs Blood.

The name of a poison used for suicide, mainly by the Rose Knights or the Royal Family's secret agents, whose names we don't even know.

However, the surprising thing is that this poison doesn't actually exist.

ItГўВҖВҷs sort of like America's Area 51, where they're said to torture aliens.

The public's fear and imagination regarding the Royal Family's secret agents created the ghost story of 'King's Blood'.

ГўВҖВңKing's BloodГўВҖВҰ! Don't tell me this man was a henchman of the King?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWatch your mouth! There might be other agents nearby!ГўВҖВқ

Sigh, such ignorant explorers.

That's not something like 'King's Blood'.

Well, the Rafdonia Royal Family might have a similar kind of poison pill, though.

It wouldn't be a poison that turns a person into a pool of blood in an instant.

In the first place, if such a powerful poison existed, why would it be used for suicide?

It would be used for assassination.

And in the first placeГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВңEveryone, calm down! Why does everyone assume that man committed suicide?ГўВҖВқ

Right, not all the explorers were idiots.

ГўВҖВңWellГўВҖВҰ he was framed as the culprit, so didn't he have no choice but to conceal the truth with his death?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhy would a secret agent of the Royal Family do that? If he had just revealed his identity and said he had a reason for the killing, we wouldn't have been able to say a thing!ГўВҖВқ

Chapter 877. Paradise Island (4)

Day 1, Day 2.

The third day on Paradise Island.

ГўВҖВҳIt wasnГўВҖВҷt death, it was a movement skill.ГўВҖВҷ

***

Chapter 3 is a truly troublesome chapter.

'Each time you kill someone, all your stats increase by 1.5 times.'

If you kill one person and then another in succession, the 1.5 times boost is applied to your already 1.5 times strengthened state, a miraculous calculation.

--- Chapter 883 ---

Chapter 883: Alternate Character (3)

I carefully reached out and took the doll.

Then, as if handling hazardous material, I lifted it very, very slowly to examine it.

The appearance itself was perfectly fine.

It was a stuffed doll, not a hard, jointed one.

However, it was made of leather, not cloth, and there were no tears or worn out spots.

To the touch, the stuffing was soft and plush.

It did not feel like a cursed doll at all.

Of course, the blank face was a bit unsettling.

ГўВҖВҳThis part will probably change to resemble the owner once one is setГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҷ

As I meticulously examined the doll, I felt my brow furrowing without realizing it.

Well, to be precise, I felt my worries deepening.

No.666, Hans FerretГўВҖВҷs Outing Doll.

I could not deny that it was a useful Numbers Item.

It would surely be of great help in my future plans.

But...

ГўВҖВңHow can a Numbers Item's name beГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

I could not bring myself to finish the sentence.

Of course, it was something I did not think much about when I played the game.

Many Numbers Items were named in this format.

For example, Kraul's Demon Crusher.

There were many Numbers Items with names like 'So-and-so's Such-and-such', and I always dropped the 'So-and-so' and just called it 'Such-and-such'.

Kraul's Demon Crusher was just Demon Crusher.

This doll too, I used to just call it the 'Outing Doll'.

ГўВҖВңEven the number is fucking terrible.ГўВҖВқ

The name had 'Hans' in it, and the number was 666?

If this was not a cursed doll, then what was!

It even had an effect that was practically a curse.

When using the Outing Doll, my Luck stat would temporarily drop by about 300.

Thump!

Just holding the doll in my hand made the Warrior's Heart beat powerfully, as if sensing danger.

An impulse to throw the doll on the floor welled up inside me.

But the stronger the impulse, the more I regained my reason.

ГўВҖВҳ...I need it.ГўВҖВҷ

The item's performance was too appealing to give up over a jinx.

ГўВҖВңDamn it.ГўВҖВқ

I immediately grabbed the ring on the doll's back and pulled the string.

[The character has used [Hans FerretГўВҖВҷs Outing Doll].]

It did not activate immediately; there was a short casting time.

On the doll's blank face, threads were stitched like embroidery, forming new facial features.

[The doll has been bound to the user.]

A face that subtly resembled me.

ГўВҖВҳSurprisingly, it doesn't feel strange.ГўВҖВҷ

ГўВҖВңAh, ah, ah...?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВҳI don't really like this part.ГўВҖВҷ

It was too high pitched for a Barbarian.

Squeeze.

I clenched and unclenched my fist.

I was in a pure Level 1 commoner state.

This doll could not copy the main body's performance.

ГўВҖВҳIt has a high degree of freedom.ГўВҖВҷ

First, there was no distance limit.

Meaning, no matter how far I went from the main body, the summoning would not be undone, and there was no separate duration either.

As long as I accepted the risk of the cooldown increasing in proportion to the usage time, I could use it for days on end.

And crucially,

ГўВҖВҳLevel Up.ГўВҖВҷ

This Outing Doll could level up and consume Essences.

Simply put, it could be used like an Alternate Character.

ГўВҖВҳWell, it's difficult to use for combat, though.ГўВҖВҷ

The Outing Doll could not remove an Essence once it was consumed.

ButГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰ.

Tap-!

ГўВҖВҳI can send this thing to the Underground First Floor first.ГўВҖВҷ

--- Chapter 886 ---

Chapter 886: The Twins (1)

Shhk, shhk.

I put on my armor.

It wasn't the sturdy plate armor that made one feel cozy just by wearing it, but a light leather armor.

'Somehow, it feels unfamiliar.'

I slung a bag over my back.

A sturdy bag made of quality leather.

A common bag with no enchantments, capable of holding only as much as its volume allowed.

I filled the bag with various useful consumables, but I didn't pack anything particularly expensive.

Just the kind of setup a well-prepared Third Floor Explorer would have.

Leather boots on my feet.

Three mid-grade potions on my belt.

AndГўВҖВҰ

"I don't like swords. I'll take this."

Instead of the longsword my companions recommended, I picked up a mace and rose from my seat.

Of course, though I called it a mace, it wasn't one of those monstrous and hideous weapons used by the blunt weapon warriors of the upper floors.

It was shaped like a club, with only the end being a bit blunt.

Whoosh, whoosh.

Swinging it in the air a few times, it didn't feel too bad to use.

"How is it? Is it uncomfortable to move in?"

"Ah, it's perfect. Leather armor is pretty good in its own way."

After also grabbing a wooden round shield, a compromise made considering the weight, I stood before the mirror and saw a rather sharp-looking figure.

The very image of a Lv1 Warrior, so to speak.

It felt like I could shatter a Goblin's head the moment I smashed it.

Ah, of course, it would be difficult against a Death Fiend.

"I'm ready."

"Ah, thank you."

Following Armin out onto the deck, I saw a single rowboat hanging from a pulley.

As I got on, the pulley's rope moved, lowering the rowboat to the sea.

After it fully touched the water's surface, the rope was untied, and I began to row.

AndГўВҖВҰ

Splash, splash!

Leaving my companions behind and heading into the rough terrain alone in the rowboat, I felt a little tense.

I had spoken confidently, but now that I was trying to move a body whose shoulders ached just from rowing, my confidence was dwindling.

How well could I perform the role of a vanguard with this body?

"Captain, please be careful on your journey!"

"Don't worry! We'll take good care of Bjorn!"

Hearing my companions' cheers from afar, I let out a small laugh.

Right, what's with the lack of confidence before I've even started?

At worst, all I'd lose is the reuse time for the Outing Doll and a few cheap pieces of equipment I got from Paradise Island.

Splash, splash!

The rowboat carrying the Lv1 Warrior cut through the waters of the Sixth Floor's Great Sea.

'It's a bit much to call out the name of the Ancestral Spirit with this body, soГўВҖВҰ'

Remembering the backstory I'd given my alternate character, I slowly made the Sign of the Cross.

"The Star at Twilight shall guide usГўВҖВҰ was that it?"

Well, I'll get used to this if I use my alternate character often enough.

After all, our Lv1 Human Warrior, Peter, is a devout believer who grew up in the Reatlas Orphanage.

He entered this Labyrinth with the grand ambition of gaining the strength to eliminate all raiders and heretics.

It was while rowing and reminding myself of the backstory one more time.

Shhk.

The moment I felt a chill, as if I had passed through a Portal.

The character has entered a Special Zone.

Field Effect - Twin Sea is now active.

Somehow, the island that was in front of me had disappeared.

***

The destination was the Twin Islands.

But at some point, the island had vanished like a mirage.

No, it wasn't just the Twin Islands that had disappeared.

'The ships that were around are all gone too.'

It was very different from what I had expected right from the start, but I maintained my composure as much as possible and calmly analyzed the situation.

Just because I was in the body of a Lv1 Warrior didn't mean the judgment of Veteran Player Lee Hansoo had vanished.

Of course, my heart was still pounding fiercely.

ThumpГўВҖВ”!!

An unknown space with no information.

But it wasn't an unfamiliar situation.

Now, there were few things I didn't know in [Dungeon & Stone], but it hadn't been like that from the beginning.

Seventh Floor, Eighth Floor, Ninth Floor, Tenth Floor...

The game had so few players that there was hardly any proper information, so each floor was an unknown place, and I had cleared away the darkness of the unknown by throwing myself at it head-on.

It was just that there were still parts I hadn't managed to uncover.

Yes, that was all.

'Seeing as the ships have disappeared, I must have entered beyond the field, butГўВҖВҰ'

The problem was that the destination island had also disappeared.

So, what on earth happened?

'The island I saw from afar wasn't the main map itself, I suppose.'

Perhaps the Twin Islands seen from the outside merely served as an 'entrance'.

Approaching the island would grant entry, and then one would have to explore a vast space like a 'Rift'.

'So, only the Twin Islands disappeared, and the rest is the sameГўВҖВҰ?'

Looking around, I saw an island in the far distance.

The Island of Beginnings - Rymia.

However, before turning the prow in earnest, I thought it over one more time.

After all, there were predecessors who had come before.

They were predecessors with much better stats than Peter, the Lv1 Human Warrior.

But would it have been any different for them?

One hundred out of one hundred would have headed for Rymia, and ninety-nine out of a hundred were kicked out naked with their memories erased.

'In other words, there's something dangerous on that islandГўВҖВҰ'

Thinking about it that way, it was a truly malicious gimmick.

The original Rymia Island was a Safe Zone where no monsters appeared.

The perception that the island was safe was bound to be ingrained in one's mind.

'Since I came here to throw myself at it, it's right to move in a way that gathers as much information as possible, but...'

Still, my worries deepened.

If Peter were at least Lv 5, I might have gone to Rymia, but at Lv 1, it felt a bit burdensome.

There was a high probability I would meet a dead end before finding out anything on that island.

'Do I go for it, or do I find another route?'

Without wasting much time, I made a quick decision.

'First, let's look for another route.'

Yes, this was more efficient.

It wouldn't be too late to go to Rymia Island after that.

Splash, splash-!

Having made my decision, I rowed the oars and set out across the vast sea.

My destination was Parune Island, located to the west of Rymia Island.

Since it was an island close enough to be seen with the naked eye from the top of Rymia Island, it was safe to say it was the only other route I could choose at the moment.

It would take days of rowing in this rowboat to reach any other island.

'And it's uncertain if I can even find my way properly with just a compass.'

Of course, that didn't mean Parune Island was an easy place.

Putting aside the monsters that appeared there.

It was also true that it was quite far to row to in this tiny rowboat.

'Still, I'll get there eventuallyГўВҖВҰ'

The first step is always the most daunting before you take it.

I earnestly turned the prow and started rowing.

The body of Lv 1 Peter was so weak that just a few hours of rowing left him drenched in sweat, but I never let go of the oars.

In times like these, a positive mindset is most important.

Yes, in terms of time, it was better than digging a tunnel for over ten days.

In terms of danger, it couldn't compare to battles with monsters, and there wasn't even the pain that came from negative Pain Resistance.

Splash, splash!

To be honest, it was very, very hard.

But that was a good thing.

It meant there were fewer competitors who chose this route, right? They would have all come in on a boat as small as mine.

'One hundred out of a hundred would have just gone to Rymia Island.'

With such positive thoughts, I headed toward my destination in the slow, turtle-like rowboat.

All the while, I periodically checked Rymia Island to reset my direction, and I didn't just mindlessly row.

I consumed enough food and water to not collapse from exhaustion, and when my body reached its limit, I also took rests.

And as a resultГўВҖВҰ

'It took a little over 12 hours.'

I arrived at my destination, Parune Island, only after the dark night had fallen.

However, instead of entering the island right away, I dropped a makeshift anchor near the coast and waited until morning.

Parune Island was where insect-type monsters appeared.

Exploring at night with a torch would make me an easy target.

"Agh, my back..."

After spending a night on the rowboat, my whole body was sore when I woke up.

The muscle aches from rowing were one thing, but the sleeping arrangement itself was the problem.

If it were Bjorn Yandel's body, I would have woken up feeling refreshed even if I had slept on a bed of fire.

Being Lv 1 is really inconvenient in these small ways.

Splash, splash-

My body's condition wasn't great, but since morning had dawned, I cautiously approached the shore and moored the boat as planned.

But was it because the mace and shield were still unfamiliar in my hands?

Thump!!

The tension grew as I entered the island.

Parune Island was a field where only monsters of Eighth Rank or lower appeared, but it was still a threatening hunting ground for Lv 1 Peter.

Rustle, rustle-

Thanks to being fully focused, I was able to detect the presence behind me in time, and I hurriedly took a stance and turned around.

And at that moment.

Pounce-!

A crayfish-like monster the size of a large dog, which had been stealthily moving across the sandy beach, jumped and charged at me.

Sturep.

The lowest-level monster of the Ninth Rank, the same rank as a Goblin.

No matter how low my level had become, the battle itself wasn't difficult because of my experience.

Clack!

After blocking its outstretched pincer with my round shield, I smashed the top of its head between its eyes with the mace...

Crack!

As I crushed it with all my might, the Sturep vanished into light.

You have defeated a Sturep.

EXP +1

...

Why on earth does this feel so satisfying?

I didn't know, but surprisingly, an Essence appeared where the Sturep that had vanished into light had been.

"Huh."

Well, is this some kind of beginner welcome event?

I hadn't expected an Essence to drop from the first monster I killed with my alternate character, but I completely ignored it and only took the Magic Stone to commemorate the moment.

"Are you trying to turn me into some crappy build?"

I had to choose the Essences my alternate character would consume carefully.

This body couldn't undergo essence removal at the Temple.

'Should I keep going for now?'

The afterglow of my first battle with my alternate character lingered slightly, but I had to explore as much as possible before nightfall, so I hurriedly resumed my pace.

Leaving the coastline, into a jungle-like forest.

As I walked straight ahead, bugs the size of a human head flew at me.

An Eighth-Rank insect-type monster, Krungbee.

But these guys weren't a problem either.

Peter might be level 1, but his equipment was at least Third Floor level.

Buzzzzzzzzz!

Their poison stings couldn't penetrate my leather armor.

So, the only place I needed to be careful of was my face.

Thud!

I just had to mind the ones aiming for my face, pushing them away with my shield.

Fwoosh!!

Using a scroll I had prepared in advance, I applied a fire enchantment to my club.

Whoooooosh~!

With the mindset that I'd hit at least one of them, I swung it around haphazardly.

I got hit by a few poison stings in the process, butГўВҖВҰ

I could just take an antidote after the battle was over.

You have defeated a Krungbee.

EXP +2

You have defeated a Krungbee.

You have defeated a Krungbee.

You have defeated a KrungГўВҖВҰ

After about 15 minutes of such brawling, the swarm of Krungbee was annihilated.

But what was this?

Whoooooosh~!

From among the corpses of the Krungbee that had vanished into light, a brilliantly colored orb rose up.

'Another Essence dropped?'

Well, I did kill a lot of them this time, but...

Even so.

'Could it beГўВҖВҰ am I just lucky?'

Of course, I didn't consume the Essence, and this time, I coolly passed on the Magic Stone as well.

One commemorative Magic Stone was enough.

Unnecessarily increasing my load would only reduce the space in my bag.

"Let's keep going."

Having successfully finished the battle with the Krungbee, I resumed my exploration without any further hesitation.

In the process, I encountered several more swarms of Krungbee, butГўВҖВҰ

You have defeated a Krungbee.

You have defeated a Krungbee.

You have defeated a KrungГўВҖВҰ

Contrary to my worries, it was more manageable than I thought.

As I gained know-how, the battle time, which was initially 15 minutes, gradually shortened.

"Ah ah, even Eighth Rank monsters are no match for meГўВҖВҰ"

It was when my confidence was gradually building.

Thud.

As I was making my way through the jungle, I froze on the spot.

"Damn it."

No wonder my luck had been so good.

***

The Siren has cast [Coral Net].

***

There was no time to do anything.

The moment I encountered a Siren while walking through the jungle, a widely spread net was thrown at me, and I was slammed to the ground.

I struggled to break free somehow, but it was futile.

The more I struggled, the tighter the nets entangling my limbs became.

""

The tough yet slippery [Coral Net] couldn't be broken with a blunt club, and even the dagger I hastily pulled from my waist couldn't leave a scratch on it.

It was only natural.

A Siren was a Fifth Rank monster.

There was no way a level 1 Warrior could break free from a Fifth Rank skill.

"Ha..."

Still, it felt a bit unfair.

The Siren was a monster that only appeared on Parune Island when you did the offering event.

"No, alrightГўВҖВҰ to be honest, I did think it could appear."

But still, this timing was not right.

Just when I was starting to enjoy raising my alternate character.

"Agh, I should have just gone to Rymia Island."

Regret washed over me belatedly.

This wasn't the end I had worked so hard rowing for.

'So, in the end, it's game over without finding out anything usefulГўВҖВҰ'

It left a bitter taste in my mouth, but I decided to just accept reality.

Right, at least I found out that the island disappears when you enter.

For a level 1 Warrior, that's having done all one could.

[What on earth are you saying, Imperial.]

--- Chapter 887 ---

Chapter 887: The Twins (2)

Resistance was futile.

Of course it was.

There's a limit to what experience or gear can cover.

How was a Level 1 supposed to take down a Fifth Rank monster?

Now, if they had tried to kill me without a word, like any other monster, I would have at least tried to fight back...

'But they can talk...'

For some reason, the Siren was speaking the Ancient Language, and I understood it perfectly.

'I didn't know the language pack would apply to my Alternate Character.'

Anyway, this was good.

As long as they weren't beings with whom communication was impossible, there was hope.

They say the tongue is mightier than the sword and spear, after all.

'Maybe I can resolve this through conversationГўВҖВ”'

Just as that thought crossed my mind.

Thwack!

The Siren's spear shaft struck me in the temple.

In that moment, all strength drained from my body, and my mind went blank.

[Filthy Empire bastards.]

The SirenГўВҖВҷs murmur was drenched in loathing.

With that, I lost consciousness completely.

And thenГўВҖВҰ

...

Thud!

The Siren who had locked me up was just securing the lock on the door and leaving.

I didn't bother trying to start a conversation.

After all, locking me in a cell meant they didn't plan on killing me right away.

He had even said something to that effect.

'Don't even think about dying peacefully until you've spilled your plans, he'd said...?

Since it had come to this, I decided it was better to assess the situation before rashly trying to talk.

ГўВҖВңAgh, my whole body achesГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

The floor was just damp, muddy earth, and I didn't want my bare skin touching it.

They won't stop their wicked ways until IГўВҖВҷm soaked.

And that was it.

There was too little information.

ГўВҖВңHey, can you hear me?ГўВҖВқ

A small, whispering voice came from the side.

Surprisingly, it was someone with whom I had a small connection.

Of course, he had no idea that connection even existed.

ГўВҖВңThis is Mike of the Raghart Clan. Which clan are you from, and what's your name?"

The Raghart Clan.

That was the clan that had willingly shared all that information with me before I came here.

They said one of their members who went in was still alive and holding on.

I never thought I'd meet him here.

Ah, I should introduce myself too.

"Peter, a warrior from the Hook Clan."

"The Hook Clan...? Never heard of them... Well, I guess that doesn't matter. I'm just glad you're a warrior."

"Glad?"

"... It's better you don't know for now."

He seemed to be brushing it off, but I didn't press further.

Once we had identified each other, Mike got straight to the point.

"Is there any way to get out of here?"

"No, I'm still looking."

"Right, you haven't been captured for long..."

"Does that mean you have a way to escape?"

"No, not yet. But what's certain is that we need to work together. If we want to get back to our families alive, away from these horrible monsters."

"... I don't really have a family."

Lv1 Warrior Peter was a hopeful orphan raised in the Reatlas Orphanage.

"... Still, you must have companions."

"I do."

"What do you say, will you work with me? Of course, you'll have to follow my lead. I know more than you do."

"'May the Star at Twilight have guided you...' Fine, I'll follow your lead."

"A believer of Reatlas?"

"Yes."

Having learned a little more about each other, Mike and I began to share information in earnest.

Well, to be honest, it was more like Mike leading the conversation one-sidedly.

"This island is home to a settlement of various monsters. The main ones are the Fifth Rank Sirens, and I've even seen a Fourth Rank Queen."

"Oh."

"They took your gear too, right?"

"Yes."

"I was dragged all over the place, and I saw that the confiscated gear is stored in a warehouse in the center of the settlement."

"It would be best if we could grab it on our way out, but we shouldn't push our luck. Our top priority is survival."

"Oh."

"Ah, and... how did you get to this island? Did you come by boat?"

"... That's a relief, then."

Mike paused, as if to gather his thoughts, and now it was my turn to ask questions.

"It sounds like you don't have a boat. How did you end up on this island?"

"It's a long story. And I don't want to talk about it."

Oh? Hiding information now?

"Aren't we supposed to be in the same boat?"

"..."

"If you hide things from me, I can't cooperate with you either."

When I spoke so bluntly, he reacted with a start, like someone whose painful wound had just been prodded.

"Dammit. They're all dead, everyone but me...!"

Ah, so that's why he was so intense.

After a moment of thought, I told him the truth about this island.

"You don't actually die here. You just get kicked out after they take your gear and memories...?"

"That's right. Your companions are fine outside. In fact, it was the leader of your clan who told me this."

"No way...!"

His voice, unable to contain its shock, was laced with elation.

But are humans ever such simple creatures?

The complete opposite emotion was also present.

"Th-then what was I...! Shit, after everything I endured...!"

Rage and sorrow.

Wondering what he meant, I asked for more details, and he shouted as if he could die from the injustice.

"These crazy monsters torture people!"

...

***

My conversation with Mike resumed only after some time had passed and he had calmed down.

I continued to ask questions to gather information, and he answered them faithfully.

However, for some reason, the passion in his voice was gone, replaced by an emptiness.

Like a person who had let go of something important.

Well, that's not to say the information was bad.

"When we first arrived here, there were more than ten teams of explorers in the sea. They naturally steered their ships towards Rymia, and my team followed them."

Just as I expected, the explorers chosen as the vanguard had headed for Rymia Island, which was immediately visible to them.

And...

"That island, which should have been completely devoid of monsters, was teeming with unidentified creatures."

Unidentified creatures.

In other words, monsters that had never been discovered before were occupying Rymia Island.

"They were all humanoid monsters. They used an incomprehensible language system, just like the monsters I've encountered on this island... Each and every one of them seemed to be a mutant, possessing unique abilities."

"Some used magic-like Supernatural Abilities, while others imbued their arrows with some kind of energy. There was even one that used Aura. It was heavily armed, just like the knights we're familiar with."

Humanoid monsters...

Sensing something here, I asked for more details about their attire and soon came to a conclusion.

'They're Empire soldiers.'

Rymia Island is occupied by Empire soldiers.

And putting the keywords together, it seemed the Sirens of Parune Island were hostile towards those Empire soldiers.

"Before we even made it past the island's entrance, we all had a gut feeling. We couldn't possibly conquer it with our current strength. So we got back on our boats and fled the island as if our lives depended on it."

"But to our surprise, those unidentified creatures even had warships. They quickly pursued us, and we all lost our ships to the relentless barrage of their mana cannons."

"And... when I came to, I was on this island. The last thing I remember is clinging to a plank and tying myself to it. It seems the current carried me all the way there."

The rest of the story was easy enough to guess.

Mike had been exploring Parune Island alone when he encountered a Siren, just like me, and was captured, then thrown into this prison.

Since then, he had been subjected to an incredible level of torture every day.

All while holding onto the hope that someone would come to rescue him.

"But to think all that suffering was for nothing...! To think I'd just lose my gear and be done with it if I died!"

"Uh..."

Wait a second.

The way this guy is talking is a little strange.

"Mike? You seem really worked up. Maybe you should calm down a bitГўВҖВ”"

"Don't worry! I'm calmer than I've ever been! Thanks for the news. Well then, I'll be making my escape first!"

As soon as he said that, a horrifying sound came from the wall where Mike was.

Thud!

Thud!

It was the sound of something being crushed against the wall.

It didn't take long to figure out what was happening.

Mike was bashing his head against the wall!

And he kept going, tirelessly.

Thud!

Thud!

Thud!!!

Seriously, why is his mental state so weak?

"Mike, get a grip and listen to me! Dying like that is meaningless! If you die here, you don't just lose your gear, you lose your memories too!"

"Do you want all the information you gained through all that suffering to be for nothing?"

I tried to persuade him calmly and logically, but unfortunately, Mike wasn't listening at all.

No, to be precise...

"Memories? Having these kinds of memories won't help me live! Shit, just seeing a Siren makes my hands tremble now!"

Perhaps the fact that he could shake off all the bad memories gave him even more strength.

Mike, who had seemed to be taking a break, started bashing his head against the wall again.

And then...

Crack!

After one last, unusually loud sound, not a single noise came from the next cell.

Is he really dead?

By hitting his own head?

'Sigh... what kind of guy is he?'

It was absurd.

I didn't know whether to call his mental state weak or the opposite.

With that kind of willpower, wouldn't it be better to just try and find a way to live?

The thought was still absurd, but I put an end to my musings about Mike.

The commotion Mike made by dying had brought a visitor.

Splish.

A sound unlike human footsteps, damp and alien.

A Siren passed by my cell, then opened the iron bars of the cell next door and stepped inside.

And then...

[Did he knock himself out by hitting the wall? I'll never understand these Empire folk.]

From his muttering, it seemed Mike was still alive.

I was surprised at first, but on second thought, it made sense.

Right, it couldn't be that easy to die just by hitting a wall by yourself.

Screech, thud!

Her business finished, the Siren closed the iron bars of Mike's cell and then opened the bars to mine.

[Follow me, Empire filth.]

Hmm, is he going to take me to the torture chamber now?

[I am not an Empire soldier.]

[So you can speak? The other one kept spouting an indecipherable code and refused to talk.]

[Anyway, I'm not an Empire soldier, so there's no need to torture me.

I just washed up on this island after being shipwrecked.

If you let me go, I'll leave this island immediatelyГўВҖВ”]

[Heh heh, that's a funny one.]

[No, I really have no connection to the EmpireГўВҖВ”]

[Enough, follow me!]

The Siren paid no attention to my words and dragged me out of the cell.

It wasn't far.

After going down one more flight of stairs, there was a place that screamed 'torture chamber'.

[High Priest, this is the one we caught this morning.

He looks weak-willed, so he should spill his wicked secrets with just a little touch.

Unlike the last one, who just kept spouting nonsense!]

The Siren then bound me to a torture chair and left.

Agh, this is creepy.

[Look, ask me anything. I'll tell you everything I knowГўВҖВ”]

[Questions come later.]

...

[... Huh?]

Thump-!

Now I could understand how Mike felt.

With a bastard like this mercilessly torturing him, it was no wonder Mike had lost his mind.

ThumpГўВҖВ”!

A torture so harsh that it would make one think it's better to just bash their head against a wall and commit suicide.

Screak.

And then...

Pluck!

...the sensation of it being pulled out.

"?"

[?]

Pluck!

A part of my body was ripped away.

But once again, I only flinched slightly, not letting out a scream.

It hurt like hell.

But it was no more and no less than the pain of having a fingernail pulled out.

I only felt that exact amount of pain.

"Oh......"

"... I think I can do this all day."
