--- Chapter 888 ---

Chapter 888: The Twins (3)

Unexpectedly, the torture did not last long.

Was it when he brought the steel tongs to my fifth finger?

The technician suddenly put the tongs down.

[Keva has no eye for people.

To call a man like this weak-hearted...]

For some reason, his voice was filled with a sense of self-loathing that went beyond emptiness.

I felt sorry for him, a man who had likely honed his skills his entire life, but it couldn't be helped.

'Tongs? Really, tongsГўВҖВҰ'

It was a bit too classic, if you could call it that.

It was good to stick to the basics, but that was all it was.

'Right, it's not like they're dipping my whole body in a carefully heated bath of acidic solution and potions, is it?'

I came to a new realization.

My pain threshold, which had already been pushed to its limits from constantly living on potions, had now soared far beyond those limits after I consumed the Essence of Tears.

Thump, thump.

Soon, the technician set down the tongs and picked up a bottle filled with a mysterious solution.

Is this the next torture device after the tongs?

Judging by what was in the bottle, it was highly likely to be an acidic solution.

Therefore, I should probably tense up a bit now.

Swoosh-!

Huh?

For some reason I didn't understand, when the technician poured the solution on my wounds, the bleeding stopped, and new flesh and nails grew back.

'A potion...?'

'A Potion.'

For what reason?

Unlike a real potion, there was no particular pain as I recovered.

As if sensing the question in my expression, the technician spoke bluntly.

[I have no hobby of wasting time.]

Ah, so physical methods didn't work, and now he intends to interrogate me through conversation?

That was a welcome development for me.

After all, it was a fact that it still hurt, and conversation was one of my strong suits.

[The Empire has created something ridiculous.

A monster that doesn't even blink when its fingernails are pulled out.]

Who is this fish-head calling a monster?

Most importantly, he was wrong about the most crucial part.

[As I've said before, I have nothing to do with the Empire.]

[What an amusing thing to say.]

As expected, he wasn't listening to me at all.

Well, it wasn't as if I had no other methods to try.

[If it's the Empire, there must be an Emperor, right? What's his name?]

The fish-head did not answer my question.

[Looks like you don't know his name either.]

It was a slight deviation from my original plan, but not a significant problem.

It would be nice to know the name, but the Emperor's name wasn't the key point.

I spoke with more seriousness than ever before.

[The Emperor is a son of a bitch.]

[The Emperor is an idiot, the Emperor is trash, a moron with a head full of shit, and a high probability of having no mother or father.]

As I rattled off the insults like a machine gun, the technician stared at me blankly.

He seemed to have no idea what was happening.

Before I lost the opportune moment, I quickly continued.

[Ah, Earth Mother! The light of my life! The flame of my existence! My sin! My soul! The Earth Mother is the best! Always new, always thrilling!]

It was the most intuitive proof of my beliefs.

At this, the technician fell into a state of confusion.

[What is this? D-don't tell me... are you a Pilgrim?]

Affirming it here wouldn't be bad, but it was my principle not to tell lies that would be easily found out, so I shook my head.

[No, that's not it.]

[But you just insulted the Emperor andГўВҖВ”.]

[That's because I'm not an imperial.]

It was a claim I had repeated several times, but perhaps the feeling it conveyed was different now.

The technician could no longer dismiss it as nonsense.

[My praise for the Earth Mother was just to prove my point.

An imperial would never be able to say such things.

Isn't that right?]

[That is true, but...]

[If you need more proof, I'll provide it anytime.

Bring any imperial to me.

I'll smash their head in right now to prove my purity.]

At my bold demand, the technician looked at me for a moment as if to organize his thoughts.

And...

[... Wait here for a moment.]

With those words, he left for about an hour before returning and leading me to the surface.

It was then that I saw their settlement with my own eyes for the first time.

This place was not a simple Siren village.

Ranemut Warriors.

Sea Giants.

Nagas, and so on.

Various sea-faring tribes lived here, and there was also a chief who led the group.

[Are you the imperial who insulted the Emperor and praised the Earth Mother?]

The Siren Queen.

A Rank 4 high-level monster that a Level 1 warrior like Peter could not even hope to scratch.

However, I was not intimidated in the slightest.

And why would I be? From a Level 1's perspective, there was no difference between a Siren and a Siren Queen.

They were both a one-hit kill.

Ah, of course, with me on the receiving end.

[I did insult the Emperor and praise the Earth Mother, but I am not an imperial.

How many times do I have to say it?]

[...

Hehe, what a bold human.]

The queen, holding a trident of intimidating size, laughed as if amused, then suddenly her expression hardened as she slammed the shaft of her weapon on the ground.

As if on cue, one of the Sirens lined up nearby promptly dropped a bundle in front of me.

Seeing the bundle struggle the moment it hit the ground, it wasn't hard to guess what was inside.

[Mmph! Mmph!]

Soon, the Siren who had set down the bundle untied the rope that bound it, and the person trapped inside was finally revealed.

[An Imperial Knight.

He is important enough that the imperial forces who occupied Rymia Island have tried to negotiate for him several times.]

The queen did not speak any further.

[Prove it.]

With those words, she clamped her mouth shut, and as if waiting, a Siren dropped a weapon in front of me.

It was the mace I had been carrying.

It was all too clear what they wanted from me, so I had no reason to hesitate.

Shhh.

Without a word, I picked up the mace and approached the terrified Imperial Knight.

The Sirens watching me sent gazes that were half in doubt, wondering if I could really go through with it.

Hah, as if this is some difficult task.

WHACK!

Without a second of hesitation, I slammed the mace into the knight's head, and the Sirens' eyes widened.

[He really did it...!]

[Was it true that he wasn't an imperial?]

[Quiet.]

The atmosphere, which was about to become disorderly, turned silent again with a single word from the queen.

She must have thought it wasn't time to judge yet.

After all, I had only struck once, and the knight was still alive.

Therefore...

WHACK! WHACK! WHACK!

I brought the mace down again and again.

It was an ordinary strike, with none of the buffs from a skill like Swing.

The Imperial Knight's durability seemed to be quite high, as it took a considerable amount of time to get a kill confirmation with just basic attacks, even though he was incapacitated and I was aiming for his head, a vital spot.

CRACKГўВҖВ”!

Only after striking the mace more than ten times did I feel that distinct sensation in my hands.

That alone was enough to instinctively know I had finished him, but even greater proof soon unfolded before my eyes.

Swoooosh.

The Imperial Knight turned into a halo of light and disappeared.

You have defeated the Imperial Knight.

EXP +6

At the same time, my heart vibrated, and a familiar energy coursed through my body.

An explosive sense of fulfillment, as if my blood vessels were expanding.

The character's level has increased.

Soul Power has increased by +10.

The maximum number of absorbable Essences has increased by +1.

Proving I'm not an imperial even gives me experience points?

[It's not enough.]

I frowned and shook my head.

[I'll prove it more, so bring me more! Regular soldiers or archers would be even better than knights!]

If I could just reach Level 3, that would be great.

***

Silence fell over the clearing.

As the sea monsters stared at me with wide eyes, the Siren Queen brought the situation to a close.

[... That's enough. The proof is complete. I acknowledge it, you are not an imperial.]

Right, so there would be no more freebies.

Feeling a sense of disappointment for some reason, I waited for the queen to continue.

[Child of man, do you have any thought of devoting yourself to the Earth Mother? If it's you, I am willing to personally bestow a blessing.]

[What is this blessing?]

[It means you can become one of us, going beyond a simple Pilgrim.]

[Does that mean I'll become a Siren too?]

[Well, that is unknown. The blessing will be bestowed according to the size of your soul.]

The size of my soul...

I didn't know for sure, but it seemed to mean it was proportional to my level.

If I were Level 3, I would probably transform into something like a Rank 7 monster, a Ranemut Warrior.

There was no need to hesitate.

[In that case, I refuse.]

A puppet transforming into a monster would have its uses at certain moments, but honestly, I thought it would be more useful to remain human.

[... If that is your will, I will not insist further.]

Surprisingly, when I refused the offer, the queen coolly accepted it and did not press the matter.

So, that was the end of the proselytizing.

[Then what happens to me now? I've proven that I'm not an imperial.]

[Nothing.]

[I would appreciate it if you could be more specific.]

[You are free.

We are different from the Empire.

We have no intention of harming those who are not involved in this war.]

Phew...

So I've survived for now, then...

As I was sighing in relief inwardly, the queen spoke.

[You may leave the island as you are, or you may stay and observe our way of life.

It would be even better if you find it in your heart to devote yourself to the Earth Mother.]

[So, what will you do? Child of man.]

Well, my decision was already made.

***

A week had already passed since I first set foot on this island as part of the vanguard.

And I was still on Parune Island.

However, it was different from before when I was a prisoner locked in a cell.

Now I could roam freely in this village, and...

[Waaaaah! Peter! Peter!]

[Do that thing! BAHELLAAAAAAAA!!]

I had become quite popular.

So much so that when I was just taking a walk, little sea monsters would cling to me, begging me to play.

The little ones found me, a human, very fascinating.

So I played well with them to curry favor, and naturally, the adults came to see me in a good light as well.

Yesterday, we even went hunting together.

You have defeated a Krungbee.

You have defeated a Krungbee.

You have defeated a Krungbee...

There was something I learned while hunting and catching bugs.

When the island's residents kill a monster, it doesn't disappear in a flash of light.

However, even when they saw a monster I killed disappear in a flash of light, they didn't think it was strange at all.

It was as if they were hypnotized.

They didn't harbor any questions whatsoever.

'Should I consider this a game mechanic...?'

From my perspective, it caused a bit of cognitive dissonance, but I decided not to think too deeply about it.

It wasn't like there were only one or two things in this Labyrinth that I couldn't understand.

[Peter, aren't you lonely? You're the only human left on this island now.]

[Not really.]

My prison mate, Mike, was dead.

Ah, of course, he wasn't executed by the island's residents.

Mike was released due to my active insistence after I became friendly with the villagers, but he himself actively wished for death.

Something about wanting to erase the memory of being tortured?

His will was so strong that in the end, unable to watch any longer, I sent him off cleanly with a hammer.

'He's probably laughing with his comrades now, having forgotten everything.'

At first, I didn't understand, but now I think Mike's choice might have been quite wise.

If it's a memory that will torment you for a lifetime, then forgetting it might be the path closer to happiness.

Anyway, I was enjoying my life on Parune Island and working hard to gain the residents' favor, and perhaps my efforts paid off.

[Peter, the queen calls for you.]

For the first time since the day I killed the Imperial Knight, the queen had summoned me.

And it was even a private meeting, with all her surrounding retainers dismissed.

[Before we begin our talk, I will say this first, child of man.

I am going to ask a favor of you today, and whether you grant this favor or not is entirely up to your will.

I will not coerce you in any way, nor will I give you any disadvantages, such as kicking you off this island, for refusing.

Such things are only done in the evil Empire.]

To put it simply, she was giving me a quest...

I didn't know how to interpret the long preamble she gave before even stating the quest's content.

When she emphasized it that much, I wondered if it was reverse psychology.

'Does she mean she'll kick me off the island if I refuse?'

Though such a reasonable suspicion arose, I decided to first hear everything the queen had to say.

[Furthermore, if you grant my request, I promise in the name of the Earth Mother that I will bestow a corresponding reward.]

[Alright, just tell me.

What's the favor?]

[Go to the island occupied by the imperials and find a child.

Then, grant peace to that suffering child.]

An assassination quest given to a Level 2 warrior.

I wanted to ask if she had mistaken my class, but...

[First, I'd like to hear who that child is.]

I decided to first confirm the rest of the quest's content, and as a result, I heard a surprising name.

'Hmm, on second thought, is it really that surprising?'

I had actually predicted to some extent that this island would be related to that person.

[Tetrasea, please grant peace to that poor child.]

--- Chapter 889 ---

Chapter 889: The Twins (4)

Tetrasea.

The moment I heard that name, I knew it instinctively.

That this quest the Queen was giving me was closely related to conquering this hidden island.

ГўВҖВҳIf I succeed in the assassination and give it rest or whatever, is that the clear condition?ГўВҖВҷ

Hmm, I thought that might be it... but on the other hand, I wondered if it would really be that simple.

It wasn't something I could be sure of yet.

It was unclear if coming to Parune Island to build favor and get this quest, instead of going to Rymia Island, was even the correct path.

ГўВҖВҳEven if I had gone to Rymia Island, as long as I knew the Ancient Language, I feel like I could have progressed somehow...ГўВҖВҷ

It's a common type of scenario in games.

Choosing a faction and carrying out quests suited to that faction.

[I want to hear more details before I decide.]

Anyway, now that the name Tetrasea had come up, I needed to know more about the current situation.

[What are you curious about?]

[Everything. Starting with what kind of situation Tetrasea is in.]

[... Very well.]

Was it because she was the one giving me the quest?

The Queen answered my questions faithfully, even though they weren't directly related to the assassination mission itself.

[Do you know about the treaty we made with the Empire soldiers?]

[I heard from the residents. You promised not to invade each other's territory, right?]

[That treaty came with a price.

We had to give them the Bloodline of the Idol.]

[What did you receive in return?]

[The daily lives of our people.]

From what I heard, the treaty didn't seem like a ceasefire agreement made between equals.

It was more like a typical unequal treaty signed by a defeated nation.

Parune Island gave them what they wanted, and in return, they were promised survival.

ГўВҖВҳFirst, the Bloodline of the Idol seems to refer to Tetrasea...ГўВҖВҷ

[What does 'the Idol' refer to?]

The one who commands the storm...

Considering I'm on Parune Island, I think I have an idea.

[Is it someone higher than the Queen? I've never seen them.]

[That one no longer stays on this island.

That, too, was part of the treaty.]

ГўВҖВҳStormgush...ГўВҖВҷ

In other words, Tetrasea, the Bloodline of the Idol, meant baby Stormgush.

And once I reached that interpretation, various other hypotheses began to form.

Like the event that occurred during the Battle of the Lord of Tears.

I only considered things like the Crown of Anguish as activation conditions, but maybe the Essence of Stormgush had an effect too.

Well, it doesn't matter now.

[But in this situation, is it okay to kill the Bloodline of the Idol, which was the price of the treaty?]

[Because you are human.

Even if you kill him and grant him rest, the Empire soldiers will not be able to connect it to us.]

Ah... so that's why they're having me do it instead of doing it themselves.

If I, in human form, kill Tetrasea, it will just be a failure on the part of the Empire soldiers.

There's no reason for Parune Island to take responsibility.

No, they might even be able to make various demands, citing poor management.

[Do you know anything about the target's situation, or where they are?]

[... I do not know.]

[So you mean I have to find all that out myself.]

The Queen said she would provide me with the uniform worn by the Empire soldiers, and that wearing it would lower the risk of being caught.

But that was something I'd only know after trying.

As far as I was concerned, it was a mission so dangerous that even several lives of Peter, the Level 2 Warrior, wouldn't be enough.

In other words...

[The reward.]

[?]

[You said you would give a corresponding reward.

I'd like to hear about it.]

I needed a reward great enough to be worth sacrificing several lives of Peter, the Level 2 Warrior.

***

Hah, I didn't think I'd be doing this again.

Splash, splash-!

I am rowing.

The destination is Rymia, the Island of Beginnings.

It hasn't been long since I left Parune Island, and my shoulders are already stiff...

Splash, splash-!

As tired as my body was, the rowboat I came in cut through the waves swiftly.

Of course, this was my second time.

I was more skilled than the first time, so the boat moved straight ahead without losing its direction.

Hmm, at this rate, I think I can arrive 1.5 times faster than when I came...

ГўВҖВңHooo...ГўВҖВқ

As my body quickly grew tired from the strenuous rowing, I continued my thoughts.

From the plan to infiltrate Rymia Island, which was full of Empire soldiers, to the reward the Queen had offered...

ГўВҖВҳThe Queen's Engraving...?ГўВҖВҷ

It was what the Queen had offered as the quest reward.

She had suggested a few things before, but when I remained silent as if unsatisfied, she had offered it with her eyes squeezed shut, as if she really couldn't offer anything more.

So I accepted it at that point, but I still don't know its exact use.

ГўВҖВҳFrom the rough explanation, it sounds like a permanent buff.ГўВҖВҷ

It doesn't turn you into a monster like the blessing you get from devoting yourself to the Earth Mother, but maybe it lets you use a part of her power?

ГўВҖВҳIf it's the Queen's Engraving, it probably grants one of the abilities the Siren Queen has...ГўВҖВҷ

No matter how I think about it, it doesn't sound like a simple stat-increasing Engraving.

But if it really gives a skill, that wouldn't make sense.

Since an Engraving doesn't seem to use an Essence slot.

Or maybe there's a restriction, like you can only receive an Engraving once?

ГўВҖВҳIf that's the case, getting the Queen's Engraving would be a loss...ГўВҖВҷ

Hmm, then I should probably receive that reward as Peter.

Or maybe it would be better to have my companions receive it first.

It's a bit risky to experiment with my main character.

ГўВҖВңOh.ГўВҖВқ

As I continued rowing while lost in thought, Rymia Island finally came into view.

At this point, I stopped rowing for a moment, changed into the Imperial uniform the Queen had prepared for me, and checked the identification badge.

[Naval Special Forces Division]

[2-17CHCH]

The top two lines were the affiliation.

[Private Soldier]

This seemed to be the rank.

And at the very bottom was the name I would be using for the duration of the assassination quest...

ГўВҖВңUh...?ГўВҖВқ

[Hans Elfire]

You've got to be kidding me.

Whoooosh, splash!

The moment I saw the name, I reflexively threw it into the sea.

ГўВҖВңAh...ГўВҖВқ

I messed up.

The plan will fall apart without the identification badge.

I felt a slight regret a moment later, but...

ГўВҖВҳNo, it couldn't be helped.ГўВҖВҷ

Right, dealing with the 'Outing Doll' alone is tough enough, but having to be called by that name too?

There's no way the quest would succeed.

ГўВҖВҳYes, this is better.ГўВҖВҷ

Besides, the plan to use the existing identity had many uncertain parts anyway.

For example, if I ran into someone who knew the original owner, it would be over.

Well, the Queen had assured me that since the unit had been wiped out, no one would recognize me.

ГўВҖВҳAnyway, with this, the story of being a soldier who escaped Parune Island is ruined...ГўВҖВҷ

After organizing my thoughts for a moment, I tore off all the insignias on the uniform.

I figured that without an identification badge, they would only get in the way.

ГўВҖВҳSince it has come to this, let's go with a classic...ГўВҖВҷ

The rolling waves.

The distant shore.

The plan is simple.

From now on, I will abandon this rowboat and swim to the coast.

Then, I'll lie down by the waves pretending to be unconscious, and when an Empire soldier finds me...

ГўВҖВҳWhere am I? Who am I?ГўВҖВҷ

I'll go with the amnesiac Empire soldier setting.

It seems like a very flimsy plan, but I know.

Sometimes, these simple plans work better.

Besides, this seems to have a much higher chance of success than infiltrating with that cursed name.

ГўВҖВҳBy the way, with this setting, it would be good to have a head wound.ГўВҖВҷ

Details are the life of an performance.

With that thought, I struck my own head with a club without any hesitation.

However...

Thwack-!

Was the problem that I had no hesitation?

Or was it that I momentarily forgot this body was Level 2?

ГўВҖВңUgh...ГўВҖВқ

It was probably both.

ГўВҖВңThe Hans effect...ГўВҖВқ

My vision started to spin, and my body lost all strength.

My legs gave out, my body swayed...

Splash!

And just like that, I lost consciousness and fell into the sea.

***

Someone kept slapping my cheek, so I slowly opened my eyes to see a man I'd never seen before looking down at me with an urgent expression.

[Hey! Wake up! Hey!]

What in the world is going on?

My vision was blurry, my head was dizzy, and I couldn't breathe well, so it took me quite some time to grasp the situation.

[Don't close your eyes! Think of the Empire! Think of your family!]

I didn't care about family, but hearing the keyword 'Empire' made the memories of what happened before unfold like a panorama.

ГўВҖВҳOh...ГўВҖВҷ

I tried to give myself a head wound, but I ended up knocking myself out and falling into the water.

But seeing an Empire soldier waking me up, it seemed I had luckily drifted to the shore as planned.

They say method acting is the pinnacle of acting.

Because of my mistake, I had completed a performance more realistic than any other.

[Where... is this?]

[It's Rymia Island!]

[Rymia Island...?]

I asked back blankly and brought a hand to my forehead, frowning.

Not because I was pretending to be in pain, but because I really felt it.

[Yes, Rymia Island! How did you end up in the sea? What's your affiliation?]

[... I don't know. M-my memory...]

Hmm, is this a bit much?

As I continued what I felt was a terrible performance, the Empire soldier before me nodded his head in understanding.

[Tsk, looks like you've seriously injured your head.

Come and support this man.

We're ending the patrol and returning to base!]

Ah, he wasn't alone?

I soon turned my head to the side and saw other soldiers.

[Squad leader, something's suspicious.]

[What is suspicious?]

[Lately, unidentified humans keep entering this island, don't they? Perhaps this man is one of them.

He's only wearing a uniform; there's no way to prove his identity, is there?]

At one soldier's suspicion, another soldier chimed in and nodded.

[Private Soldier Jake has a point! Amnesia? Does that even make sense?]

Ah...

I guess it's not working?

Arguing here wouldn't help me push the amnesia setting, so I silently watched the situation.

It was indeed the right decision.

Soon enough, the one called the squad leader began to actively defend me.

[Enough! This man is a soldier of our Empire. We must quickly take him to the base for treatment.]

[How can you not even be suspicious?]

[Because all the unidentified humans spoke an unknown language.

But this man spoke ours.]

[But that alone is not enough...!]

[According to the staff officers' speculation, it's highly likely they are monsters sent by the Pilgrims.

They actually used abilities that monsters use.]

Hmm, this seems to be referring to the power of Essences...

[But what about this man? I can't feel any of those monsters' powers from him.

This means he is one of the sons of our Empire.]

[... Just because he's not a monster doesn't mean we can be certain he's an ally.

We can't rule out the possibility that a Pilgrim stole a uniform and infiltrated.]

[Hmm, you're right, we can't rule out that possibility, but...]

At the soldier's words, the squad leader also looked at me with somewhat uncertain eyes.

He didn't seem to have any intention of killing me right away, but he seemed to be contemplating whether to treat me as a 100% ally or as an unidentified person.

[Squad leader! Would you take a look at this? This is something Rick found during the coastal patrol!]

A soldier handed an item to the squad leader.

My vision was still blurry and out of focus, but it still looked very familiar.

Soon, the squad leader, who had been closely examining the item, looked at me and smirked.

[It seems this is your Imperial Badge.]

[Imperial Badge...?]

[Yes, it seems it was swept away by the current and washed up on the shore, and we were lucky enough to find it.]

I couldn't help but look confused.

It wasn't because I was acting; I genuinely didn't understand.

[If it's the 2-17th Battalion, that's the unit that was wiped out in the last battle before the treaty.

It's good that you made it back alive.]

...

[I suppose we'll only hear the full story when your memory returns...

For now, go back and get some rest.]

The squad leader then smiled and patted my shoulder encouragingly.

[Private Soldier, Hans Elfire.]

It was utterly unbelievable.

Chapter 890: The Twins (5)

[Private Soldier, Hans Elfire.]

An unfamiliar ceiling.

I was currently lying in a medical tent at the Imperial ArmyГўВҖВҷs outpost on Rymia Island.

It was because I had just met with a Priest to receive treatment for amnesia.

The diagnosis was memory loss due to a traumatic brain injury.

Something about how I must have hit my head somewhere while being washed ashore by the waves?

The wound on my head was very helpful in getting this diagnosis, and the prescription was a shot of Heal.

Thanks to that, the injury on my head was now fully recovered.

However, I maintained the concept of amnesia, and the Priest didn't find it strange, saying it was common for aftereffects to remain permanently if the treatment window was missed.

[You are currently without an affiliation.

You will be reassigned soon...

Until then, get some good rest.]

It was a truly perfect disguised infiltration.

Well, there was still a problem that was far too threatening to me to be called minor.

[Do you understand? Hans.]

[Elfire.]

[?]

[Please call me by my last name.]

[Despite your appearance, you're quite a stiff fellow, aren't you?]

The middle-aged Priest looked at me drawing a line and left, seemingly a little offended.

And so, I was left alone in the empty medical tent.

According to the Priest, it would be so packed during battles that it would feel cramped, but lately, there had been no soldiers getting injured.

Apparently, until about a week ago, there had been quite a few injured soldiers due to monsters wearing human skin, but now they had all been discharged?

'From what I hear, it seems like no one else is entering anymore.'

I quietly calculated the time.

'Day 39 of entering the Labyrinth.'

And since the Sixth Floor closes on day 60...

'There are 21 days left, huh...'

Within 21 days, I had to find and kill Tetrasea, who was hidden on Rymia Island, which had become an Imperial ArmyГўВҖВҷs outpost.

***

A time limit of 21 days.

Considering I had to kill Tetrasea and then return to Parune Island to receive the reward, I would need a few more days to spare.

'Although, it would be impossible to kill him and escape this island as Level 2 Peter in the first place.'

So, I prioritized information gathering over completing the assassination quest.

I'd roughly check the difficulty of this island.

Once I confirmed where that bastard Tetrasea was, I would re-enter with my main character to finish the job.

That was my plan for now, but I still didn't know for sure.

I might discover something new while gathering information on this island.

According to my speculation, 'Twin Islands' was a mission-type Hidden Field, and I still didn't know exactly what that 'mission' was.

'It might not just end with a simple assassination and receiving a reward.'

Therefore, under the pretext of taking a 'walk', I wandered around the medical tent and gathered information in my own way.

After three days, the unit I would belong to was finally decided.

It was the result of actively appealing to the Priest that I had fully recovered.

[From now on, you are a member of the 1-16th Battalion.]

Surprisingly, the unit I was assigned to was the one that had rescued me when I washed up on the coast.

[It will be tough for now since you have no memories, but try to adapt well.

Private Soldier, Elfire.]

Especially in the way he called me by my last name without me even asking.

Anyway.

Since the treaty, this island has found stability.

The current mission assigned to our battalion is just 'coastline patrol and guard duty'.]

And so, I began my life in the Imperial Army as Private Soldier Elfire of the 1-16th.

Morning, noon, and night.

I stood on patrol duty in shifts, and in the process, I tried my best to get close to the other soldiers.

Of course, the process wasn't exactly smooth.

There was too great of a wall between me and them.

[Hans.]

[...

I'm sorry, but I'd appreciate it if you'd call me by my last name.]

The sad fate of having to draw a line with the soldiers who started calling me by my first name, thinking we were getting close.

Ah, was this truly the heart of Hong Gildong?

Still, thankfully, the kind and comrade-filled Imperial soldiers warmly embraced me as I was.

[That Elfire fellow... doesn't it seem like he's strangely drawing a line between us?]

[Try to understand, his battalion was annihilated...]

[Even if he's lost his memories, I guess that wound etched in his heart can't be erased.]

[Ah... that's true! My thinking was shortsighted! To think I was upset over something like this! I'll work hard enough that one day he'll ask me to call him by his first name!]

When I accidentally overheard them talking amongst themselves, I even felt like changing factions and swearing loyalty to the Imperial Army.

Ah, of course, it was just a fleeting thought.

One must separate public and private matters.

One day, two days, three days, four days...

Even after becoming Private Soldier Elfire, time flowed quickly, and it was soon the 47th day of entering the Labyrinth.

Patrolling the coastline every day and building comradeship with my colleagues, I naturally came to learn many things, and with more knowledge came more questions.

[... Why on earth did we sign a treaty? Haha, this fellow is filled with hatred for those monster bastards, isn't he?]

[Well, I suppose those memories won't really disappear.

They must still remain in the deepest part of his heart.]

[By the way, it seems you've definitely lost your memories.

Seeing as you're asking such a question.]

The Imperial Army is incredibly strong.

Strong enough to wipe out all of Parune Island in a day.

'After all, a Stormgush is only a Third-Rank monster...'

But why did the Imperial Army only sign a 'treaty' and leave them alive?

It was a question that started from that doubt, but a name I hadn't expected at all suddenly appeared.

[It is because they are under the blessing of the Sea God of Calamity.]

The Sea God of Calamity, Leviathan.

A Rank 1 creature that can be encountered in the Sixth Floor, the Great Sea.

[If we were to go all the way, he would surely intervene, so we had to draw the line.]

[I see...]

[Still, we managed to obtain the most important thing without much damage, so it can be called a blessing for the Empire...

Ah, I'm sorry.

I misspoke.]

Without much damage.

Upon using such an expression in front of me, fellow soldier A wore a look of regret, but to me, who wasn't the real Elfire, it was of no concern.

The truly important part was something else.

[Obtained something important? What is it? I heard you took some monster as a hostage, is it that important?]

At my question, soldier A made a troubled expression.

But perhaps because I'd been building goodwill, and my story weighed on his mind?

[... Alright, I suppose you have a right to know.

But you must not tell anyone about this.

Understand?]

[I swear on my heart.]

[My younger brother is in the research department.

Unlike me, he was very smart since he was young.

I heard from him... that the Empire is currently developing a new War Machine using that monster they took hostage.

He said if it succeeds, it will be an invention great enough to end this dreadful war.]

What, is it something like a nuclear bomb?

[So don't overdo it.

If you just wait patiently like this, the great Empire will fulfill your wish.]

I had no idea what kind of War Machine it was, but I ended the conversation there.

I judged that digging deeper wouldn't yield any proper clues, and I might just arouse suspicion for no reason.

'I should probably bring my main character in around now.'

I succeeded in infiltrating as an Imperial soldier, but information gathering wasn't going as well as I thought.

I guess this was the limit of a regular soldier.

The warehouse where the equipment of the explorers who challenged Twin Islands was stored, the structure of the Imperial Army's units, patrol routes, and so on.

That was all I could find out.

The information I just got was quite high-level, but even that was a result I could only obtain because I was thoroughly lucky.

If there hadn't been a guy with a younger brother working in the research department in our unit, I would never have been able to find out.

'In the end, I couldn't even find out where Tetrasea is being held captive.'

Well, it's not that I have no idea where it might be.

In the center of the outpost, there is a restricted zone that not only regular soldiers but even officers cannot enter without permission.

Six.

I'd say.

Perhaps I was being overly cautious.

It felt a little anticlimactic.

A place I had given up on because it seemed impossible to get into as a regular soldier.

Chapter 891. The Twins (6)

The inside of the laboratory was very simple.

It was a square structure with a very high ceiling for a normal room.

However, in the center of the floor, there was a circular groove, like a hot spring...

ГўВҖВҳThey must have filled it with water.ГўВҖВҷ

The remaining traces of water told me what that circular groove was used for.

It was probably full just a moment ago.

Of course, it wouldn't have been ordinary water.

Well, it probably wasn't anything incredibly special either.

As someone who couldn't help but know more about Stormgush's abilities than anyone, I realized what it was as soon as I saw it.

ГўВҖВҳSeawater...ГўВҖВҷ

Stormgush's passive skill, [Origin].

This skill increases its Regeneration Ability when it touches seawater, and the longer the duration, the greater the increase.

ГўВҖВҳ...

Lee Hansoo becoming Bjorn Yandel.

ГўВҖВў [That... won't work.]

An explorer who was among the first to reach the Sixth Floor had his fingernails pulled out for a few days and sought to forget the memory through death.

Chapter 892. First Playthrough (1)

No way.

The Queen probably knew that too.

But the reason she still entrusted me with the request was... 'It must be because of guilt.'

Right, the Queen didn't give me the quest because she was desperate enough to grasp at straws.

I at least tried.

.

As if the entire building had been teleported into the middle of the ocean.

I, who had been frozen in a daze, came to my senses at the presence of people I felt from above.

--- Chapter 896 ---

Chapter 896. First Playthrough (5)

The very first thought that came to my mind when I came to my senses was quite clichГғВ©.

'I'm back...?'

I was back.

This could simply mean that I had returned to the outside, but it also held another meaning.

The mace and leather armor I had discarded into the sea while disguised as an Imperial soldier.

And...

Bobbing, bobbing.

Even the rowboat I had taken to the island for the first time.

What in the world was this?

Was it because I hadn't died, but had come out through a portal, so they were returning all my equipment?

'No, even so, they're returning everything I lost along the way?'

I guess I should call this a strange kind of kindness.

As I continued these thoughts, I froze.

"Ah."

I realized it belatedly, but my memories were intact.

In this case, however, there were two possibilities.

'Either, as I had hoped, the memories gained through the 'Outing Doll' were judged not to be erased...'

Or, I had succeeded in 'surviving' and came out through the portal, so they weren't erased.

I wasn't sure which one was correct.

Well, perhaps it was both.

I had felt it many times while playing the game, but the 'Outing Doll' was a very peculiar item.

Anyway, that was enough thought on that matter.

Swoosh.

I quickly looked around.

Many things had changed from when I first entered.

For one, the dozens of ships that had been anchored around the island were all gone.

Why?

I actually had a guess about this part too...

But it would be much faster and more accurate to hear it from them.

ShwaaaaaГўВҖВ”!

The only ship that was rapidly approaching me as soon as I regained consciousness on the rowboat.

My companions on the deck were pressed against the prow, waving their hands at me.

"BjorrrrrnГўВҖВ”!!"

"I told you not to use that name when Bjorn is in that form, meow!"

"Ugh... It's just us anyway! Stop nagging!"

Afterward, I was hauled up, rowboat and all, logged out of my alternate character, and opened my eyes in my main character.

And...

"I'm sorry."

First, I offered words of apology to my companions.

"It took longer than I thought. I didn't expect it to take this long."

"Haha, there's no need to apologize at all. Weren't you late because you were on a scouting mission?"

"But still, in the end, we gained almost nothing from this expedition, did we?"

The only reason explorers risk their lives on expeditions is for money.

But what about this expedition?

From the 'Nameless Fragment' to the Fifth Floor Rift, and then the Sixth FloorГўВҖВҷs Twin Islands.

We had wasted the entire expedition period.

While Misha and Ainar might not mind, I felt genuinely sorry to Armin's expedition and Bear Uncle.

But what part of my apology was so funny?

"Yandel, why are you so sensitive about such strange things?"

Bear Uncle laughed as if he was genuinely dumbfounded, then continued.

"Who here would care about something like that? You went to scout an unexplored region alone, taking on all the risks. We just sat back and reaped the benefits."

"Benefits? Huh? I didn't see much benefit."

Ainar mumbled something, but no one replied.

They just continued the conversation as if it were natural.

"Yes. If you could have gold tomorrow instead of bread today, who would choose the bread?"

"You guys! What are you talking about! Let me in on it too!"

"Well... I suppose that's true."

As I nodded, the navigator, who had been cautiously observing, added his words.

"Haha, as for me! I don't think I've ever had such a comfortable expedition in my life!"

Right, this guy must have had it easy.

He just rested on the Fifth Floor and the Sixth Floor.

Ugh, my stomach hurts from envy.

Is this what it feels like to be the boss?

Just as I was starting to regret the meaninglessly spent employment fee, Bear Uncle and Armin approached with gleaming eyes.

"So what happened?"

"Yes. Seeing that your equipment is intact, it seems you haven't lost your memories either."

"..."

"Let's go inside. We should eat something while we talk."

It seemed like it would be a long story.

***

As I ate the meal filled with Misha's care, I recounted the stories of what happened inside, and with each tale, my companions' expressions gradually changed.

Going to Parune Island instead of Rymia Island.

The monster village I encountered on that island.

It was as if they were looking at someone who was sick.

My life as an Imperial soldier.

The research base I arrived at after being taken as a test subject.

"A guy who was locked up naked interrogated and killed the guy who was pretty much the boss there? And there was even a knight...? With a body weaker than a Ninth-Rank Explorer?"

"No, as I said before, it wasn't that difficult."

"Phew... Bjorn, it's a good thing you became a warrior! Not an assassin."

"..."

Lord of the Sea, Leviathan, and the 'senior researcher' who faced him, and the First-Class soldier 'Berzak' who watched the battle.

"You've really been through a lot...!"

'It's only natural that it took a long time.

No, wait... is that even possible? A normal person without a single Essence?'

Anyway, the tale of Peter's great escape came to an end like that.

Oh, by the way, the navigator was not present at this time.

I felt bad for excluding him, but there was no reason to expose this information to an outsider.

He didn't seem offended, as if he was used to such things.

"So what happened here? The island was gone when I came out, is that why all the other explorers left?"

"Yes, that's right. On the 40th day, the island suddenly disappeared. Most of them left then, and after a few more days, even those who were lingering around in hopes of something all left."

"Hmm, so it seems entry closes after the 40th day..."

After sharing information, a full-fledged meeting began.

I mean, the term 'collective intelligence' exists for a reason, right?

Armin is an explorer who loves 'adventure,' a rarity in this industry, so he's perfect to put our heads together.

"What do you think, Armin? Your thoughts."

"... I'm not certain, but I see similarities to Rifts here and there."

"Huh? It's different from a Rift, isn't it, meow? The place Bjorn went to didn't even have a Guardian, and a portal opened just by bringing out that Tesik...? Anyway, him..."

"The similarity I'm talking about is in a different aspect. If my prediction is correct... when we re-enter that island next time, it will likely be the same as before. Before the Lord of the Sea appeared and everything was turned upside down."

"Huh? You mean time goes back, meow...?"

"Yes, whether time goes back or is newly created. Whatever the reason, the result will be the same. Isn't that how Rifts are? Even if you die right before the Guardian after killing all the monsters, the next explorer to enter has to start from the beginning again."

In short, a Rift 'resets'.

And it was highly probable that the Twin Islands would be the same.

I had a feeling from the fact that a 'story' existed, but I became certain after seeing the Lord of the Sea invade and everything getting turned into a mess at the end.

However, the difference between the Twin Islands and a Rift...

'Is that the chapters aren't clearly divided.'

If a Rift has a closed-off stage structure, the Twin Islands are an open world, offering much more freedom.

For example, I think the story would have progressed somehow even if I had gone directly to Rymia Island instead of Parune Island.

"For the second playthrough, we should aim to reach the Sixth Floor as early as possible."

"We'll have to. Not only will it increase the explorable time, but there might be things that can only be done during that time period."

"Ah, I see. I hadn't thought that far."

The meeting continued after that.

Well, in the middle, Ainar and Misha, who had lost interest in the meeting's content, left, so it ended up feeling like just me and Armin discussing things.

Anyway.

"For now, it's best to confirm that this wasn't a perfect conquest."

"Right. Other than coming out without losing my memories and equipment, I didn't receive any particular rewards."

"It's highly likely that you met the conditions for escape, not conquest. The condition must have been either 'Tetrasea's escape' or 'survival until the 50th day'... one of those two."

"What should we do for the next playthrough?"

"Unless we send another vanguard to gather information... we'll have to prepare thoroughly. It's a place teeming with Rank 1 monsters and other creatures of similar strength. The approach will also have to be very different from now..."

The meeting grew longer and longer, finally ending the next morning, after which we held a vote.

The Labyrinth was closing soon anyway, and there was far too little time to reach the Seventh Floor.

Should we just play around and pass the time here?

Or should we at least try to do something?

Everyone was so sick of boredom that the latter option was chosen unanimously, and we headed to Parune Island, which was nearby.

The goal was to search for any information or clues that could be used on the Twin Islands, but we couldn't find anything particularly noteworthy.

Well, a meaningful question did arise.

"What in the world is this offering...?"

"Huh? You don't know?"

"If you're talking about the phenomenon where 'Stormgush' appears when the offering is destroyed, or the 'Lord of Tears,' then of course I know."

"Oh..."

I knew what Armin was talking about.

The 'Wrath of the Sea' event that starts when you destroy the offering on Parune Island.

In fact, similar-looking offerings exist on other islands, and destroying all of them summons the 'Lord of Tears'.

"Both are related to the 'Lord of Tears'."

"Yes. Furthermore, perhaps because I heard your story, Captain, I've become curious as to why these offerings came to be."

"This part is worth investigating further."

Chapter 897: Rebuilding (1)

To put it simply, the items from the random box were all below expectations.

Despite the GM's words, I felt a sense of disappointment first.

That's because the No.981 Firefly Ring felt like it was already part of the random box package.

Like a guaranteed item?

I would have felt much better if there had been just one more item of that caliber, nothing more, nothing less.

"Will you be selling it?"

"Everything but the ring."

"Hmm, are you planning to use the ring yourself?"

"That's the plan."

"A wise decision. As long as you don't plan on quitting expeditions, it's an item that is always better to use yourself."

The GM spoke as if he would have tried to stop me from selling it, but truth be told, if I had obtained this ring in the early days, I would have sold it without a second thought.

I would have judged it to be of little use to me.

In fact, when I played the game, at some point I started playing by just selling this ring for a high price to buy other gear whenever I found it.

This ring isn't for combat.

"As you may know, that ring is specialized for finding Gabrielius's Arrangement."

"I know."

Gabrielius's Arrangement.

The very thing I used to call a 'Hidden Piece' when I played the game.

When there's a Hidden Element nearby, this ring activates with a very low probability and acts as a Guide.

I benefited greatly from it during my playthroughs as well.

However...

'It was around the time I could see the end of the game.'

From then on, the Firefly Ring was nothing more or less than an expensive Magic Stone to me.

'Because I was confident I knew everything there was to know about [Dungeon & Stone].'

First Floor.

Of course, that was arrogance.

The 'new island' that appeared after the event with the underground Lord of Tears, which could be called a new floor.

Not to mention the updated content, but it was the same even without that.

Therefore, I had to admit it.

'... There's still so much left.'

Yes, there was still so much left.

Things in this world that I still didn't know.

What in the world is a Labyrinth Historian?

ГўВҖВңAnyway, the estate is nearing completionГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes. The construction, which was supposed to take a year, was delayed for several years due to various incidents. But now you know, right? Melves always keeps its promises.ГўВҖВқ

Wow, so this day is really coming.

ItГўВҖВҷs a shame that my other companions like Amelia and Erwen are trapped on the Underground First Floor.

To not be able to share this joyous news.

ГўВҖВңPlease come visit our assembly sometimes. Everyone wants to see you, Viscount. Don't just attend the Royal Family councilГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҰAh, I understand. I'll be sure to go next time.ГўВҖВқ

Was it because I heard about the estate's completion?

Recently, I had been skipping the assembly participation requests.

ГўВҖВҳГўВҖВҰIs this why they insisted on rebuilding the mansion and gifting it to me?ГўВҖВҷ

Hmm, that might be possible.

After I became a regular member of the Royal Family council, my relationship with Melves was completely reversed.

A new wind for the non-human noble houses.

Having far surpassed their expectations, I had now become their hope itself.

ГўВҖВҳI feel a bit sorry for them tooГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҷ

-----

Anyway, that was about it for the major things.

As for the minor things, I stopped by the Guild yesterday and chewed out the Guild Master a bit.

For the rest, I was busy planning the next expedition, but despite the effort, there were no results.

The input values havenГўВҖВҷt come out accurately yet.

ГўВҖВҳWill I get a rough idea after todayГўВҖВҰ?ГўВҖВҷ

For that reason, I left the Holy Land bright and early and came to Karnon.

Because today was the day the leaders of the six tribes gathered for a tribal summit.

The Dragon Uncle.

MishaГўВҖВҷs dad (not).

The narrow-minded Dwarf chief.

The elf who dislikes me for some suspicious reason.

AndГўВҖВҰ

ГўВҖВҳThis time, the human representative is the Mage Tower Lord.ГўВҖВҷ

Last time, some wannabe baron came and just served as an emotional punching bag before leaving.

ГўВҖВңWell then, since everyone has arrived, shall we begin?ГўВҖВқ

The Dwarf chief, who was acting benevolent, smiled and broke the ice, starting the summit.

ГўВҖВңThe first agenda isГўВҖВҰ oh? It was submitted by the Barbarian side?ГўВҖВқ

The Dwarf chief, who was presiding today, looked at me with a surprised expression.

Well, I had never officially submitted an agenda at the summit before.

Besides, the tribal summit has a slightly different atmosphere from the Royal Family council.

Official agendas are rarely brought up, and it proceeds more like a free-talk session.

Perhaps thatГўВҖВҷs why things like this are possible.

ГўВҖВңHowever, there is only the submission of the agenda, but the detailed contents are blankГўВҖВҰ I am very curious about its contents. Now, please, go ahead.ГўВҖВқ

My turn to speak came, but I didn't bother to stand up.

My sitting height is someone else's standing height, after all.

Therefore, even when I'm sitting, it's the same as standing.

ГўВҖВңI will not explain about the ГўВҖВҳislandГўВҖВҷ that has newly appeared, as I'm sure you all know about it.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh, it was an agenda about the Labyrinth?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs a hassle, so I'll get straight to the point.ГўВҖВқ

I then continued speaking quickly after only a short pause.

ГўВҖВңI conducted an expedition to the new island this time and gained quite a lot.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңГўВҖВҰSeeing that your equipment is intact, it seems you were able to return alive?ГўВҖВқ

I nodded.

Well, since I used the Outing Doll to enter, I wouldn't have lost my equipment even if I had failed.

But thatГўВҖВҷs not whatГўВҖВҷs important right now.

I had contemplated endlessly while planning the expedition after returning to the city, but this was indeed the best course of action.

ГўВҖВңOn the next expedition, I will enter that island again.ГўВҖВқ

The Rank 1 monster, Leviathan.

The senior researcher who fought on par with it.

And on top of that, Berzak, who could act as a wild card.

ГўВҖВңIf you want, IГўВҖВҷll help you guys join in too.ГўВҖВқ

More power is needed.

--- Chapter 899 ---

Chapter 899: Rebuilding (3)

A new exploration area, officially inscribed on the Stone of Honor.

The moment it was mentioned, the air changed.

"Hmm......."

"It seems you are in over your head, trying to do it alone?"

It was a somewhat lukewarm response, but I could feel it instinctively.

That very reaction was proof of their immense interest, more than anything else.

'Hah, trying to play it cool.'

Well, they had no choice but to be interested.

After all, there was a truth that had never once changed since the Labyrinth came into being.

New things always mean big money.

"Still... I would like to hear the details first."

Even the Mage Tower Lord's eyes were shining.

He was attending as the human representative, but he was, first and foremost, a Mage.

It was inevitable that personal interests would come into play during the summit.

In fact, records of past tribal summits often showed him representing the interests of Mages.

'The case of the Barbarian heart is a prime example, isn't it?'

It was one of the sad histories of the powerless Barbarian Tribe.

However, that was in the past, so I'll set it aside.

"The Mage Tower Lord is right. We must know the exact situation to decide whether we will offer support or not."

The representatives of each tribe had their eyes on the prize, but they were still testing the waters before jumping in.

Well, it was a natural attitude to take.

No matter how profitable a new area might be, if the probability of success was too low, it would be a gamble, not an investment.

They would need at least a basic estimate.

ButГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰГўВҖВҰ.

'Who do they think they are, trying to get information for free?'

I had no intention of just giving them an estimate.

How do you think I obtained this information?

I know the value of this information better than anyone.

That is why.

"I believe I've already explained the situation?"

I responded firmly to their probing attitude.

"The expedition is already well underway, and I will be entering that island again. All you need to do is decide whether you're in or out."

"I understand what you are saying, but if that is all you tell us, none of us will be able to join the game you wish for, Viscount Yandel. Everyone here is a representative of their own group."

"Hmm, that is also true."

At the Mage Tower Lord's words, I nodded my head as if taking a step back.

"However, I can't just give it away for free either. Pay up. Hmm... 200 million Stone per person should do!"

Needless to say, the expressions of the representatives who were told to pay were not pleased.

Come on, 200 million isn't even that much money for them.

"Ah, of course, this is a kind of deposit. If you decide to join my game after hearing the information, I will return the information fee."

Simply put, it meant I would absolutely not let them hear the information for free.

At this, the expressions of each representative grew serious.

It seemed they were all running calculations in their heads.

"Very well. I shall pay."

The first to finish his calculations was the Mage Tower Lord.

Next were the Beastkin and the Fairy Tribe.

"We Beastkin will do the same."

"If we can hear of the most secret mystery in the Labyrinth at this time for 200 million Stone, it would not be a loss."

Three of them agreed to the terms of the paid service, but not everyone else made the same choice.

"Hmph, I feel like I'm being scammed. It's best to back out in times like these."

The Dwarf Chief, a typical petty man, refused the terms, and next was the Dragon Uncle.

"It is a pity, but we Dragonkin will also sit this one out. Unless it is an internal matter of the tribe, I do not have much authority to force anything."

The Dragon Uncle's refusal was expected.

The Dragonkin were not that serious about Labyrinth expeditions in the first place.

He probably expressed his refusal at the very end as a way of being considerate to me.

He didn't want to spoil my business.

"Alright, then the three of you, stay behind after the summit ends. Since there are outsiders present, we will talk in earnest then."

"My, my, it feels like I'm being openly ostracized."

The Dwarf Chief grumbled, but I gave him no attention and simply ignored him.

People with no friends are usually the only ones who don't know why.

***

The tribal summit, which lasted half a day, was over.

This was unusually fast, even among all past summits.

Well, the sideshow was bigger than the main event.

This time, there was a common interest more important than the summit itself.

Everyone had tacitly agreed to end it quickly...

"Ha, they are all blinded by greed. As I see it, they're just going to waste their money."

The Dwarf Chief who had refused the terms was now claiming a mental victory, but those who had agreed showed no reaction.

Was that why?

"He's letting you join in the first place? Isn't it obvious it will be dangerous? To think you would waste the lives of your precious Bloodline in such a place...."

The Dwarf Chief had moved beyond simply 'The grapes must be sour!' to 'The grapes must be poisonous!' and at this, the other representatives' expressions soured slightly.

It wasn't because they were persuaded by his words, but...

Well, if everyone bought a stock, and the one guy who didn't started shouting 'That stock is going to crash!', who would have anything nice to say?

"I don't know about that. I find it difficult to agree with you on that point. While it is difficult to say our Mage Tower has a good relationship with Viscount Yandel... and the Barbarian Tribe... this is a separate matter."

"Separate?"

"Yes. I have never heard even a rumor of Viscount Yandel backstabbing someone for his own gain. He is highly likely to move in a direction that benefits everyone."

A truly logical answer befitting a Mage.

Hearing this, the Family Head Karlstein added a word.

"Tsk, that part is certainly believable. Though his relationships with women seem to be a mess."

Though her voice was slightly displeased, it was undoubtedly a statement in my defense.

"Dumoka, when it comes to the Labyrinth, no one can deny the achievements of Viscount Yandel."

To top it all off, even the pointy-ear who hated me for some strange reason made a comment that seemed to acknowledge my abilities, and the Dwarf Chief, with an expression like a rotten peanut, left the summit.

And...

"Phew, then I suppose I should make way as well."

Soon, even the Dragon Uncle left, leaving only those who had agreed to the terms.

"Now then, hurry up and tell us. You took the promissory notes with our names on them, did you not?"

Hmm... the fact that I received the advance payment in checks was a bit concerning, but with the value of these people's names, I doubt there would be any problems.

Insisting on cash here would only make me an unlikeable Barbarian, so at this point, I readily shared the information.

'How many times have I repeated this story?'

Since it was a tale I had told several times already, I summarized the key points and briefed the representatives.

And...

"Hmm......."

As the briefing continued, the expressions of the representatives, who had been listening with excitement at first, began to change.

They had realized the trap.

"So, you must be able to speak 'Ancient Language' at least as well as the Viscount to conduct a proper expedition..."

They probably intended to just take the information and run, but that path was now blocked.

Those who could speak the Ancient Language were not common.

If they did not join my expedition, they would just be out 200 million Stone.

And...

"A Leviathan... that is a Rank 1 monster, is it not?"

"And there is even an unidentified creature that fought the Leviathan to a draw..."

"... This changes the story. The Mage Tower, as well as each of our tribes, would have to stake our very fates on this support, or this conquest will fail."

Finally, as the tale of the Twin Islands reached its climax, the representatives' expressions began to rot.

They were inwardly thinking that they had wasted 200 million Stone.

They must have already concluded that this conquest could never succeed, and even if it did, they would have to endure enormous losses.

But...

'Come on, at least hear me out until the end.'

As the organizer, I could not stand by and watch the investors' motivation dwindle, so I quickly continued.

"Don't worry too much. My personal Labyrinth Historian and I are diligently researching the Twin Islands."

"Labyrinth Historian? What is that?"

"Tsk, you don't even know that?"

I clicked my tongue and chided Misha's father, who was tilting his head, before stating only the important points.

"Anyway, the important thing is that we might not have to fight a Rank 1 monster to conquer this island."

"Hmm, that would change things... but that is not certain yet, is it?"

"Well, that's true. But even if we do have to fight a Rank 1 monster, it won't be much of a problem."

"What do you mean?"

At my words, the pointy-eared man's eyes narrowed sharply.

"Are you planning to force our sacrifice, as Dumoka said?"

My, what a murderous glare.

It was a look that made me want to tease him a little, but I held back, not knowing how that serious pointy-eared man would react.

"Of course not. I simply meant that even if we do have to fight a Rank 1 creature, the damage won't be as great as you think."

The pointy-eared man fell silent at my words.

He just stared at me intently, as if telling me to continue.

"Why are you so confident?"

In the end, the Family Head Karlstein asked on behalf of everyone, and I shrugged, my words trailing off.

"It's a bit of a long story."

In fact, I had only realized this information relatively recently.

After finishing my entire journey and returning to the city, I was organizing my inventory... no, to be precise, I was organizing Level 2 Peter's bag.

When I checked inside the bag, I was shocked.

"To summarize it briefly."

I had definitely used up potions and scrolls while fighting monsters on Parune Island, but they were all replenished to the amount I had when I first entered.

I interpreted this phenomenon as such.

"The consumables consumed there are not consumed."

Hmm, is that a bit confusing?

Anyway, to put it simply in modern terms.

'If you succeed in escaping the Twin Islands, you get a full payback on all used consumables.'

It means they get duplicated.

***

The representatives, who at first seemed to think I was spouting nonsense, completely changed their tune once the story of Level 2 Peter's mysterious bag unfolded.

They were all smart enough to understand.

"If the potions and scrolls were really all replenished, then it's highly likely the same rule applies to other consumables as well."

"Perhaps it's not just consumables. You said all the equipment you wore was returned, didn't you? Even though you had lost it inside."

"Which means...?"

Soon, everyone exchanged glances of mutual understanding and nodded.

"This is worth a shot."

The biggest obstacle in raiding a Rank 1 monster was none other than 'cost-effectiveness'.

In a way, it was similar to modern gold mines.

If the cost of developing a gold mine was greater than the profit it yielded, that gold mine would remain buried and abandoned forever, just like that.

'Everyone avoids hunting Rank 1 monsters because the cost of consumables is just too high.'

But at this very moment, that problem was solved.

If clearing it guaranteed the return of the principal, then 'over-investing' without any regard for cost-effectiveness became possible.

Yes, for example...

"... I see now, you needed the 'Arrows of Paralysis'."

Like the Fairy Tribe's three and only arrows.

"You must have had our Mage Tower's 'Little Flare' in mind as well."

Or the Mage Tower's secret weapon, which was powered by a high-grade magi-tech core that had been auctioned off for hundreds of millions at the Sky Auction House.

An investment that would normally result in a loss even after killing a Rank 1 monster had now become feasible.

"Uh... we don't really have anything like that..."

Well, the Beastkin would have to invest that much more manpower and use their bodies, I suppose.

Either that, or they'd have to settle for the leftovers after we'd eaten.

"If a fair distribution based on contribution is made, our Mage Tower is willing to participate in this expedition. Ah, of course, we will also provide at least ten shots of the Little Flare."

"The same goes for our Fairy Tribe. We will provide one Arrow of Paralysis. If you have clear evidence to prove your words that the consumed items will be returned, we can even provide two. Of course, we will have to discuss distribution before that."

"We Beastkin will participate as well."

Only the Family Head Karlstein, representing the Beastkin, did not mention distribution.

It seemed he had some conscience, at least.

--- Chapter 900 ---

Chapter 900: Rebuilding (4)

Three days after the tribal summit.

I once again visited the House of the Rabbit Baron.

After all, our exclusive labyrinth historian had finally contacted me.

[Ah, you're here!]

As if he had been waiting at the entrance since morning, the rabbit boy popped out and greeted me the moment I arrived.

[It's been a while, Maroang.]

[Yes! Viscount!]

He spoke in the Ancient Language, so I replied in the Ancient Language as well, and as if that pleased him greatly, the rabbit boy grinned foolishly.

[Come to my room! Quickly!]

As if all fear of me had completely vanished, the boy even grabbed my hand and pulled me toward his room.

ГўВҖВҰHe is rather cute.

I felt that if I had a nephew, it would be just like this.

Pretending I couldn't resist, I let myself be dragged along, and we soon arrived at the rabbit boy's room.

The room's interior was just as I had imagined.

ГўВҖВңYou have a lot of relics?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHehe, would you like to have a look around first?ГўВҖВқ

The walls, bookshelves, and shelvesГўВҖВ”the room was filled with all sorts of collections labeled 'research materials'.

If I said I wouldn't, I felt like the boy's ears would droop, so I just looked around the room without a word.

Well, to be honest, it was quite interesting.

Relics of the Labyrinth were a field I was interested in too.

ГўВҖВңThis here is the ApostleГўВҖВҷs Book!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңA relic that has a low chance of appearing inside the corpse of the Guardian of the Green Coal Mine if you defeat it after casting ГўВҖВҳDistortionГўВҖВҷ on it.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWow, how did you know?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt's basic knowledge for an Explorer.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңStill, you probably don't know the contents, do you? It's heavily damaged, so there's not much that's readable, but the word 'Earth Mother' appears in this book!ГўВҖВқ

Hmm, did it...?

When I played the game, it was just junk I sold to shops.

Well, most of the relics here are like that.

'I always wondered who would buy this stuff when I sold it...'

And the person who bought it was right here.

In any case, the rabbit boy, excited that I recognized his things, introduced his collection with sparkling eyes.

ГўВҖВңAh, and this is....ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThe Necronomicon.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWow! You know this one too?ГўВҖВқ

How could I not.

In a way, the Bloodstained Fortress was the first Rift I conquered after coming to this world.

'It feels nostalgic.'

Back then, Raven farmed for the Necronomicon by herself and slyly pocketed it.

I watched that ruthless scene with my own two eyes but couldn't say a word, afraid I'd be suspected of being an Evil Spirit.

ГўВҖВңBut isn't this a book of dark magic? It doesn't seem like something you'd collect.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, that's true! That's why it's a bit on the expensive side among relics. These are popular with Mages too, so even the Mage Tower buys them... Ah! That's not what you were asking, was it?ГўВҖВқ

The rabbit boy, who had been suspiciously talkative ever since we entered the room, came to his senses and answered anew.

ГўВҖВңThe Necronomicon is classified as a book of dark magic, but that doesn't mean there's no information to be gained from it! If you look at this page, there's a sentence that seems to hate the 'Empire'...ГўВҖВқ

Ah, right, right...

This kid is fine with anything from the Labyrinth as long as he can 'read' it.

I now see that being a labyrinth historian was a profession of weaving together tens of thousands of scattered phrases like puzzle pieces into a single story.

ГўВҖВңAnyway, I think that's enough looking around... I'd like to get to the main point.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, I'm sorry! I got too excited, didn't I?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңNot at all. I had a fun time looking around too. So, the reason you called me here must be because you've discovered something, right?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOf course! I'll tell you right now!ГўВҖВқ

And so, we finished looking at the collection and moved on to the main topic.

ГўВҖВңFirst, before I begin, would you like to see this research material?ГўВҖВқ

The rabbit boy handed me a book in the form of a notebook.

ГўВҖВңThis book is a relic so rare that it doesn't even have an official academic name like the ГўВҖВҳApostleГўВҖВҷs BookГўВҖВҷ or the ГўВҖВҳNecronomiconГўВҖВҷ. Where to acquire it is also completely unknown. I happened to find it at the Free Market while looking for ГўВҖВҳAncient LanguageГўВҖВҷ books.... Ah! It's such a shame. If only I knew who the seller was, I could have found out where it was acquired....ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң... So what is this?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt's the diary of an Empire soldier. For now, I've named it the ГўВҖВҳRecord of the Nameless CastawayГўВҖВҷ.ГўВҖВқ

He seemed about to ramble on about how he wished it could become an official academic name someday, so I cut him off appropriately, and only then did the real discussion begin.

ГўВҖВңThe main content of this book is about a nameless Empire soldier who was shipwrecked on an island. And I think that island might be ГўВҖВҳParune IslandГўВҖВҷ.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWhat?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt also appears in this record. Things like the village being full of sea monsters, and the fact that there's an Imperial ArmyГўВҖВҷs outpost close enough to return to with just a raft... and even that the ГўВҖВҳSea GodГўВҖВҷ appears in the story.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOh.ГўВҖВқ

The sound escaped my lips unconsciously.

If this was true, then there was a high possibility that the island in the record was Parune Island, and the Imperial ArmyГўВҖВҷs outpost was Rymia Island.

ГўВҖВңMay I take a look?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңOf course!ГўВҖВқ

I took the record from him and quickly skimmed through it.

There were parts damaged beyond readability here and there, but after reading to the end, to summarize it briefly....

ГўВҖВңIt's about being stranded on a sea monster island, secretly building a raft to escape, and then encountering the Sea God of Calamity and dying.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes, that's right. So I also wondered if it was some kind of fairy tale... but as soon as I heard your story at the library, the contents of this book came to mind. I thought that maybe this isn't a fairy tale after all...ГўВҖВқ

Right, this is not a fairy tale.

It must have been something that actually happened in the very distant future.

ГўВҖВңAnyway, this book isn't the only thing I've been holed up in my room researching. I've searched through countless relics for information that seems to stem from the same source and compared them.ГўВҖВқ

For example, the two islands on this 'sea chart' are a 99% match in shape to Parune Island and Rymia Island.

Or how the word ГўВҖВҳRymiaГўВҖВҷ appears in a relic from the Panthelion Ruins, and the text that follows is very suggestive.

He tried to list all the steps, so I stopped him.

That wasn't what was important right now.

ГўВҖВңJust the key points.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, ah... Yes!ГўВҖВқ

Hearing my demand, the rabbit boy quickly listed only the results and keywords.

ГўВҖВңTetraseaГўВҖВҷs twin sibling's name is ГўВҖВҳRymiaГўВҖВҷ. We need to think about why that island was named Rymia.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңThe condition for the Sea God of Calamity's appearance is also linked to 'rage'. Of course, this is a trait seen in normal zones, but you said it appeared in the Twin Sea because the Empire burned down Parune Island, right? Perhaps if we prevent that event, the Sea God of Calamity won't appear either!ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIt's not Parune Island, but there are similar offerings throughout the Great Sea, right? This is a record related to one of those offerings. There was a phrase, ГўВҖВҳPraying for her eternal rest.ГўВҖВҷ Considering that Tetrasea is a ГўВҖВҳmale formГўВҖВҷ, there might be some other hidden story.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAmong the records related to the Empire, there's a description of a creature similar to the ГўВҖВҳsenior researcherГўВҖВҷ you mentioned, and that creature's weakness is Divine Power.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAccording to the EmpireГўВҖВҷs soldier ranks, a Lieutenant General is the lowest among the generals. Colonel is the highest... Based on the records, the estimated combat power of a Lieutenant General is about that of a Rank 2 monster.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңTh... that's about all I found... Was it helpful...?ГўВҖВқ

Looking at the shyly speaking rabbit boy, I was at a loss for words.

ГўВҖВҳYou ask if it was helpful?ГўВҖВҷ

Of course, I don't know yet.

I haven't even verified how many of this boy's conjectures are true.

However, I could say one thing for sure.

ГўВҖВңIt was a very big help.ГўВҖВқ

Even if only half of this is true, it will be an enormous help.

Wouldn't it be?

The combat power of Berzak.

The weakness of the senior researcher.

And on top of that, the conditions for the Sea God of Calamity's appearance.

ГўВҖВңBeing a labyrinth historian is quite an amazing profession.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңHehe.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI'll be counting on you from now on.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes? From now on...?ГўВҖВқ

Hah, you tempt a perfectly sane person to the point of madness and now you're pretending not to know?

ГўВҖВңFrom now on, you are my exclusive labyrinth historian. I'll even give you a spot in my clan if you want.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВң... Y-yes? In the Anabada Clan?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYou don't want to? From now on, you'll be the first to see and research the relics I find in the Labyrinth.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңH-how could I not! Please let me! I'll be the Viscount's exclusive labyrinth historian!ГўВҖВқ

I have a new companion.

***

'Is it next week now...'

Before I knew it, only a few days remained until the next Labyrinth opened.

It was because an invitation had arrived from someone I had almost forgotten about recently, having had too many other things on my mind.

The Knight of the King.

Astarotta Verun.

ГўВҖВңI'm already busy as hell, so calling me all the way here must mean a decision has been made, right?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes, the talks with the Chancellor are already over. I will hand over the Secret Security Force to you. If you go to the Secret Security Force with this letter of appointment, the predecessor will handle the handover.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYour work is clean?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIf I may give some advice, it will be easier to find a suitable deputy and entrust them with the main duties.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, just like the King did to you?ГўВҖВқ

I said it playfully, but Astarotta didn't reply.

ГўВҖВңAnyway, don't worry about that. That was my plan from the beginning. I've already chosen a deputy.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWho is it?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAh, Harin Suebwi... that woman. She's a smart woman, so it's not a bad choice.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI think so too.ГўВҖВқ

I was vague about that part.

ГўВҖВңOur goals just happened to align. The Evil Spirit Integration Policy was also a long-cherished wish of Countess Peprock.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI see, so you used that as bait. Then I'm less worried. With loyalty enough to guard her unconscious master every day, she won't betray you.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңYes, that's it.ГўВҖВқ

Fortunately, Astarotta didn't seem to suspect anything about my vague explanation, and that was the end of that conversation.

ГўВҖВңIs it because you have business? You came right away this time, didn't you? Ah, not right away. It's just before the Labyrinth opens again.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWow, you really did it?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңSo, what about you? Have you made your decision?ГўВҖВқ

Like a Barbarian, I went straight to the point, but that didn't mean I wasn't nervous.

If Hyunbyeol shook her head here, my future plans would be significantly disrupted.

Ssk.

Hyunbyeol then stood up and walked over to the bed where Ragna lay.

She stood there for a long time, looking down at Ragna, who was lying with her eyes comfortably closed.

What on earth could she be thinking right now? All I could know was the result, skipping the process.

ГўВҖВңI'll come again.ГўВҖВқ

Right, so it's a YES.

What's this? Is she telling me to carry it?

ГўВҖВңHey, it's not even that heavy...ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңIf you keep this up, should I just not go?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңAlright. I'll carry it.ГўВҖВқ

...

But was there anything she could say?

ГўВҖВңWhat are you doing, not coming?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңWe're going to stop by the Secret Security Force today. Since I've decided to do it, I have no intention of doing it half-heartedly.ГўВҖВқ

Hah, this girl is quite reliable.
