--- Chapter 901 ---

Chapter 901: Rebuilding (5)

After leaving the Royal Palace, I headed straight to the Secret Security Force in Commelby, as Hyunbyeol had requested.

It was an ill-fated organization that once boasted great prestige, but after its core duties like infiltration, intelligence, and operations were transferred to independent agencies, it was left with little to do besides hunting Evil Spirits.

However, even though its limbs had been torn off, so to speak, the Secret Security Force was still ingrained in the minds of ordinary citizens as an object of fear.

"The predecessor... no, the person who will soon become the predecessor, seems to have wanted it that way."

"Why?"

"Because if people don't fear the Secret Security Force, they can't be controlled. You've met the Evil Spirit Hunters, haven't you?"

"I have."

"Then you'd know. Their title sounds grand, but they weren't particularly strong, were they?"

Hmm... that was certainly true.

It was a surprise attack, but even back in my bare-handed Barbarian days, I was able to defeat an Evil Spirit Hunter.

"It's the unavoidable weakness of the Secret Security Force. With more prestigious organizations like Mozlan, the Public Security Office, and the Knight Orders... talented individuals with actual combat value have no reason to join the Secret Security Force."

"So you're saying there are no knight-class personnel at all?"

"There might be some. It's the perfect place for those who have been pushed out in political struggles and demoted."

"Ah..."

So this was what the Secret Security Force was like.

I guess that's why they gave it to me when I asked.

If I had asked for something like the Public Security Office, they would have never agreed.

That position held real, immense power.

"You've probably seen it at least once, right? When a child is crying, adults scare them by saying, 'The Evil Spirit Hunter will come and get you.'"

"Ah, I have seen that."

It was only then that I realized.

The Secret Security Force was the boogeyman.

"Well then, shall we go in?"

Having arrived at the Secret Security Force building, we slowly stepped inside.

We entered a lobby, and its atmosphere was different from any other organization.

For starters, there was only one person stationed at the lobby desk.

Well, given the nature of the organization, it probably wasn't a place that received 'guests', so it wasn't that strange, but...

"What brings you here?"

No, why does the lobby employee look so gloomy.

Was he demoted and stuck here?

"I am Viscount Bjorn Yandel. I have business with the Chief, so guide me quickly."

"Yes."

The employee's expression didn't change even after hearing my name.

It wasn't because he was a seasoned agent, but rather he just seemed to lack any will to live.

"You may go in."

The employee who guided us to the top floor simply gave a perfunctory nod before returning to his post.

We let out a dry laugh at his apparent lack of interest and entered the Chief's office.

"You've arrived earlier than expected. I've already heard that Viscount Yandel would be my successor."

The look in the predecessor's eyes as he watched me was very strange.

It seemed filled with dejection, yet there was also a hint of relief.

"May I ask you just one thing?"

"Go ahead."

"Why did you choose this place? A man like you, Viscount Yandel, could have obtained a position at a much better organization..."

Hmm, well..

"Not really. They wouldn't have handed over such important organizations willingly... and even if I forced my way in, I'd only face troublesome matters."

"Hmm, is that so... I can understand that to some extent."

"But what happens to you now? Will you be transferred to another organization?"

"At first, I thought that would be the case... but I was simply given a temporary suspension from my position. It's unlikely that anyone will call for me again, so it's practically a dismissal."

"Oh dear, that's too bad."

I prodded him slightly, trying to gauge his inner thoughts, but he surprisingly showed little reaction.

"So I plan to submit my resignation as soon as the handover is complete. It's a relief that I'll receive a pension. At least I won't have to worry about taxes, so I'm thinking of taking it easy from now on."

"Good for you. That's the happiest kind of life."

"Haha, perhaps you're right."

The Chief laughed it off, but I was being sincere.

A life in a house with a kitchen, where you can get treatment when you're sick, and after fifty, you can quit working and live leisurely, maybe raising three dogs.

That was actually my original goal.

"Anyway, there isn't much to hand over regarding the work. I already organized everything into documents as soon as I heard the news. I didn't have any malicious intent to mess with my successor, so I diligently wrote down everything necessary for the job. As long as you familiarize yourself with this, you won't have any trouble."

"Is that so."

"I've already packed my things. I've spent over twenty years in this room, but it turns out there isn't much to take with me."

"In any case, if you have any questions or are curious about anything, come find me. I will answer anything faithfully until my resignation is approved. Oh, and this is a gift."

"He seems to have put some work into this."

Hyunbyeol immediately sat in the Chief's chair and began to read through the organized documents in front of her.

"It seems it's true that he didn't have any malicious intent. This is incredibly detailed. There are even tips for practical work."

While Hyunbyeol spent a long time reading through the documents, I grew bored and left the Chief's office to look around the building.

Four floors above ground.

Four floors below.

The Secret Security Force was a building with a total of eight floors, and there was less to see than I expected.

Perhaps work had stopped since the news of the handover, as the underground prison, which should have been filled with Evil Spirits, was completely empty.

The eyes of the employees I encountered while exploring the building were also lifeless.

So, I returned to the Chief's office.

"Okay, I'm done familiarizing myself."

"Huh? Already?"

"The basic framework is similar in any group. If there's something I don't understand, I can just pull it out and look at it again later."

"So what are you reading now?"

"Ah, the employee roster. After checking the personnel evaluations, I need to pick the most competent one to be my aide. I absolutely hate troublesome work."

I couldn't hide my astonishment.

Hyunbyeol, whom I had chosen as my deputy, was going to pick an aide to delegate her work to?

"Subcontracting the subcontracted work of a subcontractor...?"

"What?"

"Harin Suebwi. Stop this terrible absurdity right now. Subcontracting only begets more subcontracting, it does nothing to benefit the worlГўВҖВ”"

"What are you talking about? You want me to do it?"

"Who looks the most competent?"

Well, whatever.

As long as it makes my life easier.

***

The very first administrative order Hyunbyeol gave after becoming the second-in-command of the Secret Security Force was an 'inauguration ceremony'.

Well, 'inauguration ceremony' was a bit of an overstatement.

It was just gathering all the employees who were at work and giving some greetings in the backyard, but anyway.

As instructed, I introduced Hyunbyeol to the employees and then created and bestowed upon her the new title of 'Deputy Chief'.

Even at this point, the employees showed no reaction.

They just looked at me as if thinking, 'He's just a puppet boss, and Hyunbyeol will be doing all the actual work.'

However...

"You feel pathetic, don't you?"

"?"

"Yourselves, I mean. Wasting your days away without any motivation in a workplace where you can't feel any sense of accomplishment."

Hyunbyeol's performance began immediately.

Having taken the position of 'Deputy Chief', Hyunbyeol continued her speech, which bordered on personal insult, prodding at the employees' sore spots.

Unable to bear it, one employee finally protested.

"So what is it you're trying to say!"

"Money."

"?"

"If you're going to do a pathetic job anyway, shouldn't you at least earn proper money to make it feel worthwhile?"

At her shocking and unpredictable words, all the employees were stunned.

And Hyunbyeol did not miss that moment.

"A 200% salary increase."

"?"

"Performance-based bonuses and paid leave."

"..."

"Increased manpower, construction of an employee cafeteria. Full transportation reimbursement. A 100% bonus upon marriage."

As Hyunbyeol continued, the dazed employees came to their senses and frowned.

"Do you think that's possible! The Secret Security Force doesn't have that kind of money!"

"Yes. We don't. Because all of you are pathetically doing only what you're told, without any motivation."

"I, I can't tolerate these insults any longГўВҖВ”!"

"That's why I'm going to change you. I'll make you so good at your jobs that even that stingy Administrative Office will have no choice but to give us a bigger budget!"

"Th-That's impossible..."

"Ah, so you're admitting that you can never change your pathetic self?"

"If not, is there any reason to be upset? Ah, is it because you believe the promise won't be kept? Then don't worry. Viscount Yandel will pay you the promised money, even if he has to use his own funds."

Uh...

I don't want to do that...

I wanted to intervene, but I kept my mouth shut at Hyunbyeol's sharp gaze.

"The Viscount...?"

"Well, if it's him..."

"But why would he go to such lengths for us..."

As expected, when she said I would pay them even out of my own pocket, the employees began to stir significantly.

Once again, Hyunbyeol did not miss the opportunity.

"Just a few years ago, Viscount Yandel had nothing. He entered the Labyrinth with just a single Shield and no shoes. But what about now?"

No one answered.

It was a story everyone already knew.

"Though your beginning was small, your latter days shall be very great! I believe it. You just lacked an opportunity! Your lives are not pathetic! It's just that the great first step has not yet been taken!"

There was undeniable power in Hyunbyeol's words.

Enough to plant the seed of 'what if?' in the employees' eyes and make them dream of a rosy future, if only for a moment.

But that was all it was.

Reality is a world ruled by cold reason.

The entire staff did not rise up and chant Hyunbyeol's name.

Hope and doubt.

The two intertwined exquisitely, creating a strange silence, and at this point, Hyunbyeol also took a step back.

"It's natural to not believe and to doubt. I haven't given you any reason to trust me yet. But I will ask this of you. Please, give me a chance to earn your trust. Trust me, and just this once, take off the colored glasses through which you see yourselves!"

"To those who trust me and take that great step forward with me, I promise. I, 'Deputy Chief' Harin Suebwi, will make it happen."

"Just like in the old days when everyone looked up to us."

"A Secret Security Force with a larger budget!"

"A Secret Security Force with more authority!"

"A Secret Security Force with more honor!"

Moved by her powerful voice, some applauded enthusiastically.

Others clapped only formally.

The rest didn't even clap, just sending her suspicious glances.

But Hyunbyeol didn't care.

ГўВҖВңMake the Secret Security Force great again!ГўВҖВқ

What in the world have I appointed as my deputy?

***

After the inauguration ceremony ended, I left Hyunbyeol, who had started working late as if it were the most natural thing to do, and departed from the Secret Security Force.

One way or another, I had obtained the Secret Security Force.

It was time to do what I had postponed.

"You want to activate the Spiritual World?"

The GM's eyes widened when I said I wanted to open the community's server.

"It's still impossible to bring in the participants from the previous assembly, but..."

"We can do that later, as the research progresses."

"But, even if you activate it now, it will just be empty."

"Don't worry. Empty spots are a problem that can be solved by filling them."

"But still, it will take quite a long time to select who to invite..."

Hearing the GM's concerns, I let out a smirk.

He was misunderstanding something.

"No, it'll be over soon. There's no need to meticulously select them like before. Send invitations to all Explorers of the Fifth Rank and higher."

"What?"

"Ah, don't worry. I have a way to get the roster and their addresses from the Guild Master."

"No, it's not that... If you do that, a large number of beings who aren't Evil Spirits will enter...?"

"Ah, that?"

In fact, that's what I want.

Don't they say you should put new wine into new wineskins?

"I suppose I forgot to mention. The 'assembly' I'm about to create is for both Evil Spirits and the Natives."

"What?"

"It will be completely different from before, so it'll need a new name. The previous assembly's name was Ghost Busters, wasn't it?"

The GM, stunned by my declaration, didn't reply, so I didn't ask for his opinion either.

"Ahh, yes... this name will be good."

The community I would create wasn't a space just for Evil Spirits, but for this entire city.

Therefore.

ГўВҖВңThe Rafdonia Busters.ГўВҖВқ

There couldn't be a better name.

--- Chapter 902 ---

Chapter 902: Second playthrough (1)

Rafdonia Busters.

A community for the entire city, not just for Evil Spirits.

The GM was aghast, asking what on earth I wanted, so I briefly explained the new form of community I was envisioning.

What role this community would play in the city.

Why such a community was needed in the world.

And even what benefits it would bring me.

After hearing the full explanation, the GM was surprisingly quick to accept and assured me that adding the few features I mentioned wouldn't be too difficult.

"Good. Then bring me the list. As we discussed, send invitations to all Fifth-Rank and higher explorers."

"Yes, I understand. I'll get it done."

ГўВҖВңA good attitude.ГўВҖВқ

And so, the conversation about the community ended there.

I'll know the results next month, on the day of the assembly...

Therefore, I moved on to the next topic.

ГўВҖВңYou must have heard that I'm in the process of tackling the new island on the Sixth Floor.ГўВҖВқ

"Yes, and?"

"You're participating this time. I'm not asking for your opinion, so just be aware of that."

ГўВҖВңI understand.ГўВҖВқ

Phew, the preparations are almost complete.

***

Time flows without stopping.

And following the nature of time, I prepared and prepared until the very end.

The Fairies, the Beastkin, the Mage Tower.

And myself.

A total of four groups poured their hearts and souls into forming the final roster for this expedition, and I formulated a strategy after assessing the strategic resources invested.

Most of the strategies were designed for a battle against a Rank 1 monster.

Of course, it's uncertain how things will proceed when we return to that island, but being able to fight a Rank 1 monster broadens our options.

One who has all their cards prepared earns the right to steer toward their desired outcome.

Well, I'll only know if these preparations were perfect once I'm there and face it myself.

ГЈВҖВҢYou have entered the First Floor's Crystal Cave.ГЈВҖВҚ

As soon as the Labyrinth opened, we entered through the portal and quickly ascended the floors.

The members of Team 1, who were moving with me, weren't much different from before.

Misha.

Bear Uncle.

Garton Cultner from Armin's expedition.

Malid Kevron from the Jagged Tooth Clan.

And in addition, the navigator I had hired as a mercenary last time, helmsman Jackson, had been let go, and the GM had taken his empty spot.

"Wow... that thing looks really convenient..."

Inside the Labyrinth, the GM was riding a mode of transport similar to a skateboard.

The only differences from a skateboard were that it had no wheels and flew, floating in the air.

I had seen it before when I explored outside the fortress wall with Lee Baekho's party, but for Misha and the others who hadn't, it seemed quite fascinating.

"It doesn't just look convenient, it seems highly practical as well. I've seen a Mage use a similar magical tool, but his couldn't change direction so smoothly, and it was much slower."

At Kevron's admiration, the GM couldn't hide a proud expression and smiled.

ГўВҖВңHaha, it's a magical tool I developed with great care. It can't be compared to the items on the market.ГўВҖВқ

"Oh! Could someone like me try riding it? It looks quite fun."

"Ah... haha, I'm afraid that would be difficult. First, you need to be able to handle Wind Magic... and to be honest, the controls are quite difficult. It took me several years to become this proficient."

"Is that so?"

"However, when we return to the city, I'll make something that you can ride, Mr. Urikfrit."

"Oh! Is that really true?"

"Yes, it's actually quite fun. You'll see when you try it."

The GM quickly assimilated into the team, winning over the Bear Uncle who showed interest in the flying surfboard.

It was a rather surprising scene.

I thought his social skills would be nonexistent since he's always cooped up in his tower.

He's adapting much faster than I expected, isn't he?

"Your title as a Magi-tech researcher is so famous that I don't doubt your skills. However, I'd like to know exactly what you can do."

"Yes, that's a natural question. We are companions from now on. As we ascend the floors, I'll show you as much as I can whenever the opportunity arises, so would you do the same, Mr. Kevron?"

ГўВҖВңOf course. Well, I'm not as famous or mysterious as Mr. Havelion.ГўВҖВқ

"Haha, to say you're not famous despite being the former vice-captain of the Jagged Tooth Clan."

ГўВҖВң... Now, I am merely a regular member of the Anabada Clan.ГўВҖВқ

Kevron was very interested in the GM's abilities, and indeed, the GM wasn't just making empty promises, as he unabashedly showcased various skills during the climb.

Fwoosh! Fwoosh! Fwoosh!

A squad of drones poured out from an opened pocket dimension, shooting compressed air at the enemies.

ShhhrrrkГўВҖВ”!

A golem, brandishing a greatsword reinforced with an air barrier sharp as a chainsaw's blades.

Moreover, that golem could fly and was even armed in colorful heavy armor.

'A mecha?'

A golem in the form of a robot mecha, a familiar sight in modern media.

For a moment, I wondered if that was okay...

"The golem is quite flashy, isn't it? It feels vaguely familiar..."

"The sense of dГғВ©jГғВ  vu is probably because of the ГўВҖВҳAssemble Golems.ГўВҖВҷ They were once very popular toys, but production was halted when it was revealed the creator was an Evil Spirit."

"Ah! Right! I remember buying a few and assembling them myself!"

"Haha, I quite liked the design myself, so I used it as a reference when building my golem."

Right, with an alibi like that, he just went ahead and made it that way.

ГЈВҖВҢEntered the Second FloorГўВҖВҷs Rocky Desert.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢEntered the Third Floor: Path of the Pilgrim.ГЈВҖВҚ

Watching the GM's abilities from time to time, we reached the Third Floor before I knew it.

This time, however, we didn't stop by the 'Statue Temple' to dig for 'Nameless Fragments.'

I had given up on that part from the start, which is why we came up through the 'Rocky Desert' instead of the 'Goblin Forest.'

"Hoo..."

Honestly, if I had my way, I'd forget about the Twin Islands and everything else, open the Underground First Floor portal, and shove 'Peter' in first.

But...

'The probability is too low.'

Even if Peter went to the Underground First Floor, the chance of successfully meeting my companions is extremely low.

Well, it would be a different story if they were still at the starting point on Rock Island.

Otherwise, I would have to search for them myself throughout that vast area, but would Peter, with not a single Essence, be able to do that?

'Even if I succeeded, there would be nothing I could say except to wait while I search for a way.'

So, I postponed sending Peter to the Underground First Floor.

My heart pounds thinking of my companions who have been trapped there for months since the Dimensional Collapse, but to achieve my goal, I must also be able to make cold judgments.

'After this raid is over... the chances of rescue will be much higher.'

Right, so...

ГЈВҖВҢEntered the 4th Floor: Tower of the Sky.ГЈВҖВҚ

We continue to ascend the tower.

Our destination is Rymia Island on the Sixth Floor, which also serves as the rendezvous point for the expedition.

Oh, and for the record, throughout the climb, I've been riding on the back of a golem summoned by the GM, like a piece of luggage.

No, to be precise, should I say my main body?

ГЈВҖВҢLiving Armor defeated.

EXP +2ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢCanivaro defeated.

Hmm...

'Yes, this is a second playthrough.'

A vital spot that would be fatal to any human.

Even though his entire upper abdomen was blown away, the Senior Researcher did not die, and his flight function was working fine.

ГўВҖВңAinar!!ГўВҖВқ

Chapter 914: The Route (2)

Chapter 915: The Route (3)

Chapter 917: The Route (5)

A break in my memory.

My body, having reached a state of Hyper Regeneration,

repaired the parts damaged by the 'Little Flare' as if swapping out components...

"...!"

When I took a sharp breath and opened my eyes to survey my surroundings, Berzak was nowhere to be seen.

Even though the heat had not yet faded from the ground.

'Did I get him...?'

That was my first thought, but soon, various clues came to mind, and I realized that wasn't the case.

There was nothing.

The effect of a corpse scattering into particles of light?

That could happen over time.

An Essence?

It's natural for there to be none if it didn't drop.

But there was no Magic Stone either.

If I had defeated him, one should have dropped for sure.

The fact that there isn't one means...

Hah, so even three rounds of Little Flare couldn't take him down.

I shot up to my feet.

This was no time to give up and rest.

Wasn't this an attempt I'd invested 2.4 billion Stone in? There's no way he was unscathed...

[Viscount! Viscount! The bastard is escaping toward the fog!]

...

Where do you think you're going.

To take an investment of 2.4 billion Stone and just run off like this.

[Viscount! Stop for a moment! That is.]

"I know, no need to explain."

ГЈВҖВҢField Effect Fog of the Abyss is removed.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢThe character has entered a Special Zone.ГЈВҖВҚ

ГЈВҖВҢField Effect - Sanctuary of Souls is granted.ГЈВҖВҚ

One with a single, ambiguous line as its option.

ГЈВҖВҢDeath to the livingГЈВҖВҚ

In the original game, things like this were the scariest.
